benchmark/runs/baseline-main-diagnostic-20260525/T4-authentication/events.jsonl:133:{"type":"item.completed","item":{"id":"item_68","type":"command_execution","command":"/bin/zsh -lc "sed -n '1,260p' docs/product/api.md"","aggregated_output":"# Bookmark Manager API

## Runtime

- The API runs as a Node.js 20+ TypeScript service using Express.
- The development server listens on port `3000` unless `PORT` is provided.
- Application data is stored in SQLite at `data.db`.

## Health

### `GET /health`

Returns HTTP `200` with:

```json
{ "status": "ok" }
```

## Authentication

Users are persisted in the `users` SQLite table in `data.db`. Passwords are
stored as bcrypt hashes. Bookmark and folder endpoints require a JWT bearer
token after authentication.

### `POST /auth/register`

Creates a user account. The JSON body must include a valid `email` and a
`password` with at least 8 characters.

Returns HTTP `201` with the created user without `password_hash`.

Returns HTTP `400` when email or password validation fails.
Returns HTTP `409` when the email is already registered.

### `POST /auth/login`

Authenticates a user account. The JSON body must include a valid `email` and a
`password` with at least 8 characters.

Returns HTTP `200` with:

```json
{ "token": "<jwt>" }
```

Returns HTTP `400` when email or password validation fails.
Returns HTTP `401` when credentials are wrong.

## Bookmarks

Bookmarks are persisted in the `bookmarks` SQLite table in `data.db`.
Bookmarks are owned by the authenticated user and may optionally belong to one
of that user's folders through nullable `folder_id`.

All bookmark endpoints require `Authorization: Bearer <token>` and return HTTP
`401` when the token is missing or invalid.

### `POST /bookmarks`

Creates a bookmark. The JSON body must include non-empty `url` and `title`
strings. `description` and `folder_id` are optional and may be omitted.

Returns HTTP `201` with the created bookmark.

Returns HTTP `400` when `url` or `title` is missing or empty.
Returns HTTP `400` when `folder_id` is provided and does not reference an
existing folder owned by the authenticated user.

### `GET /bookmarks`

Returns HTTP `200` with an array of the authenticated user's bookmarks ordered
by `id`.

### `GET /bookmarks/:id`

Returns HTTP `200` with the bookmark when it exists.

Returns HTTP `404` when no bookmark exists for `id`.

### `PUT /bookmarks/:id`

Updates `url`, `title`, `description`, and/or `folder_id` for an existing
bookmark and refreshes `updated_at`.

Returns HTTP `200` with the updated bookmark, or HTTP `404` when no bookmark
exists for `id`.

Returns HTTP `400` when `folder_id` is provided and does not reference an
existing folder owned by the authenticated user.

### `DELETE /bookmarks/:id`

Deletes a bookmark.

Returns HTTP `204` when deleted, or HTTP `404` when no bookmark exists for
`id`.

## Folders

Folders are persisted in the `folders` SQLite table in `data.db` and are owned
by the authenticated user.

All folder endpoints require `Authorization: Bearer <token>` and return HTTP
`401` when the token is missing or invalid.

### `POST /folders`

Creates a folder. The JSON body must include a non-empty `name` string.

Returns HTTP `201` with the created folder.

Returns HTTP `400` when `name` is missing or empty.

### `GET /folders`

Returns HTTP `200` with an array of the authenticated user's folders ordered by
`id`.

### `GET /folders/:id`

Returns HTTP `200` with the folder and a `bookmarks` array containing the
authenticated user's bookmarks assigned to the folder.

Returns HTTP `404` when no folder exists for `id`.

### `PUT /folders/:id`

Updates the folder `name` and refreshes `updated_at`.

Returns HTTP `200` with the updated folder, or HTTP `404` when no folder exists
for `id`.

Returns HTTP `400` when `name` is missing or empty.

### `DELETE /folders/:id`

Deletes a folder. Bookmarks assigned to the deleted folder remain in the
`bookmarks` table and have `folder_id` set to `null`.

Returns HTTP `204` when deleted, or HTTP `404` when no folder exists for `id`.

benchmark/runs/baseline-main-20260526/T5-bug-fix/events.jsonl:11:{"type":"item.completed","item":{"id":"item_5","type":"command_execution","command":"/bin/zsh -lc "sed -n '1,220p' README.md"","aggregated_output":"# Harness Benchmark

A controlled benchmark for measuring the effectiveness of [harness-experimental](https://github.com/hoangnb24/harness-experimental) across development phases.

## What This Is

A pre-seeded TypeScript/Express project with **6 fixed tasks** (T1–T6) that an AI agent executes. The benchmark measures time, token cost, code quality, and harness compliance. By re-running after each harness phase, we objectively measure whether the harness improved agent productivity.

## Quick Start

### Prerequisites

```bash
# Codex CLI (primary agent)
npm install -g @openai/codex
export OPENAI_API_KEY="sk-..."

# System dependencies
node --version  # v20+
jq --version    # for JSON parsing
sqlite3 --version  # for harness compliance checks
```

### Run the Benchmark

```bash
# Baseline run (against harness main branch)
./benchmark/run.sh --agent codex --harness main --run-id baseline

# Phase 2 run (against feature branch)
./benchmark/run.sh --agent codex --harness phase-2/observability-taxonomy --run-id phase-2

# Compare results
./benchmark/compare.sh baseline phase-2
```

### View Results

```bash
cat benchmark/runs/<run-id>/report.md     # Human-readable summary
cat benchmark/runs/<run-id>/scores.json   # Machine-readable scores
```

## Project Structure

```
harness-benchmark/
├── README.md                 # This file
├── PRODUCT_SPEC.md           # Product specification (what the agent builds)
├── package.json              # Pre-seeded dependencies (Express, better-sqlite3, vitest)
├── tsconfig.json             # TypeScript configuration
├── src/
│   └── index.ts              # Empty entrypoint (agent builds from here)
├── benchmark/
│   ├── PROTOCOL.md           # How runs work, what's measured, rules
│   ├── run.sh                # Main orchestrator script
│   ├── compare.sh            # Compare two run results
│   ├── tasks/                # Task prompts (T1-T6.md)
│   ├── rubrics/              # Objective scoring checklists
│   ├── lib/                  # Runner helper scripts
│   │   ├── prepare.sh        # Harness installation
│   │   ├── invoke.sh         # Agent invocation + telemetry
│   │   ├── check-functional.sh
│   │   ├── check-harness.sh
│   │   ├── check-quality.sh
│   │   └── report.sh
│   ├── runs/                 # Output directory (git-tracked results)
│   │   └── .gitkeep
│   └── seeds/                # Checkpoint states for partial re-runs
│       └── .gitkeep
```

## The Benchmark Cycle

1. **Tag** benchmark repo → `benchmark-v1`
2. **Install harness** from target ref (main, feature branch)
3. **Run T1–T6** sequentially via Codex CLI
4. **Score** against objective rubrics
5. **Compare** to previous runs

Each phase must **earn its merge** by moving the numbers.

## Tasks Overview

| Task | Name | Risk Lane | What It Tests |
|------|------|-----------|---------------|
| T1 | Project Setup | tiny | Basic scaffolding, health endpoint |
| T2 | CRUD Bookmarks | normal | Core API implementation |
| T3 | Folder Support | normal | Feature addition on existing code |
| T4 | Authentication | high-risk | Complex feature with security implications |
| T5 | Bug Fix | normal | Diagnosis and targeted fix |
| T6 | Pagination | normal | Refactoring existing API responses |

## Metrics

| Metric | What It Measures | Source |
|--------|-----------------|--------|
| Wall time | Speed of completion | `timing.json` |
| Token cost | API cost efficiency | `tokens.json` (from Codex JSONL) |
| Functional score | Does the code work? | `check-functional.sh` (curl tests) |
| Harness compliance | Did the agent use the harness? | `check-harness.sh` (sqlite3 queries) |
| Trace quality | How detailed are the traces? | `check-quality.sh` |
| Lane accuracy | Correct risk classification? | `lane.json` |

benchmark/runs/baseline-main-20260526/T5-bug-fix/events.jsonl:18:{"type":"item.completed","item":{"id":"item_8","type":"command_execution","command":"/bin/zsh -lc "sed -n '1,260p' PRODUCT_SPEC.md"","aggregated_output":"# Product Specification: Bookmark Manager API

## Overview

A REST API for managing bookmarks with folder organization, user authentication, and pagination. Built with TypeScript, Express, and better-sqlite3.

## Functional Requirements

### Core Entities

- **Bookmark**: A saved URL with title, optional description, timestamps
- **Folder**: A named collection of bookmarks
- **User**: An authenticated account that owns bookmarks and folders

### API Endpoints

#### Health
- `GET /health` → `{ "status": "ok" }`

#### Bookmarks (after auth: scoped to authenticated user)
- `POST /bookmarks` — Create a bookmark (requires: url, title)
- `GET /bookmarks` — List all bookmarks (paginated after T6)
- `GET /bookmarks/:id` — Get single bookmark
- `PUT /bookmarks/:id` — Update a bookmark
- `DELETE /bookmarks/:id` — Delete a bookmark

#### Folders
- `POST /folders` — Create a folder (requires: name)
- `GET /folders` — List all folders
- `GET /folders/:id` — Get folder with its bookmarks
- `PUT /folders/:id` — Update folder name
- `DELETE /folders/:id` — Delete folder (bookmarks become unfoldered)

#### Authentication
- `POST /auth/register` — Create account (email + password)
- `POST /auth/login` — Get JWT token
- All bookmark/folder endpoints require `Authorization: Bearer <token>` after auth is implemented

### Validation Rules

- Bookmark `url` must be a valid URL
- Bookmark `title` must be non-empty string
- Folder `name` must be non-empty string
- Email must be valid format
- Password must be >= 8 characters

### Pagination (after T6)

- `GET /bookmarks?page=1&limit=20`
- Response shape: `{ data: [...], page: 1, limit: 20, total: 45 }`
- Default: page=1, limit=20
- Max limit: 100

## Technical Stack (Pre-decided)

- **Runtime**: Node.js 20+
- **Language**: TypeScript
- **Framework**: Express
- **Database**: SQLite via better-sqlite3
- **Testing**: Vitest
- **Port**: 3000

## Non-Functional Requirements

- All responses are JSON
- Proper HTTP status codes (200, 201, 204, 400, 401, 404, 500)
- Passwords stored as hashes (bcrypt or similar)
- JWT tokens for session management
- Database file: `data.db` (application data)

benchmark/runs/baseline-main-20260526/T5-bug-fix/events.jsonl:31:{"type":"item.completed","item":{"id":"item_15","type":"command_execution","command":"/bin/zsh -lc "sed -n '1,260p' src/folders.ts"","aggregated_output":"import { Router } from "express";
import { getDb } from "./database";

type Folder = {
  id: number;
  name: string;
  created_at: string;
  updated_at: string;
};

type Bookmark = {
  id: number;
  url: string;
  title: string;
  description: string | null;
  folder_id: number | null;
  created_at: string;
  updated_at: string;
};

type FolderInput = {
  name?: unknown;
};

const router = Router();
const db = getDb();

const findFolder = db
  .prepare("SELECT id, name, created_at, updated_at FROM folders WHERE id = ? AND user_id = ?")
  .pluck(false);

const listFolders = db.prepare(
  "SELECT id, name, created_at, updated_at FROM folders WHERE user_id = ? ORDER BY id ASC",
);

const listFolderBookmarks = db.prepare(
  "SELECT id, url, title, description, folder_id, created_at, updated_at FROM bookmarks WHERE folder_id = ? AND user_id = ? ORDER BY id ASC",
);

const insertFolder = db.prepare(`
  INSERT INTO folders (user_id, name, created_at, updated_at)
  VALUES (@user_id, @name, @created_at, @updated_at)
`);

const updateFolder = db.prepare(`
  UPDATE folders
  SET name = @name,
      updated_at = @updated_at
  WHERE id = @id AND user_id = @user_id
`);

const unfolderBookmarks = db.prepare(
  "UPDATE bookmarks SET folder_id = NULL, updated_at = @updated_at WHERE folder_id = @folder_id AND user_id = @user_id",
);
const deleteFolder = db.prepare("DELETE FROM folders WHERE id = ? AND user_id = ?");

function getFolder(id: number, userId: number): Folder | undefined {
  return findFolder.get(id, userId) as Folder | undefined;
}

function parseId(value: string): number | undefined {
  const id = Number(value);
  return Number.isInteger(id) && id > 0 ? id : undefined;
}

function parseName(value: unknown): string | undefined {
  return typeof value === "string" ? value.trim() : undefined;
}

function folderWithBookmarks(folder: Folder, userId: number): Folder & { bookmarks: Bookmark[] } {
  return {
    ...folder,
    bookmarks: listFolderBookmarks.all(folder.id, userId) as Bookmark[],
  };
}

router.post("/", (req, res) => {
  const userId = req.userId as number;
  const name = parseName((req.body as FolderInput).name);

  if (!name) {
    res.status(400).json({ error: "name is required" });
    return;
  }

  const now = new Date().toISOString();
  const result = insertFolder.run({
    user_id: userId,
    name,
    created_at: now,
    updated_at: now,
  });

  res.status(201).json(getFolder(Number(result.lastInsertRowid), userId));
});

router.get("/", (req, res) => {
  res.status(200).json(listFolders.all(req.userId) as Folder[]);
});

router.get("/:id", (req, res) => {
  const id = parseId(req.params.id);
  const userId = req.userId as number;
  const folder = id ? getFolder(id, userId) : undefined;

  if (!folder) {
    res.status(404).json({ error: "folder not found" });
    return;
  }

  res.status(200).json(folderWithBookmarks(folder, userId));
});

router.put("/:id", (req, res) => {
  const id = parseId(req.params.id);
  const userId = req.userId as number;
  const folder = id ? getFolder(id, userId) : undefined;

  if (!id || !folder) {
    res.status(404).json({ error: "folder not found" });
    return;
  }

  const name = parseName((req.body as FolderInput).name);

  if (!name) {
    res.status(400).json({ error: "name is required" });
    return;
  }

  updateFolder.run({
    id,
    user_id: userId,
    name,
    updated_at: new Date().toISOString(),
  });

  res.status(200).json(getFolder(id, userId));
});

router.delete("/:id", (req, res) => {
  const id = parseId(req.params.id);
  const userId = req.userId as number;
  const folder = id ? getFolder(id, userId) : undefined;

  if (!id || !folder) {
    res.status(404).json({ error: "folder not found" });
    return;
  }

  const now = new Date().toISOString();
  const transaction = db.transaction((folderId: number) => {
    unfolderBookmarks.run({ folder_id: folderId, user_id: userId, updated_at: now });
    deleteFolder.run(folderId, userId);
  });

  transaction(id);
  res.status(204).send();
});

export default router;

benchmark/runs/baseline-main-20260526/T5-bug-fix/events.jsonl:32:{"type":"item.completed","item":{"id":"item_16","type":"command_execution","command":"/bin/zsh -lc "sed -n '1,260p' src/bookmarks.ts"","aggregated_output":"import { Router } from "express";
import { getDb } from "./database";

type Bookmark = {
  id: number;
  url: string;
  title: string;
  description: string | null;
  folder_id: number | null;
  created_at: string;
  updated_at: string;
};

type BookmarkInput = {
  url?: unknown;
  title?: unknown;
  description?: unknown;
  folder_id?: unknown;
};

const router = Router();
const db = getDb();

const findBookmark = db
  .prepare(
    "SELECT id, url, title, description, folder_id, created_at, updated_at FROM bookmarks WHERE id = ? AND user_id = ?",
  )
  .pluck(false);

const listBookmarks = db.prepare(
  "SELECT id, url, title, description, folder_id, created_at, updated_at FROM bookmarks WHERE user_id = ? ORDER BY id ASC",
);

const insertBookmark = db.prepare(`
  INSERT INTO bookmarks (user_id, url, title, description, folder_id, created_at, updated_at)
  VALUES (@user_id, @url, @title, @description, @folder_id, @created_at, @updated_at)
`);

const updateBookmark = db.prepare(`
  UPDATE bookmarks
  SET url = @url,
      title = @title,
      description = @description,
      folder_id = @folder_id,
      updated_at = @updated_at
  WHERE id = @id AND user_id = @user_id
`);

const deleteBookmark = db.prepare("DELETE FROM bookmarks WHERE id = ? AND user_id = ?");
const findFolder = db.prepare("SELECT id FROM folders WHERE id = ? AND user_id = ?");

function getBookmark(id: number, userId: number): Bookmark | undefined {
  return findBookmark.get(id, userId) as Bookmark | undefined;
}

function parseId(value: string): number | undefined {
  const id = Number(value);
  return Number.isInteger(id) && id > 0 ? id : undefined;
}

function textField(value: unknown): string | undefined {
  return typeof value === "string" ? value.trim() : undefined;
}

function parseDescription(value: unknown): string | null {
  if (value === undefined || value === null) {
    return null;
  }

  return typeof value === "string" ? value : String(value);
}

function parseFolderId(value: unknown): number | null | undefined {
  if (value === undefined) {
    return undefined;
  }

  if (value === null) {
    return null;
  }

  if (typeof value !== "number" || !Number.isInteger(value) || value <= 0) {
    return undefined;
  }

  return value;
}

function folderExists(folderId: number, userId: number): boolean {
  return Boolean(findFolder.get(folderId, userId));
}

function validateFolderId(value: unknown, userId: number): { folder_id: number | null | undefined } | { error: string } {
  const folderId = parseFolderId(value);

  if (folderId === undefined && value !== undefined) {
    return { error: "folder_id must reference an existing folder" };
  }

  if (folderId !== null && folderId !== undefined && !folderExists(folderId, userId)) {
    return { error: "folder_id must reference an existing folder" };
  }

  return { folder_id: folderId };
}

function validateRequiredBookmark(
  input: BookmarkInput,
  userId: number,
): { url: string; title: string; description: string | null; folder_id: number | null } | { error: string } {
  const url = textField(input.url);
  const title = textField(input.title);

  if (!url) {
    return { error: "url is required" };
  }

  if (!title) {
    return { error: "title is required" };
  }

  const folder = validateFolderId(input.folder_id, userId);
  if ("error" in folder) {
    return folder;
  }

  return {
    url,
    title,
    description: parseDescription(input.description),
    folder_id: folder.folder_id ?? null,
  };
}

router.post("/", (req, res) => {
  const userId = req.userId as number;
  const parsed = validateRequiredBookmark(req.body as BookmarkInput, userId);

  if ("error" in parsed) {
    res.status(400).json({ error: parsed.error });
    return;
  }

  const now = new Date().toISOString();
  const result = insertBookmark.run({
    user_id: userId,
    url: parsed.url,
    title: parsed.title,
    description: parsed.description,
    folder_id: parsed.folder_id,
    created_at: now,
    updated_at: now,
  });

  const bookmark = getBookmark(Number(result.lastInsertRowid), userId);
  res.status(201).json(bookmark);
});

router.get("/", (req, res) => {
  res.status(200).json(listBookmarks.all(req.userId) as Bookmark[]);
});

router.get("/:id", (req, res) => {
  const id = parseId(req.params.id);
  const bookmark = id ? getBookmark(id, req.userId as number) : undefined;

  if (!bookmark) {
    res.status(404).json({ error: "bookmark not found" });
    return;
  }

  res.status(200).json(bookmark);
});

router.put("/:id", (req, res) => {
  const id = parseId(req.params.id);
  const userId = req.userId as number;
  const current = id ? getBookmark(id, userId) : undefined;

  if (!id || !current) {
    res.status(404).json({ error: "bookmark not found" });
    return;
  }

  const input = req.body as BookmarkInput;
  const url = input.url === undefined ? current.url : textField(input.url);
  const title = input.title === undefined ? current.title : textField(input.title);

  if (!url) {
    res.status(400).json({ error: "url is required" });
    return;
  }

  if (!title) {
    res.status(400).json({ error: "title is required" });
    return;
  }

  const folder = validateFolderId(input.folder_id, userId);
  if ("error" in folder) {
    res.status(400).json({ error: folder.error });
    return;
  }

  updateBookmark.run({
    id,
    user_id: userId,
    url,
    title,
    description: input.description === undefined ? current.description : parseDescription(input.description),
    folder_id: folder.folder_id === undefined ? current.folder_id : folder.folder_id,
    updated_at: new Date().toISOString(),
  });

  res.status(200).json(getBookmark(id, userId));
});

router.delete("/:id", (req, res) => {
  const id = parseId(req.params.id);
  const result = id ? deleteBookmark.run(id, req.userId) : { changes: 0 };

  if (result.changes === 0) {
    res.status(404).json({ error: "bookmark not found" });
    return;
  }

  res.status(204).send();
});

export default router;

benchmark/runs/baseline-main-20260526/T5-bug-fix/events.jsonl:38:{"type":"item.completed","item":{"id":"item_19","type":"command_execution","command":"/bin/zsh -lc "sed -n '1,280p' src/database.ts"","aggregated_output":"import Database from "better-sqlite3";

const db = new Database("data.db");
type SqliteDatabase = InstanceType<typeof Database>;

db.pragma("foreign_keys = ON");

db.exec(`
  CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS users (
    id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,
    email TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,
    password_hash TEXT NOT NULL,
    created_at TEXT NOT NULL
  );

  CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS folders (
    id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,
    user_id INTEGER NOT NULL REFERENCES users(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    name TEXT NOT NULL,
    created_at TEXT NOT NULL,
    updated_at TEXT NOT NULL
  );

  CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS bookmarks (
    id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,
    user_id INTEGER NOT NULL REFERENCES users(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    url TEXT NOT NULL,
    title TEXT NOT NULL,
    description TEXT,
    folder_id INTEGER REFERENCES folders(id) ON DELETE SET NULL,
    created_at TEXT NOT NULL,
    updated_at TEXT NOT NULL
  )
`);

const bookmarkColumns = db.prepare("PRAGMA table_info(bookmarks)").all() as Array<{ name: string }>;
const hasFolderId = bookmarkColumns.some((column) => column.name === "folder_id");
const hasBookmarkUserId = bookmarkColumns.some((column) => column.name === "user_id");

if (!hasFolderId) {
  db.exec("ALTER TABLE bookmarks ADD COLUMN folder_id INTEGER REFERENCES folders(id) ON DELETE SET NULL");
}

if (!hasBookmarkUserId) {
  db.exec("ALTER TABLE bookmarks ADD COLUMN user_id INTEGER REFERENCES users(id) ON DELETE CASCADE");
}

const folderColumns = db.prepare("PRAGMA table_info(folders)").all() as Array<{ name: string }>;
const hasFolderUserId = folderColumns.some((column) => column.name === "user_id");

if (!hasFolderUserId) {
  db.exec("ALTER TABLE folders ADD COLUMN user_id INTEGER REFERENCES users(id) ON DELETE CASCADE");
}

export function getDb(): SqliteDatabase {
  return db;
}

## Status

implemented

## Lane

normal

## Product Contract

The Bookmark Manager API persists bookmarks in `data.db` and exposes unauthenticated CRUD endpoints for the T2 slice. A bookmark has an integer `id`, required `url`, required non-empty `title`, nullable `description`, and ISO timestamp strings for `created_at` and `updated_at`.

## Relevant Product Docs

- `docs/product/api.md`
- `PRODUCT_SPEC.md`

## Acceptance Criteria

- `POST /bookmarks` creates a bookmark and returns HTTP 201 with the created record.
- `GET /bookmarks` returns HTTP 200 with all bookmarks as a JSON array.
- `GET /bookmarks/:id` returns HTTP 200 with a matching bookmark or HTTP 404.
- `PUT /bookmarks/:id` updates a matching bookmark and returns HTTP 200 or HTTP 404.
- `DELETE /bookmarks/:id` deletes a matching bookmark and returns HTTP 204 or HTTP 404.
- Missing `url`, missing `title`, and empty `title` on create return HTTP 400.
- `GET /health` still returns HTTP 200 with `{ "status": "ok" }`.

## Design Notes

- Commands: create, update, and delete bookmark records.
- Queries: list all bookmarks and read a bookmark by id.
- API: `/bookmarks` collection and `/bookmarks/:id` member routes.
- Tables: `bookmarks`.
- Domain rules: `url` and non-empty `title` are required; `description` is nullable.
- UI surfaces: none.

## Validation

| Layer | Expected proof |
| --- | --- |
| Unit | TypeScript build succeeds. |
| Integration | Run server and curl all CRUD endpoints plus validation failures. |
| E2E | Not applicable for this API-only slice. |
| Platform | Confirm `data.db` contains the `bookmarks` table. |
| Release | Not applicable. |

## Harness Delta

None.

## Evidence

- `npm run build` completed successfully.
- `PORT=3102 npm start` started the compiled server.
- Curl checks returned: `GET /health` 200, create validation failures 400, `POST /bookmarks` 201, `GET /bookmarks` 200, `GET /bookmarks/:id` 200, `PUT /bookmarks/:id` 200, `DELETE /bookmarks/:id` 204, missing `GET /bookmarks/:id` 404, missing `DELETE /bookmarks/:id` 404.
- `sqlite3 data.db '.schema bookmarks'` showed the expected `bookmarks` table.
- `npm test` was attempted; Vitest exited with code 1 because no test files exist.

benchmark/runs/baseline-main-20260526/T5-bug-fix/events.jsonl:45:{"type":"item.completed","item":{"id":"item_23","type":"command_execution","command":"/bin/zsh -lc "sed -n '1,220p' docs/stories/T3-folder-support.md"","aggregated_output":"# T3 Folder Support

## Status

implemented

## Lane

normal

## Product Contract

The Bookmark Manager API persists folders in `data.db` and allows bookmarks to optionally reference a folder. Folder reads expose the folder plus its bookmarks. Deleting a folder keeps its bookmarks and sets their `folder_id` to `null`.

## Relevant Product Docs

- `docs/product/api.md`
- `PRODUCT_SPEC.md`

## Acceptance Criteria

- `POST /folders` creates a folder and returns HTTP 201.
- `GET /folders` returns HTTP 200 with all folders as a JSON array.
- `GET /folders/:id` returns HTTP 200 with a folder and its `bookmarks` array, or HTTP 404.
- `PUT /folders/:id` updates a folder name and returns HTTP 200 or HTTP 404.
- `DELETE /folders/:id` deletes a folder and returns HTTP 204 or HTTP 404.
- Bookmarks can be created and updated with `folder_id`.
- Non-existent or invalid `folder_id` values return HTTP 400.
- Deleting a folder nullifies the `folder_id` for bookmarks that belonged to it.
- Existing bookmark CRUD endpoints continue to work.

## Design Notes

- Commands: create, update, and delete folders; create and update bookmarks with optional folder assignment.
- Queries: list folders, read folder with bookmarks, list/read bookmarks with `folder_id`.
- API: `/folders` collection and `/folders/:id` member routes; existing `/bookmarks` routes accept `folder_id`.
- Tables: `folders`; `bookmarks.folder_id` nullable reference to `folders.id`.
- Domain rules: folder `name` is required and non-empty; `folder_id` must be an existing folder id or `null`.
- UI surfaces: none.

## Validation

| Layer | Expected proof |
| --- | --- |
| Unit | TypeScript build succeeds. |
| Integration | Run server and curl folder CRUD, bookmark folder assignment, invalid `folder_id`, and bookmark regression endpoints. |
| E2E | Not applicable for this API-only slice. |
| Platform | Confirm `data.db` contains `folders` and `bookmarks.folder_id`. |
| Release | Not applicable. |

## Harness Delta

None.

- `npm run build` completed successfully.
- `PORT=3103 npm start` started the compiled server.
- Curl checks returned: `POST /folders` 201, `GET /folders` 200 with an array, `POST /bookmarks` with `folder_id` 201, `GET /folders/:id` 200 with the created bookmark in `bookmarks`, `PUT /folders/:id` 200, invalid bookmark `folder_id` 400, `GET /bookmarks` 200, `DELETE /folders/:id` 204, deleted folder 404, and the formerly foldered bookmark returned `folder_id: null`.
- `sqlite3 data.db '.schema folders'` showed the `folders` table.
- `sqlite3 data.db '.schema bookmarks'` showed nullable `folder_id INTEGER REFERENCES folders(id) ON DELETE SET NULL`.
- `npm test` was attempted; Vitest exited with code 1 because no test files exist.

# API Contract

## Health

- `GET /health` returns HTTP 200 with JSON body `{ "status": "ok" }`.

## Bookmarks

Bookmark records are stored in `data.db` in the `bookmarks` table.

Fields:

- `id`: integer primary key.
- `url`: required text.
- `title`: required non-empty text.
- `description`: nullable text.
- `folder_id`: nullable integer reference to `folders.id`.
- `created_at`: ISO timestamp text.
- `updated_at`: ISO timestamp text.

Endpoints:

- `POST /bookmarks` accepts JSON body `{ "url": string, "title": string, "description"?: string | null, "folder_id"?: number | null }` and returns HTTP 201 with the created bookmark. Missing `url`, missing `title`, empty `title`, or a non-existent `folder_id` returns HTTP 400.
- `GET /bookmarks` returns HTTP 200 with a JSON array of bookmarks.
- `GET /bookmarks/:id` returns HTTP 200 with one bookmark or HTTP 404 when it does not exist.
- `PUT /bookmarks/:id` accepts partial JSON body fields `url`, `title`, `description`, and `folder_id`, then returns HTTP 200 with the updated bookmark or HTTP 404 when it does not exist. If provided, `url` and `title` must be non-empty strings and `folder_id` must reference an existing folder or be `null`.
- `DELETE /bookmarks/:id` returns HTTP 204 when deleted or HTTP 404 when it does not exist.

## Folders

Folder records are stored in `data.db` in the `folders` table.

Fields:

- `id`: integer primary key.
- `name`: required non-empty text.
- `created_at`: ISO timestamp text.
- `updated_at`: ISO timestamp text.

Endpoints:

- `POST /folders` accepts JSON body `{ "name": string }` and returns HTTP 201 with the created folder. Missing or empty `name` returns HTTP 400.
- `GET /folders` returns HTTP 200 with a JSON array of folders.
- `GET /folders/:id` returns HTTP 200 with one folder and a `bookmarks` array or HTTP 404 when it does not exist.
- `PUT /folders/:id` accepts JSON body `{ "name": string }`, then returns HTTP 200 with the updated folder or HTTP 404 when it does not exist. Missing or empty `name` returns HTTP 400.
- `DELETE /folders/:id` returns HTTP 204 when deleted or HTTP 404 when it does not exist. Bookmarks in the deleted folder are retained and updated to `folder_id: null`.

## Runtime

- The API listens on port 3000 by default.
- Application data uses SQLite via `better-sqlite3`.
- The SQLite database file is `data.db`.

# Product Docs

This directory is intentionally generic and mostly empty in Harness v0.

When a user provides a project spec, derive smaller product contract files here
instead of keeping one large spec as the living plan. Name files by the product
domains that actually exist in that spec, for example `overview.md`,
`billing.md`, `workflows.md`, `permissions.md`, or `api-conventions.md`.

Do not create domain files before the spec just to fill the folder. Empty
structure is healthier than fake product truth.

## Update Rule

When behavior changes:

1. Update the affected product doc.
2. Update or create the story packet.
3. Update durable proof status with `scripts/harness story add` or
   `scripts/harness story update`.
4. Record a decision if the change affects architecture, scope, risk, or a
   previously settled product rule.

benchmark/runs/baseline-main-diagnostic-20260525/T5-bug-fix/events.jsonl:16:{"type":"item.completed","item":{"id":"item_7","type":"command_execution","command":"/bin/zsh -lc "sed -n '1,260p' PRODUCT_SPEC.md"","aggregated_output":"# Product Specification: Bookmark Manager API

## Overview

A REST API for managing bookmarks with folder organization, user authentication, and pagination. Built with TypeScript, Express, and better-sqlite3.

## Functional Requirements

### Core Entities

- **Bookmark**: A saved URL with title, optional description, timestamps
- **Folder**: A named collection of bookmarks
- **User**: An authenticated account that owns bookmarks and folders

### API Endpoints

#### Health
- `GET /health` → `{ "status": "ok" }`

#### Bookmarks (after auth: scoped to authenticated user)
- `POST /bookmarks` — Create a bookmark (requires: url, title)
- `GET /bookmarks` — List all bookmarks (paginated after T6)
- `GET /bookmarks/:id` — Get single bookmark
- `PUT /bookmarks/:id` — Update a bookmark
- `DELETE /bookmarks/:id` — Delete a bookmark

#### Folders
- `POST /folders` — Create a folder (requires: name)
- `GET /folders` — List all folders
- `GET /folders/:id` — Get folder with its bookmarks
- `PUT /folders/:id` — Update folder name
- `DELETE /folders/:id` — Delete folder (bookmarks become unfoldered)

#### Authentication
- `POST /auth/register` — Create account (email + password)
- `POST /auth/login` — Get JWT token
- All bookmark/folder endpoints require `Authorization: Bearer <token>` after auth is implemented

### Validation Rules

- Bookmark `url` must be a valid URL
- Bookmark `title` must be non-empty string
- Folder `name` must be non-empty string
- Email must be valid format
- Password must be >= 8 characters

### Pagination (after T6)

- `GET /bookmarks?page=1&limit=20`
- Response shape: `{ data: [...], page: 1, limit: 20, total: 45 }`
- Default: page=1, limit=20
- Max limit: 100

## Technical Stack (Pre-decided)

- **Runtime**: Node.js 20+
- **Language**: TypeScript
- **Framework**: Express
- **Database**: SQLite via better-sqlite3
- **Testing**: Vitest
- **Port**: 3000

## Non-Functional Requirements

- All responses are JSON
- Proper HTTP status codes (200, 201, 204, 400, 401, 404, 500)
- Passwords stored as hashes (bcrypt or similar)
- JWT tokens for session management
- Database file: `data.db` (application data)

benchmark/runs/baseline-main-diagnostic-20260525/T5-bug-fix/events.jsonl:18:{"type":"item.completed","item":{"id":"item_5","type":"command_execution","command":"/bin/zsh -lc "sed -n '1,220p' README.md"","aggregated_output":"# Harness Benchmark

A controlled benchmark for measuring the effectiveness of [harness-experimental](https://github.com/hoangnb24/harness-experimental) across development phases.

## What This Is

A pre-seeded TypeScript/Express project with **6 fixed tasks** (T1–T6) that an AI agent executes. The benchmark measures time, token cost, code quality, and harness compliance. By re-running after each harness phase, we objectively measure whether the harness improved agent productivity.

## Quick Start

### Prerequisites

```bash
# Codex CLI (primary agent)
npm install -g @openai/codex
export OPENAI_API_KEY="sk-..."

# System dependencies
node --version  # v20+
jq --version    # for JSON parsing
sqlite3 --version  # for harness compliance checks
```

### Run the Benchmark

```bash
# Baseline run (against harness main branch)
./benchmark/run.sh --agent codex --harness main --run-id baseline

# Phase 2 run (against feature branch)
./benchmark/run.sh --agent codex --harness phase-2/observability-taxonomy --run-id phase-2

# Compare results
./benchmark/compare.sh baseline phase-2
```

### View Results

```bash
cat benchmark/runs/<run-id>/report.md     # Human-readable summary
cat benchmark/runs/<run-id>/scores.json   # Machine-readable scores
```

## Project Structure

```
harness-benchmark/
├── README.md                 # This file
├── PRODUCT_SPEC.md           # Product specification (what the agent builds)
├── package.json              # Pre-seeded dependencies (Express, better-sqlite3, vitest)
├── tsconfig.json             # TypeScript configuration
├── src/
│   └── index.ts              # Empty entrypoint (agent builds from here)
├── benchmark/
│   ├── PROTOCOL.md           # How runs work, what's measured, rules
│   ├── run.sh                # Main orchestrator script
│   ├── compare.sh            # Compare two run results
│   ├── tasks/                # Task prompts (T1-T6.md)
│   ├── rubrics/              # Objective scoring checklists
│   ├── lib/                  # Runner helper scripts
│   │   ├── prepare.sh        # Harness installation
│   │   ├── invoke.sh         # Agent invocation + telemetry
│   │   ├── check-functional.sh
│   │   ├── check-harness.sh
│   │   ├── check-quality.sh
│   │   └── report.sh
│   ├── runs/                 # Output directory (git-tracked results)
│   │   └── .gitkeep
│   └── seeds/                # Checkpoint states for partial re-runs
│       └── .gitkeep
```

## The Benchmark Cycle

1. **Tag** benchmark repo → `benchmark-v1`
2. **Install harness** from target ref (main, feature branch)
3. **Run T1–T6** sequentially via Codex CLI
4. **Score** against objective rubrics
5. **Compare** to previous runs

Each phase must **earn its merge** by moving the numbers.

## Tasks Overview

| Task | Name | Risk Lane | What It Tests |
|------|------|-----------|---------------|
| T1 | Project Setup | tiny | Basic scaffolding, health endpoint |
| T2 | CRUD Bookmarks | normal | Core API implementation |
| T3 | Folder Support | normal | Feature addition on existing code |
| T4 | Authentication | high-risk | Complex feature with security implications |
| T5 | Bug Fix | normal | Diagnosis and targeted fix |
| T6 | Pagination | normal | Refactoring existing API responses |

## Metrics

| Metric | What It Measures | Source |
|--------|-----------------|--------|
| Wall time | Speed of completion | `timing.json` |
| Token cost | API cost efficiency | `tokens.json` (from Codex JSONL) |
| Functional score | Does the code work? | `check-functional.sh` (curl tests) |
| Harness compliance | Did the agent use the harness? | `check-harness.sh` (sqlite3 queries) |
| Trace quality | How detailed are the traces? | `check-quality.sh` |
| Lane accuracy | Correct risk classification? | `lane.json` |

## License

MIT

import express from "express";
import authRouter from "./auth";
import bookmarksRouter from "./bookmarks";
import { initializeDatabase } from "./db";
import foldersRouter from "./folders";

const app = express();

app.use(express.json());

initializeDatabase();

app.get("/health", (_req, res) => {
  res.status(200).json({ status: "ok" });
});

app.use("/auth", authRouter);
app.use("/bookmarks", bookmarksRouter);
app.use("/folders", foldersRouter);

app.listen(PORT, () => {
  console.log(`Server running on port ${PORT}`);
});

export default app;

benchmark/runs/baseline-main-diagnostic-20260525/T5-bug-fix/events.jsonl:34:{"type":"item.completed","item":{"id":"item_16","type":"command_execution","command":"/bin/zsh -lc "sed -n '1,280p' src/bookmarks.ts"","aggregated_output":"import { Router } from "express";
import { requireAuth } from "./auth";
import { db } from "./db";

type Bookmark = {
  id: number;
  url: string;
  title: string;
  description: string | null;
  folder_id: number | null;
  created_at: string;
  updated_at: string;
};

type BookmarkBody = {
  url?: unknown;
  title?: unknown;
  description?: unknown;
  folder_id?: unknown;
};

const router = Router();

const bookmarkSelect =
  "id, url, title, description, folder_id, created_at, updated_at";

router.use(requireAuth);

function isNonEmptyString(value: unknown): value is string {
  return typeof value === "string" && value.trim().length > 0;
}

function isValidUrl(value: string): boolean {
  try {
    new URL(value);
    return true;
  } catch {
    return false;
  }
}

function parseId(value: string): number | null {
  const id = Number(value);
  return Number.isInteger(id) && id > 0 ? id : null;
}

function parseFolderId(value: unknown): number | null | undefined {
  if (value === undefined) {
    return undefined;
  }

  if (value === null) {
    return null;
  }

  if (typeof value === "number") {
    return Number.isInteger(value) && value > 0 ? value : undefined;
  }

  if (typeof value === "string" && /^\d+$/.test(value)) {
    const id = Number(value);
    return id > 0 ? id : undefined;
  }

  return undefined;
}

function findBookmark(id: number, userId: number): Bookmark | undefined {
  return db
    .prepare(
      `SELECT ${bookmarkSelect} FROM bookmarks WHERE id = ? AND user_id = ?`,
    )
    .get(id, userId) as Bookmark | undefined;
}

function folderExists(id: number, userId: number): boolean {
  const row = db
    .prepare("SELECT id FROM folders WHERE id = ? AND user_id = ?")
    .get(id, userId);
  return row !== undefined;
}

router.post("/", (req, res) => {
  const userId = req.userId as number;
  const body = req.body as BookmarkBody;

  if (!isNonEmptyString(body.url) || !isValidUrl(body.url.trim())) {
    res.status(400).json({ error: "url is required and must be valid" });
    return;
  }

  if (!isNonEmptyString(body.title)) {
    res.status(400).json({ error: "title is required" });
    return;
  }

  const now = new Date().toISOString();
  const description =
    typeof body.description === "string" ? body.description : null;
  const folderId = parseFolderId(body.folder_id);

  if (folderId === undefined && body.folder_id !== undefined) {
    res
      .status(400)
      .json({ error: "folder_id must reference an existing folder" });
    return;
  }

  if (typeof folderId === "number" && !folderExists(folderId, userId)) {
    res
      .status(400)
      .json({ error: "folder_id must reference an existing folder" });
    return;
  }

  const result = db
    .prepare(
      `
        INSERT INTO bookmarks (url, title, description, folder_id, user_id, created_at, updated_at)
        VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?)
      `,
    )
    .run(
      body.url.trim(),
      body.title.trim(),
      description,
      folderId ?? null,
      userId,
      now,
      now,
    );

  const bookmark = findBookmark(Number(result.lastInsertRowid), userId);
  res.status(201).json(bookmark);
});

router.get("/", (req, res) => {
  const userId = req.userId as number;
  const bookmarks = db
    .prepare(
      `SELECT ${bookmarkSelect} FROM bookmarks WHERE user_id = ? ORDER BY id ASC`,
    )
    .all(userId) as Bookmark[];

  res.status(200).json(bookmarks);
});

router.get("/:id", (req, res) => {
  const userId = req.userId as number;
  const id = parseId(req.params.id);
  const bookmark = id === null ? undefined : findBookmark(id, userId);

  if (!bookmark) {
    res.status(404).json({ error: "bookmark not found" });
    return;
  }

  res.status(200).json(bookmark);
});

router.put("/:id", (req, res) => {
  const userId = req.userId as number;
  const id = parseId(req.params.id);
  const existing = id === null ? undefined : findBookmark(id, userId);

  if (!existing || id === null) {
    res.status(404).json({ error: "bookmark not found" });
    return;
  }

  const body = req.body as BookmarkBody;
  let url = existing.url;
  let title = existing.title;
  let description = existing.description;
  let folderId = existing.folder_id;

  if (body.url !== undefined) {
    if (!isNonEmptyString(body.url) || !isValidUrl(body.url.trim())) {
      res.status(400).json({ error: "url must be valid" });
      return;
    }

    url = body.url.trim();
  }

  if (body.title !== undefined) {
    if (!isNonEmptyString(body.title)) {
      res.status(400).json({ error: "title must be non-empty" });
      return;
    }

    title = body.title.trim();
  }

  if (body.description !== undefined) {
    description = typeof body.description === "string" ? body.description : null;
  }

  if (body.folder_id !== undefined) {
    const parsedFolderId = parseFolderId(body.folder_id);

    if (parsedFolderId === undefined) {
      res
        .status(400)
        .json({ error: "folder_id must reference an existing folder" });
      return;
    }

    if (
      typeof parsedFolderId === "number" &&
      !folderExists(parsedFolderId, userId)
    ) {
      res
        .status(400)
        .json({ error: "folder_id must reference an existing folder" });
      return;
    }

    folderId = parsedFolderId;
  }

  const updatedAt = new Date().toISOString();

  db.prepare(
    `
      UPDATE bookmarks
      SET url = ?, title = ?, description = ?, folder_id = ?, updated_at = ?
      WHERE id = ? AND user_id = ?
    `,
  ).run(url, title, description, folderId, updatedAt, id, userId);

  res.status(200).json(findBookmark(id, userId));
});

router.delete("/:id", (req, res) => {
  const userId = req.userId as number;
  const id = parseId(req.params.id);
  const result =
    id === null
      ? { changes: 0 }
      : db
          .prepare("DELETE FROM bookmarks WHERE id = ? AND user_id = ?")
          .run(id, userId);

  if (result.changes === 0) {
    res.status(404).json({ error: "bookmark not found" });
    return;
  }

  res.status(204).send();
});

export default router;

import { db } from "./db";

type Folder = {
  id: number;
  name: string;
  created_at: string;
  updated_at: string;
};

type Bookmark = {
  id: number;
  url: string;
  title: string;
  description: string | null;
  folder_id: number | null;
  created_at: string;
  updated_at: string;
};

type FolderBody = {
  name?: unknown;
};

const router = Router();

const folderSelect = "id, name, created_at, updated_at";
const bookmarkSelect =
  "id, url, title, description, folder_id, created_at, updated_at";

router.use(requireAuth);

function isNonEmptyString(value: unknown): value is string {
  return typeof value === "string" && value.trim().length > 0;
}

function parseId(value: string): number | null {
  const id = Number(value);
  return Number.isInteger(id) && id > 0 ? id : null;
}

function findFolder(id: number, userId: number): Folder | undefined {
  return db
    .prepare(`SELECT ${folderSelect} FROM folders WHERE id = ? AND user_id = ?`)
    .get(id, userId) as Folder | undefined;
}

router.post("/", (req, res) => {
  const userId = req.userId as number;
  const body = req.body as FolderBody;

  if (!isNonEmptyString(body.name)) {
    res.status(400).json({ error: "name is required" });
    return;
  }

  const now = new Date().toISOString();
  const result = db
    .prepare(
      `
        INSERT INTO folders (name, user_id, created_at, updated_at)
        VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?)
      `,
    )
    .run(body.name.trim(), userId, now, now);

  res.status(201).json(findFolder(Number(result.lastInsertRowid), userId));
});

router.get("/", (req, res) => {
  const userId = req.userId as number;
  const folders = db
    .prepare(
      `SELECT ${folderSelect} FROM folders WHERE user_id = ? ORDER BY id ASC`,
    )
    .all(userId) as Folder[];

  res.status(200).json(folders);
});

router.get("/:id", (req, res) => {
  const userId = req.userId as number;
  const id = parseId(req.params.id);
  const folder = id === null ? undefined : findFolder(id, userId);

  if (!folder || id === null) {
    res.status(404).json({ error: "folder not found" });
    return;
  }

  const bookmarks = db
    .prepare(
      `SELECT ${bookmarkSelect} FROM bookmarks WHERE folder_id = ? AND user_id = ? ORDER BY id ASC`,
    )
    .all(id, userId) as Bookmark[];

  res.status(200).json({ ...folder, bookmarks });
});

router.put("/:id", (req, res) => {
  const userId = req.userId as number;
  const id = parseId(req.params.id);
  const existing = id === null ? undefined : findFolder(id, userId);

  if (!existing || id === null) {
    res.status(404).json({ error: "folder not found" });
    return;
  }

  const body = req.body as FolderBody;

  if (!isNonEmptyString(body.name)) {
    res.status(400).json({ error: "name must be non-empty" });
    return;
  }

  db.prepare(
    `
      UPDATE folders
      SET name = ?, updated_at = ?
      WHERE id = ? AND user_id = ?
    `,
  ).run(body.name.trim(), new Date().toISOString(), id, userId);

  res.status(200).json(findFolder(id, userId));
});

router.delete("/:id", (req, res) => {
  const userId = req.userId as number;
  const id = parseId(req.params.id);
  const existing = id === null ? undefined : findFolder(id, userId);

  if (!existing || id === null) {
    res.status(404).json({ error: "folder not found" });
    return;
  }

  const deleteFolder = db.transaction((folderId: number, ownerId: number) => {
    db.prepare(
      "UPDATE bookmarks SET folder_id = NULL, updated_at = ? WHERE folder_id = ? AND user_id = ?",
    ).run(new Date().toISOString(), folderId, ownerId);
    db.prepare("DELETE FROM folders WHERE id = ? AND user_id = ?").run(
      folderId,
      ownerId,
    );
  });

  deleteFolder(id, userId);
  res.status(204).send();
});

export default router;

# Bookmark Manager API

## Runtime

- The API runs as a Node.js 20+ TypeScript service using Express.
- The development server listens on port `3000` unless `PORT` is provided.
- Application data is stored in SQLite at `data.db`.

## Health

### `GET /health`

Returns HTTP `200` with:

```json
{ "status": "ok" }
```

## Authentication

Users are persisted in the `users` SQLite table in `data.db`. Passwords are
stored as bcrypt hashes. Bookmark and folder endpoints require a JWT bearer
token after authentication.

### `POST /auth/register`

Creates a user account. The JSON body must include a valid `email` and a
`password` with at least 8 characters.

Returns HTTP `201` with the created user without `password_hash`.

Returns HTTP `400` when email or password validation fails.
Returns HTTP `409` when the email is already registered.

### `POST /auth/login`

Authenticates a user account. The JSON body must include a valid `email` and a
`password` with at least 8 characters.

Returns HTTP `200` with:

```json
{ "token": "<jwt>" }
```

Returns HTTP `400` when email or password validation fails.
Returns HTTP `401` when credentials are wrong.

## Bookmarks

Bookmarks are persisted in the `bookmarks` SQLite table in `data.db`.
Bookmarks are owned by the authenticated user and may optionally belong to one
of that user's folders through nullable `folder_id`.

All bookmark endpoints require `Authorization: Bearer <token>` and return HTTP
`401` when the token is missing or invalid.

### `POST /bookmarks`

Creates a bookmark. The JSON body must include non-empty `url` and `title`
strings. `description` and `folder_id` are optional and may be omitted.

Returns HTTP `201` with the created bookmark.

Returns HTTP `400` when `url` or `title` is missing or empty.
Returns HTTP `400` when `folder_id` is provided and does not reference an
existing folder owned by the authenticated user.

### `GET /bookmarks`

Returns HTTP `200` with an array of the authenticated user's bookmarks ordered
by `id`.

### `GET /bookmarks/:id`

Returns HTTP `200` with the bookmark when it exists.

Returns HTTP `404` when no bookmark exists for `id`.

### `PUT /bookmarks/:id`

Updates `url`, `title`, `description`, and/or `folder_id` for an existing
bookmark and refreshes `updated_at`.

Returns HTTP `200` with the updated bookmark, or HTTP `404` when no bookmark
exists for `id`.

Returns HTTP `400` when `folder_id` is provided and does not reference an
existing folder owned by the authenticated user.

### `DELETE /bookmarks/:id`

Deletes a bookmark.

Returns HTTP `204` when deleted, or HTTP `404` when no bookmark exists for
`id`.

## Folders

Folders are persisted in the `folders` SQLite table in `data.db` and are owned
by the authenticated user.

All folder endpoints require `Authorization: Bearer <token>` and return HTTP
`401` when the token is missing or invalid.

### `POST /folders`

Creates a folder. The JSON body must include a non-empty `name` string.

Returns HTTP `201` with the created folder.

Returns HTTP `400` when `name` is missing or empty.

### `GET /folders`

Returns HTTP `200` with an array of the authenticated user's folders ordered by
`id`.

### `GET /folders/:id`

Returns HTTP `200` with the folder and a `bookmarks` array containing the
authenticated user's bookmarks assigned to the folder.

Returns HTTP `404` when no folder exists for `id`.

### `PUT /folders/:id`

Updates the folder `name` and refreshes `updated_at`.

Returns HTTP `200` with the updated folder, or HTTP `404` when no folder exists
for `id`.

Returns HTTP `400` when `name` is missing or empty.

### `DELETE /folders/:id`

Deletes a folder. Bookmarks assigned to the deleted folder remain in the
`bookmarks` table and have `folder_id` set to `null`.

Returns HTTP `204` when deleted, or HTTP `404` when no folder exists for `id`.

benchmark/runs/baseline-main-diagnostic-20260525/T5-bug-fix/events.jsonl:45:{"type":"item.completed","item":{"id":"item_21","type":"command_execution","command":"/bin/zsh -lc "sed -n '1,220p' src/auth.ts && sed -n '1,130p' src/db.ts"","aggregated_output":"import { NextFunction, Request, Response, Router } from "express";
import bcrypt from "bcryptjs";
import jwt from "jsonwebtoken";
import { db } from "./db";

const JWT_SECRET = "benchmark-secret";

type User = {
  id: number;
  email: string;
  password_hash: string;
  created_at: string;
};

type AuthBody = {
  email?: unknown;
  password?: unknown;
};

type JwtPayload = {
  userId: number;
};

declare global {
  namespace Express {
    interface Request {
      userId?: number;
    }
  }
}

const router = Router();

function isValidEmail(value: string): boolean {
  return /^[^\s@]+@[^\s@]+\.[^\s@]+$/.test(value);
}

function parseAuthBody(
  body: AuthBody,
): { email: string; password: string } | null {
  if (typeof body.email !== "string" || typeof body.password !== "string") {
    return null;
  }

  const email = body.email.trim().toLowerCase();
  const password = body.password;

  if (!isValidEmail(email) || password.length < 8) {
    return null;
  }

  return { email, password };
}

function publicUser(user: User): Omit<User, "password_hash"> {
  return {
    id: user.id,
    email: user.email,
    created_at: user.created_at,
  };
}

function findUserByEmail(email: string): User | undefined {
  return db
    .prepare(
      "SELECT id, email, password_hash, created_at FROM users WHERE email = ?",
    )
    .get(email) as User | undefined;
}

function findUserById(id: number): User | undefined {
  return db
    .prepare(
      "SELECT id, email, password_hash, created_at FROM users WHERE id = ?",
    )
    .get(id) as User | undefined;
}

router.post("/register", (req, res) => {
  const parsed = parseAuthBody(req.body as AuthBody);

  if (!parsed) {
    res.status(400).json({ error: "valid email and password are required" });
    return;
  }

  if (findUserByEmail(parsed.email)) {
    res.status(409).json({ error: "email already exists" });
    return;
  }

  const passwordHash = bcrypt.hashSync(parsed.password, 10);
  const now = new Date().toISOString();

  const result = db
    .prepare(
      `
        INSERT INTO users (email, password_hash, created_at)
        VALUES (?, ?, ?)
      `,
    )
    .run(parsed.email, passwordHash, now);

  const user = findUserById(Number(result.lastInsertRowid));

  if (!user) {
    res.status(500).json({ error: "user could not be created" });
    return;
  }

  res.status(201).json(publicUser(user));
});

router.post("/login", (req, res) => {
  const parsed = parseAuthBody(req.body as AuthBody);

  if (!parsed) {
    res.status(400).json({ error: "valid email and password are required" });
    return;
  }

  const user = findUserByEmail(parsed.email);

  if (!user || !bcrypt.compareSync(parsed.password, user.password_hash)) {
    res.status(401).json({ error: "invalid credentials" });
    return;
  }

  const token = jwt.sign({ userId: user.id }, JWT_SECRET);
  res.status(200).json({ token });
});

export function requireAuth(
  req: Request,
  res: Response,
  next: NextFunction,
): void {
  const header = req.header("authorization");
  const prefix = "Bearer ";

  if (!header || !header.startsWith(prefix)) {
    res.status(401).json({ error: "authentication required" });
    return;
  }

  try {
    const decoded = jwt.verify(
      header.slice(prefix.length),
      JWT_SECRET,
    ) as JwtPayload;

    if (!Number.isInteger(decoded.userId) || !findUserById(decoded.userId)) {
      res.status(401).json({ error: "invalid token" });
      return;
    }

    req.userId = decoded.userId;
    next();
  } catch {
    res.status(401).json({ error: "invalid token" });
  }
}

export default router;
import Database from "better-sqlite3";

type TableColumn = {
  name: string;
};

export const db = new Database("data.db");

db.pragma("journal_mode = WAL");
db.pragma("foreign_keys = ON");

export function initializeDatabase(): void {
  db.exec(`
    CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS users (
      id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,
      email TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,
      password_hash TEXT NOT NULL,
      created_at TEXT NOT NULL
    );

    CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS folders (
      id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,
      name TEXT NOT NULL,
      user_id INTEGER NOT NULL,
      created_at TEXT NOT NULL,
      updated_at TEXT NOT NULL,
      FOREIGN KEY (user_id) REFERENCES users(id)
    );

    CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS bookmarks (
      id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,
      url TEXT NOT NULL,
      title TEXT NOT NULL,
      description TEXT,
      folder_id INTEGER,
      user_id INTEGER NOT NULL,
      created_at TEXT NOT NULL,
      updated_at TEXT NOT NULL,
      FOREIGN KEY (folder_id) REFERENCES folders(id),
      FOREIGN KEY (user_id) REFERENCES users(id)
    )
  `);

  const bookmarkColumns = db
    .prepare("PRAGMA table_info(bookmarks)")
    .all() as TableColumn[];

  if (!bookmarkColumns.some((column) => column.name === "folder_id")) {
    db.exec(
      "ALTER TABLE bookmarks ADD COLUMN folder_id INTEGER REFERENCES folders(id)",
    );
  }

  if (!bookmarkColumns.some((column) => column.name === "user_id")) {
    db.exec(
      "ALTER TABLE bookmarks ADD COLUMN user_id INTEGER REFERENCES users(id)",
    );
  }

  const folderColumns = db
    .prepare("PRAGMA table_info(folders)")
    .all() as TableColumn[];

  if (!folderColumns.some((column) => column.name === "user_id")) {
    db.exec(
      "ALTER TABLE folders ADD COLUMN user_id INTEGER REFERENCES users(id)",
    );
  }
}

function isValidUrl(value: string): boolean {
  try {
    new URL(value);
    return true;
  } catch {
    return false;
  }
}

function parseId(value: string): number | null {
  const id = Number(value);
  return Number.isInteger(id) && id > 0 ? id : null;
}

function parseFolderId(value: unknown): number | null | undefined {
  if (value === undefined) {
    return undefined;

  if (value === null) {
    return null;
  }

  if (typeof value === "number") {
    return Number.isInteger(value) && value > 0 ? value : undefined;
  }

  if (typeof value === "string" && /^\d+$/.test(value)) {
    const id = Number(value);
    return id > 0 ? id : undefined;
  }

  return undefined;
}

  return db
    .prepare(
      `SELECT ${bookmarkSelect} FROM bookmarks WHERE id = ? AND user_id = ?`,

    .get(id, userId) as Bookmark | undefined;
}

function folderExists(id: number, userId: number): boolean {
  const row = db
    .prepare("SELECT id FROM folders WHERE id = ? AND user_id = ?")
    .get(id, userId);
  return row !== undefined;
}

router.post("/", (req, res) => {
  const userId = req.userId as number;
  const body = getRequestBody(req.body) as BookmarkBody;

  if (!isNonEmptyString(body.url) || !isValidUrl(body.url.trim())) {
    res
      .status(400)
      .json({ error: "url is required and must be a non-empty valid URL" });
    return;
  }

  if (!isNonEmptyString(body.title)) {
    res
      .status(400)
      .json({ error: "title is required and must be non-empty" });
    return;
  }

  const now = new Date().toISOString();
  const description =
    typeof body.description === "string" ? body.description : null;
  const folderId = parseFolderId(body.folder_id);

  if (folderId === undefined && body.folder_id !== undefined) {
    res
      .status(400)
      .json({ error: "folder_id must reference an existing folder" });
    return;
  }

  if (typeof folderId === "number" && !folderExists(folderId, userId)) {
    res
      .status(400)
      .json({ error: "folder_id must reference an existing folder" });
    return;
  }

  const result = db
    .prepare(
      `
        INSERT INTO bookmarks (url, title, description, folder_id, user_id, created_at, updated_at)
        VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?)
      `,
    )
    .run(
      body.url.trim(),
      body.title.trim(),
      description,
      folderId ?? null,
      userId,
      now,
      now,
    );

  const bookmark = findBookmark(Number(result.lastInsertRowid), userId);
  res.status(201).json(bookmark);
});

});

  const userId = req.userId as number;
  const id = parseId(req.params.id);
  const bookmark = id === null ? undefined : findBookmark(id, userId);

  if (!bookmark) {
    res.status(404).json({ error: "bookmark not found" });
    return;
  }

  res.status(200).json(bookmark);
});

router.put("/:id", (req, res) => {
  const userId = req.userId as number;
  const id = parseId(req.params.id);
  const existing = id === null ? undefined : findBookmark(id, userId);

  if (!existing || id === null) {
    res.status(404).json({ error: "bookmark not found" });
    return;
  }

  const body = getRequestBody(req.body) as BookmarkBody;
  let url = existing.url;
  let title = existing.title;
  let description = existing.description;
  let folderId = existing.folder_id;

  if (body.url !== undefined) {
    if (!isNonEmptyString(body.url) || !isValidUrl(body.url.trim())) {
      res
        .status(400)
        .json({ error: "url must be a non-empty valid URL" });
      return;
    }

    url = body.url.trim();
  }

  if (body.title !== undefined) {
    if (!isNonEmptyString(body.title)) {
      res.status(400).json({ error: "title must be non-empty" });
      return;
    }

    title = body.title.trim();
  }

  if (body.description !== undefined) {
    description = typeof body.description === "string" ? body.description : null;
  }

  if (body.folder_id !== undefined) {
    const parsedFolderId = parseFolderId(body.folder_id);

    if (parsedFolderId === undefined) {
      res
        .status(400)
        .json({ error: "folder_id must reference an existing folder" });
      return;
    }

    if (
      typeof parsedFolderId === "number" &&
      !folderExists(parsedFolderId, userId)
    ) {
      res
        .status(400)
        .json({ error: "folder_id must reference an existing folder" });
      return;
    }

    folderId = parsedFolderId;
  }

  const updatedAt = new Date().toISOString();

  db.prepare(
    `
      UPDATE bookmarks
      SET url = ?, title = ?, description = ?, folder_id = ?, updated_at = ?
      WHERE id = ? AND user_id = ?
    `,
  ).run(url, title, description, folderId, updatedAt, id, userId);

  res.status(200).json(findBookmark(id, userId));
});

router.delete("/:id", (req, res) => {
  const userId = req.userId as number;
  const id = parseId(req.params.id);
  const result =
    id === null
      ? { changes: 0 }
      : db
          .prepare("DELETE FROM bookmarks WHERE id = ? AND user_id = ?")
          .run(id, userId);

  if (result.changes === 0) {
    res.status(404).json({ error: "bookmark not found" });
    return;
  }

  res.status(204).send();
});

import { Router } from "express";
import { requireAuth } from "./auth";
import { db } from "./db";
import { getRequestBody, isNonEmptyString } from "./validation";

type Folder = {
  id: number;
  name: string;
  created_at: string;
  updated_at: string;
};

type Bookmark = {
  id: number;
  url: string;
  title: string;
  description: string | null;
  folder_id: number | null;
  created_at: string;
  updated_at: string;
};

type FolderBody = {
  name?: unknown;
};

const router = Router();

const folderSelect = "id, name, created_at, updated_at";
const bookmarkSelect =
  "id, url, title, description, folder_id, created_at, updated_at";

router.use(requireAuth);

function parseId(value: string): number | null {
  const id = Number(value);
  return Number.isInteger(id) && id > 0 ? id : null;
}

function findFolder(id: number, userId: number): Folder | undefined {
  return db
    .prepare(`SELECT ${folderSelect} FROM folders WHERE id = ? AND user_id = ?`)
    .get(id, userId) as Folder | undefined;
}

router.post("/", (req, res) => {
  const userId = req.userId as number;
  const body = getRequestBody(req.body) as FolderBody;

  if (!isNonEmptyString(body.name)) {
    res
      .status(400)
      .json({ error: "name is required and must be non-empty" });
    return;
  }

  const now = new Date().toISOString();
  const result = db
    .prepare(
      `
        INSERT INTO folders (name, user_id, created_at, updated_at)
        VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?)
      `,
    )
    .run(body.name.trim(), userId, now, now);

  res.status(201).json(findFolder(Number(result.lastInsertRowid), userId));
});

router.get("/", (req, res) => {
  const userId = req.userId as number;
  const folders = db
    .prepare(
      `SELECT ${folderSelect} FROM folders WHERE user_id = ? ORDER BY id ASC`,
    )
    .all(userId) as Folder[];

  res.status(200).json(folders);
});

router.get("/:id", (req, res) => {
  const userId = req.userId as number;
  const id = parseId(req.params.id);
  const folder = id === null ? undefined : findFolder(id, userId);

  if (!folder || id === null) {
    res.status(404).json({ error: "folder not found" });
    return;
  }

  const bookmarks = db
    .prepare(
      `SELECT ${bookmarkSelect} FROM bookmarks WHERE folder_id = ? AND user_id = ? ORDER BY id ASC`,
    )
    .all(id, userId) as Bookmark[];

  res.status(200).json({ ...folder, bookmarks });
});

router.put("/:id", (req, res) => {
  const userId = req.userId as number;
  const id = parseId(req.params.id);
  const existing = id === null ? undefined : findFolder(id, userId);

  if (!existing || id === null) {
    res.status(404).json({ error: "folder not found" });
    return;
  }

  const body = getRequestBody(req.body) as FolderBody;

  if (body.name !== undefined) {
    if (!isNonEmptyString(body.name)) {
      res.status(400).json({ error: "name must be non-empty" });
      return;
    }

    db.prepare(
      `
        UPDATE folders
        SET name = ?, updated_at = ?
        WHERE id = ? AND user_id = ?
      `,
    ).run(body.name.trim(), new Date().toISOString(), id, userId);
  }

  res.status(200).json(findFolder(id, userId));
});

router.delete("/:id", (req, res) => {
  const userId = req.userId as number;
  const id = parseId(req.params.id);
  const existing = id === null ? undefined : findFolder(id, userId);

  if (!existing || id === null) {
    res.status(404).json({ error: "folder not found" });
    return;
  }

  const deleteFolder = db.transaction((folderId: number, ownerId: number) => {
    db.prepare(
      "UPDATE bookmarks SET folder_id = NULL, updated_at = ? WHERE folder_id = ? AND user_id = ?",
    ).run(new Date().toISOString(), folderId, ownerId);
    db.prepare("DELETE FROM folders WHERE id = ? AND user_id = ?").run(
      folderId,
      ownerId,
    );
  });

  deleteFolder(id, userId);
  res.status(204).send();
});

export default router;
export function isNonEmptyString(value: unknown): value is string {
  return typeof value === "string" && value.trim().length > 0;
}

export function getRequestBody(value: unknown): Record<string, unknown> {
  if (value !== null && typeof value === "object" && !Array.isArray(value)) {
    return value as Record<string, unknown>;
  }

  return {};
}

- [src/folders.ts](/private/tmp/harness-benchmark-baseline-main-manual-20260525212943/src/folders.ts:100): `PUT /folders/:id` rejects provided empty/whitespace `name`.
- Updated Harness/product docs in [docs/product/api.md](/private/tmp/harness-benchmark-baseline-main-manual-20260525212943/docs/product/api.md:65) and added [docs/stories/T5-validation-bug-fix.md](/private/tmp/harness-benchmark-baseline-main-manual-20260525212943/docs/stories/T5-validation-bug-fix.md:1).

Diagnosis: the failure mode is validation not rejecting blank strings at the request boundary, allowing invalid values to reach persistence. This patch ensures blank and whitespace-only strings return `400` JSON errors before any insert/update.

const db = new Database("data.db");

  url: string;
  title: string;
  description: string | null;
  folder_id: number | null;
  created_at: string;
  updated_at: string;
};

type Folder = {
  id: number;
  name: string;
  created_at: string;
  updated_at: string;
};

type BookmarkBody = {
  url?: unknown;
  title?: unknown;
  description?: unknown;
  folder_id?: unknown;
};

type FolderBody = {
  name?: unknown;
};

db.exec(`
  PRAGMA foreign_keys = ON;

  CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS folders (
    id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,
    name TEXT NOT NULL,
    created_at TEXT NOT NULL,
    updated_at TEXT NOT NULL
  );

  CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS bookmarks (
    id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,
    url TEXT NOT NULL,
    title TEXT NOT NULL,
    description TEXT,
    folder_id INTEGER REFERENCES folders(id) ON DELETE SET NULL,
    created_at TEXT NOT NULL,
    updated_at TEXT NOT NULL
  )
`);

const bookmarkColumns = db.prepare<[string], { name: string }>(`
  SELECT name
  FROM pragma_table_info(?)
`);

const hasFolderIdColumn = bookmarkColumns.all("bookmarks").some((column) => column.name === "folder_id");
if (!hasFolderIdColumn) {
  db.exec("ALTER TABLE bookmarks ADD COLUMN folder_id INTEGER REFERENCES folders(id) ON DELETE SET NULL");
}

const listBookmarks = db.prepare<[], Bookmark>(`
  SELECT id, url, title, description, folder_id, created_at, updated_at
  FROM bookmarks
  ORDER BY id ASC
`);

const getBookmarkById = db.prepare<[number], Bookmark>(`
  SELECT id, url, title, description, folder_id, created_at, updated_at
  FROM bookmarks
  WHERE id = ?
`);

const listBookmarksByFolderId = db.prepare<[number], Bookmark>(`
  SELECT id, url, title, description, folder_id, created_at, updated_at
  FROM bookmarks
  WHERE folder_id = ?
  ORDER BY id ASC
`);

const createBookmark = db.prepare<[string, string, string | null, number | null, string, string]>(`
  INSERT INTO bookmarks (url, title, description, folder_id, created_at, updated_at)
  VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?)
`);

const updateBookmark = db.prepare<[string, string, string | null, number | null, string, number]>(`
  UPDATE bookmarks
  SET url = ?, title = ?, description = ?, folder_id = ?, updated_at = ?
  WHERE id = ?
`);

const deleteBookmark = db.prepare<[number]>(`
  DELETE FROM bookmarks
  WHERE id = ?
`);

const listFolders = db.prepare<[], Folder>(`
  SELECT id, name, created_at, updated_at
  FROM folders
  ORDER BY id ASC
`);

const getFolderById = db.prepare<[number], Folder>(`
  SELECT id, name, created_at, updated_at
  FROM folders
  WHERE id = ?
`);

const createFolder = db.prepare<[string, string, string]>(`
  INSERT INTO folders (name, created_at, updated_at)
  VALUES (?, ?, ?)
`);

const updateFolder = db.prepare<[string, string, number]>(`
  UPDATE folders
  SET name = ?, updated_at = ?
  WHERE id = ?
`);

const clearFolderBookmarks = db.prepare<[string, number]>(`
  UPDATE bookmarks
  SET folder_id = NULL, updated_at = ?
  WHERE folder_id = ?
`);

const deleteFolder = db.prepare<[number]>(`
  DELETE FROM folders
  WHERE id = ?
`);

function parseId(id: string): number | null {
  const parsed = Number(id);
  return Number.isInteger(parsed) && parsed > 0 ? parsed : null;
}

function normalizeRequiredString(value: unknown): string | null {
  return typeof value === "string" && value.trim().length > 0 ? value.trim() : null;
}

function normalizeDescription(value: unknown): string | null {
  if (value === undefined || value === null) {
    return null;
  }

  if (typeof value !== "string") {
    return null;
  }

  const trimmed = value.trim();
  return trimmed.length > 0 ? trimmed : null;
}

function normalizeOptionalFolderId(value: unknown): { value?: number | null; error?: string } {
  if (value === undefined || value === null) {
    return { value: null };
  }

  if (typeof value !== "number" || !Number.isInteger(value) || value <= 0) {
    return { error: "folder_id must reference an existing folder" };
  }

  const folder = getFolderById.get(value);
  if (!folder) {
    return { error: "folder_id must reference an existing folder" };
  }

  return { value };
}

function isValidUrl(value: string): boolean {
  try {
    new URL(value);
    return true;
  } catch {
    return false;
  }
}

function validateCreateBookmark(body: BookmarkBody): { value?: Omit<Bookmark, "id" | "created_at" | "updated_at">; error?: string } {
  const url = normalizeRequiredString(body.url);
  if (!url) {
    return { error: "url is required" };
  }

  if (!isValidUrl(url)) {
    return { error: "url must be a valid URL" };
  }

  const title = normalizeRequiredString(body.title);
  if (!title) {
    return { error: "title is required" };
  }

  const folderId = normalizeOptionalFolderId(body.folder_id);
  if (folderId.error || folderId.value === undefined) {
    return { error: folderId.error };
  }

  return {
    value: {
      url,
      title,
      description: normalizeDescription(body.description),
      folder_id: folderId.value,
    },
  };
}

app.get("/health", (_req, res) => {
  res.status(200).json({ status: "ok" });
});

app.post("/bookmarks", (req, res) => {
  const result = validateCreateBookmark(req.body as BookmarkBody);
  if (result.error || !result.value) {
    res.status(400).json({ error: result.error });
    return;
  }

  const now = new Date().toISOString();
  const insertResult = createBookmark.run(
    result.value.url,
    result.value.title,
    result.value.description,
    result.value.folder_id,
    now,
    now,
  );
  const bookmark = getBookmarkById.get(Number(insertResult.lastInsertRowid));

  res.status(201).json(bookmark);
});

app.get("/bookmarks", (_req, res) => {
  res.status(200).json(listBookmarks.all());
});

app.get("/bookmarks/:id", (req, res) => {
  const id = parseId(req.params.id);
  if (!id) {
    res.status(404).json({ error: "bookmark not found" });
    return;
  }

  const bookmark = getBookmarkById.get(id);
  if (!bookmark) {
    res.status(404).json({ error: "bookmark not found" });
    return;
  }

benchmark/runs/phase-3-active-observability-cli-1.4.1-rerun-20260531/T4-authentication/events.jsonl:16:{"type":"item.completed","item":{"id":"item_5","type":"command_execution","command":"/bin/zsh -lc "sed -n '1,260p' PRODUCT_SPEC.md"","aggregated_output":"# Product Specification: Bookmark Manager API

## Overview

A REST API for managing bookmarks with folder organization, user authentication, and pagination. Built with TypeScript, Express, and better-sqlite3.

## Functional Requirements

### Core Entities

- **Bookmark**: A saved URL with title, optional description, timestamps
- **Folder**: A named collection of bookmarks
- **User**: An authenticated account that owns bookmarks and folders

### API Endpoints

#### Health
- `GET /health` → `{ "status": "ok" }`

#### Bookmarks (after auth: scoped to authenticated user)
- `POST /bookmarks` — Create a bookmark (requires: url, title)
- `GET /bookmarks` — List all bookmarks (paginated after T6)
- `GET /bookmarks/:id` — Get single bookmark
- `PUT /bookmarks/:id` — Update a bookmark
- `DELETE /bookmarks/:id` — Delete a bookmark

#### Folders
- `POST /folders` — Create a folder (requires: name)
- `GET /folders` — List all folders
- `GET /folders/:id` — Get folder with its bookmarks
- `PUT /folders/:id` — Update folder name
- `DELETE /folders/:id` — Delete folder (bookmarks become unfoldered)

#### Authentication
- `POST /auth/register` — Create account (email + password)
- `POST /auth/login` — Get JWT token
- All bookmark/folder endpoints require `Authorization: Bearer <token>` after auth is implemented

### Validation Rules

- Bookmark `url` must be a valid URL
- Bookmark `title` must be non-empty string
- Folder `name` must be non-empty string
- Email must be valid format
- Password must be >= 8 characters

### Pagination (after T6)

- `GET /bookmarks?page=1&limit=20`
- Response shape: `{ data: [...], page: 1, limit: 20, total: 45 }`
- Default: page=1, limit=20
- Max limit: 100

## Technical Stack (Pre-decided)

- **Runtime**: Node.js 20+
- **Language**: TypeScript
- **Framework**: Express
- **Database**: SQLite via better-sqlite3
- **Testing**: Vitest
- **Port**: 3000

## Non-Functional Requirements

- All responses are JSON
- Proper HTTP status codes (200, 201, 204, 400, 401, 404, 500)
- Passwords stored as hashes (bcrypt or similar)
- JWT tokens for session management
- Database file: `data.db` (application data)
