benchmark/runs/phase-3-active-observability-cli-1.4.1-rerun-20260531/T4-authentication/events.jsonl:17:{"type":"item.completed","item":{"id":"item_7","type":"command_execution","command":"/bin/zsh -lc "sed -n '1,220p' README.md"","aggregated_output":"# Harness Benchmark

A controlled benchmark for measuring the effectiveness of [harness-experimental](https://github.com/hoangnb24/harness-experimental) across development phases.

## What This Is

A pre-seeded TypeScript/Express project with **6 fixed tasks** (T1–T6) that an AI agent executes. The benchmark measures time, token cost, code quality, and harness compliance. By re-running after each harness phase, we objectively measure whether the harness improved agent productivity.

## Quick Start

### Prerequisites

```bash
# Codex CLI (primary agent)
npm install -g @openai/codex
export OPENAI_API_KEY="sk-..."

# System dependencies
node --version  # v20+
jq --version    # for JSON parsing
sqlite3 --version  # for harness compliance checks
```

### Run the Benchmark

```bash
# Baseline run (against harness main branch)
./benchmark/run.sh --agent codex --harness main --run-id baseline

# Phase 2 run (against feature branch)
./benchmark/run.sh --agent codex --harness phase-2/observability-taxonomy --run-id phase-2

# Compare results
./benchmark/compare.sh baseline phase-2
```

### View Results

```bash
cat benchmark/runs/<run-id>/report.md     # Human-readable summary
cat benchmark/runs/<run-id>/scores.json   # Machine-readable scores
```

## Project Structure

```
harness-benchmark/
├── README.md                 # This file
├── PRODUCT_SPEC.md           # Product specification (what the agent builds)
├── package.json              # Pre-seeded dependencies (Express, better-sqlite3, vitest)
├── tsconfig.json             # TypeScript configuration
├── src/
│   └── index.ts              # Empty entrypoint (agent builds from here)
├── benchmark/
│   ├── PROTOCOL.md           # How runs work, what's measured, rules
│   ├── run.sh                # Main orchestrator script
│   ├── compare.sh            # Compare two run results
│   ├── tasks/                # Task prompts (T1-T6.md)
│   ├── rubrics/              # Objective scoring checklists
│   ├── lib/                  # Runner helper scripts
│   │   ├── prepare.sh        # Harness installation
│   │   ├── invoke.sh         # Agent invocation + telemetry
│   │   ├── check-functional.sh
│   │   ├── check-harness.sh
│   │   ├── check-quality.sh
│   │   └── report.sh
│   ├── runs/                 # Output directory (git-tracked results)
│   │   └── .gitkeep
│   └── seeds/                # Checkpoint states for partial re-runs
│       └── .gitkeep
```

## The Benchmark Cycle

1. **Tag** benchmark repo → `benchmark-v1`
2. **Install harness** from target ref (main, feature branch)
3. **Run T1–T6** sequentially via Codex CLI
4. **Score** against objective rubrics
5. **Compare** to previous runs

Each phase must **earn its merge** by moving the numbers.

## Tasks Overview

| Task | Name | Risk Lane | What It Tests |
|------|------|-----------|---------------|
| T1 | Project Setup | tiny | Basic scaffolding, health endpoint |
| T2 | CRUD Bookmarks | normal | Core API implementation |
| T3 | Folder Support | normal | Feature addition on existing code |
| T4 | Authentication | high-risk | Complex feature with security implications |
| T5 | Bug Fix | normal | Diagnosis and targeted fix |
| T6 | Pagination | normal | Refactoring existing API responses |

## Metrics

| Metric | What It Measures | Source |
|--------|-----------------|--------|
| Wall time | Speed of completion | `timing.json` |
| Token cost | API cost efficiency | `tokens.json` (from Codex JSONL) |
| Functional score | Does the code work? | `check-functional.sh` (curl tests) |
| Harness compliance | Did the agent use the harness? | `check-harness.sh` (sqlite3 queries) |
| Trace quality | How detailed are the traces? | `check-quality.sh` |
| Lane accuracy | Correct risk classification? | `lane.json` |

## License

MIT

benchmark/runs/phase-3-active-observability-cli-1.4.1-rerun-20260531/T4-authentication/events.jsonl:23:{"type":"item.completed","item":{"id":"item_10","type":"command_execution","command":"/bin/zsh -lc "sed -n '1,260p' docs/FEATURE_INTAKE.md"","aggregated_output":"# Feature Intake

Every implementation prompt enters the intake gate before code changes. A new
project spec also enters through this gate before it becomes product docs,
stories, or implementation work.

The human does not need to classify risk. The harness does.

## Intake Flow

```text
User prompt
    |
    v
Classify input type
    |
    v
Restate as work item
    |
    v
Find affected product docs and stories
    |
    v
Run risk checklist
    |
    v
Choose lane: tiny, normal, or high-risk
```

## Input Types

Use the input type to decide where the work should land before choosing the risk
lane.

| Type | Use when | Typical artifact |
| --- | --- | --- |
| New spec | Turning a user-provided project spec into harness-ready docs | Product docs, candidate epics, decisions |
| Spec slice | Implementing selected behavior from an accepted spec | Story packet |
| Change request | Changing, fixing, or refining accepted behavior | Story packet or direct patch |
| New initiative | Adding a larger product area that needs multiple stories | Initiative notes plus story packets |
| Maintenance request | Changing technical, operational, or dependency behavior | Story packet, validation report, or decision |
| Harness improvement | Improving how humans and agents collaborate | Direct docs update or `scripts/harness backlog add` |

Do not create or extend a monolithic spec by default after intake. Use product
docs, stories, decisions, and initiative notes as the living surface.

## Lanes

### Tiny

Use for low-risk docs, copy, names, or narrow edits.

Also use for initial project setup when the work is limited to installing
declared dependencies, wiring a server entrypoint, adding a health/smoke
endpoint, or opening a local development database connection without creating
domain schema, CRUD behavior, auth, authorization, provider integration, or
data migration. A health endpoint in a new benchmark or scaffolded project is
smoke proof, not a public contract escalation by itself.

Requirements:

- Patch directly.
- Keep affected docs current.
- Run available quick checks.
- Update the harness only if friction was found.

### Normal

Use for story-sized behavior with bounded blast radius.

Requirements:

- Create or update one story file from `docs/templates/story.md`.
- Link relevant product docs.
- Add or update validation expectations.
- Implement the smallest vertical slice when implementation exists.
- Record or update proof status with `scripts/harness story add` and
  `scripts/harness story update`.

### High-Risk

Use when the work can affect security, data, scope, contracts, or multiple
roles/platforms.

Requirements:

- Create a story folder using `docs/templates/high-risk-story/`.
- Fill in `execplan.md`, `overview.md`, `design.md`, and `validation.md`.
- Ask for human confirmation before implementation if direction is ambiguous.
- Record a decision when behavior or architecture changes meaningfully.

## Risk Checklist

Mark one flag for each item that applies:

| Risk flag | Applies when the work touches |
| --- | --- |
| Auth | login, logout, sessions, JWT, password, refresh token |
| Authorization | roles, permissions, tenant or company scope |
| Data model | schema, migrations, uniqueness, deletion, retention |
| Audit/security | audit logs, privacy, sensitive data, access logs |
| External systems | email, payments, cloud services, provider SDKs, queues, webhooks |
| Public contracts | API shape, response envelope, client-visible behavior |
| Cross-platform | desktop/mobile/browser split, native shell behavior, deep links |
| Existing behavior | already implemented or test-covered behavior changes |
| Weak proof | unclear or missing tests around the affected area |
| Multi-domain | more than one product domain changes at once |

## Classification

```text
0-1 flags:
  tiny or normal, based on code impact

2-3 flags:
  normal with stronger validation

4+ flags:
  high-risk

Any hard gate:
  high-risk unless the human explicitly narrows scope
```

Hard gates:

- Auth.
- Authorization.
- Data loss or migration.
- Audit/security.
- External provider behavior.
- Removing or weakening validation requirements.

## Output

At the end of intake, the agent should be able to say:

```text
Lane: normal
Reason: touches authorization, API contract, and audit behavior.
Docs: permissions, account-settings, audit-log.
Story: docs/stories/epics/E02-access-control/US-014-manager-updates-role.md.
Validation: unit, integration, E2E.
```

app.put("/bookmarks/:id", (req, res) => {
  const id = parseId(req.params.id);
  if (!id) {
    res.status(404).json({ error: "bookmark not found" });
    return;
  }

  const existing = getBookmarkById.get(id);
  if (!existing) {
    res.status(404).json({ error: "bookmark not found" });
    return;
  }

  const body = req.body as BookmarkBody;
  const url = body.url === undefined ? existing.url : normalizeRequiredString(body.url);
  if (!url) {
    res.status(400).json({ error: "url is required" });
    return;
  }

  if (!isValidUrl(url)) {
    res.status(400).json({ error: "url must be a valid URL" });
    return;
  }

  const title = body.title === undefined ? existing.title : normalizeRequiredString(body.title);
  if (!title) {
    res.status(400).json({ error: "title is required" });
    return;
  }

  const description = Object.prototype.hasOwnProperty.call(body, "description")
    ? normalizeDescription(body.description)
    : existing.description;
  const folderId = Object.prototype.hasOwnProperty.call(body, "folder_id")
    ? normalizeOptionalFolderId(body.folder_id)
    : { value: existing.folder_id };
  if (folderId.error || folderId.value === undefined) {
    res.status(400).json({ error: folderId.error });
    return;
  }
  const now = new Date().toISOString();

  updateBookmark.run(url, title, description, folderId.value, now, id);

  res.status(200).json(getBookmarkById.get(id));
});

app.delete("/bookmarks/:id", (req, res) => {
  const id = parseId(req.params.id);
  if (!id) {
    res.status(404).json({ error: "bookmark not found" });
    return;
  }

  const result = deleteBookmark.run(id);
  if (result.changes === 0) {
    res.status(404).json({ error: "bookmark not found" });
    return;
  }

  res.status(204).send();
});

app.post("/folders", (req, res) => {
  const name = normalizeRequiredString((req.body as FolderBody).name);
  if (!name) {
    res.status(400).json({ error: "name is required" });
    return;
  }

  const now = new Date().toISOString();
  const insertResult = createFolder.run(name, now, now);
  const folder = getFolderById.get(Number(insertResult.lastInsertRowid));

  res.status(201).json(folder);
});

app.get("/folders", (_req, res) => {
  res.status(200).json(listFolders.all());
});

app.get("/folders/:id", (req, res) => {
  const id = parseId(req.params.id);
  if (!id) {
    res.status(404).json({ error: "folder not found" });
    return;
  }

  const folder = getFolderById.get(id);
  if (!folder) {
    res.status(404).json({ error: "folder not found" });
    return;
  }

  res.status(200).json({
    ...folder,
    bookmarks: listBookmarksByFolderId.all(id),
  });
});

app.put("/folders/:id", (req, res) => {
  const id = parseId(req.params.id);
  if (!id) {
    res.status(404).json({ error: "folder not found" });
    return;
  }

  const existing = getFolderById.get(id);
  if (!existing) {
    res.status(404).json({ error: "folder not found" });
    return;
  }

  const name = normalizeRequiredString((req.body as FolderBody).name);
  if (!name) {
    res.status(400).json({ error: "name is required" });
    return;
  }

  updateFolder.run(name, new Date().toISOString(), id);

  res.status(200).json(getFolderById.get(id));
});

app.delete("/folders/:id", (req, res) => {
  const id = parseId(req.params.id);
  if (!id) {
    res.status(404).json({ error: "folder not found" });
    return;
  }

  const existing = getFolderById.get(id);
  if (!existing) {
    res.status(404).json({ error: "folder not found" });
    return;
  }

  const now = new Date().toISOString();
  const removeFolder = db.transaction((folderId: number) => {
    clearFolderBookmarks.run(now, folderId);
    deleteFolder.run(folderId);
  });
  removeFolder(id);

  res.status(204).send();
});

app.listen(PORT, () => {
  console.log(`Server running on port ${PORT}`);
});

export default app;

benchmark/runs/phase-3-active-observability-cli-1.4.1-rerun-20260531/T4-authentication/events.jsonl:37:{"type":"item.completed","item":{"id":"item_18","type":"command_execution","command":"/bin/zsh -lc "sed -n '1,220p' docs/product/bookmarks.md"","aggregated_output":"# Bookmarks

Bookmarks are saved URLs with a title, optional description, optional folder, and creation/update timestamps. Folders are named collections of bookmarks.

## API Contract

- `POST /bookmarks` creates a bookmark from `url`, `title`, optional `description`, and optional `folder_id`.
- `GET /bookmarks` returns all bookmarks as a JSON array.
- `GET /bookmarks/:id` returns one bookmark or `404` when it does not exist.
- `PUT /bookmarks/:id` updates an existing bookmark, including optional `folder_id`, and returns it, or `404` when it does not exist.
- `DELETE /bookmarks/:id` removes an existing bookmark and returns `204`, or `404` when it does not exist.
- `POST /folders` creates a folder from required `name` and returns `201`.
- `GET /folders` returns all folders as a JSON array.
- `GET /folders/:id` returns one folder with a `bookmarks` array or `404` when it does not exist.
- `PUT /folders/:id` updates an existing folder name and returns it, or `404` when it does not exist.
- `DELETE /folders/:id` removes a folder and returns `204`, or `404` when it does not exist. Bookmarks in the deleted folder are not deleted; their `folder_id` becomes `null`.

## Data Contract

Bookmark records are stored in SQLite `data.db` inn./node_modules/strip-json-comments/readme.md

// See PRODUCT_SPEC.md for full requirements
// The agent will build this out across tasks T1-T6

import express from "express";
import { db } from "./database";

const app = express();
const PORT = Number(process.env.PORT ?? 3000);

app.use(express.json());

app.get("/health", (_req, res) => {
  res.status(200).json({ status: "ok" });
});

app.listen(PORT, () => {
  db.prepare("SELECT 1").get();
  console.log(`Server running on port ${PORT}`);
});

export default app;

benchmark/runs/baseline-main-20260527/T2-crud-bookmarks/events.jsonl:47:{"type":"item.completed","item":{"id":"item_23","type":"command_execution","command":"/bin/zsh -lc "sed -n '1,220p' benchmark/tasks/T5-bug-fix.md"","aggregated_output":"# Task T5: Bug Fix

## Context

You are continuing work on a Bookmark Manager API with full CRUD, folders, and authentication. Read `PRODUCT_SPEC.md` for the full specification.

## Bug Report

**Title**: Server returns 500 instead of 400 when creating a bookmark with an empty title

**Steps to reproduce**:
1. Login to get a valid token
2. `POST /bookmarks` with body `{"url": "https://example.com", "title": ""}`
3. Expected: 400 Bad Request with error message
4. Actual: 500 Internal Server Error (likely database constraint violation bubbling up)

**Additional observations**:
- Same issue may exist for empty `url` field
- Same issue may exist for folder `name` field
- The validation should catch empty strings BEFORE attempting database insertion

## Task

1. Diagnose the root cause of the 500 error
2. Fix the validation to properly reject empty strings (not just missing fields)
3. Ensure consistent validation across all creation/update endpoints:
   - `POST /bookmarks`: `title` and `url` must be non-empty strings
   - `PUT /bookmarks/:id`: if `title` or `url` is provided, must be non-empty
   - `POST /folders`: `name` must be non-empty string
   - `PUT /folders/:id`: if `name` is provided, must be non-empty
4. Return appropriate 400 responses with descriptive error messages

## Acceptance Criteria

- `POST /bookmarks` with `{"url": "https://example.com", "title": ""}` → 400 (not 500)
- `POST /bookmarks` with `{"url": "", "title": "Test"}` → 400
- `POST /folders` with `{"name": ""}` → 400
- All previous functionality still works
- Error responses include a `message` or `error` field explaining what's wrong
- No 500 errors for validation issues

## Notes

- This is a targeted fix — don't refactor unrelated code
- The bug likely exists because validation checks for `undefined`/`null` but not empty string `""`
- Add validation for whitespace-only strings too (e.g., `"   "` should be rejected)

    29	  const id = Number(req.params.id);
    30	  return Number.isInteger(id) && id > 0 ? id : null;
    31	}
    32	
    33	function normalizeDescription(value: unknown): string | null {
    34	  if (value === undefined || value === null) {
    35	    return null;
    36	  }
    37	
    38	  if (typeof value !== "string") {
    39	    return String(value);
    40	  }
    41	
    42	  return value;
    43	}
    44	
    45	export function createBookmarksRouter(db: SqliteDatabase): Router {
    46	  const router = Router();
    47	
    48	  router.post("/", (req, res) => {
    49	    const body = req.body as BookmarkInput;
    50	
    51	    if (!isNonEmptyString(body.url)) {
    52	      sendValidationError(res, "url is required");
    53	      return;
    54	    }
    55	
    56	    if (!isNonEmptyString(body.title)) {
    57	      sendValidationError(res, "title is required");
    58	      return;
    59	    }
    60	
    61	    const now = new Date().toISOString();
    62	    const result = db
    63	      .prepare(
    64	        `
    65	        INSERT INTO bookmarks (url, title, description, created_at, updated_at)
    66	        VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?)
    67	      `,
    68	      )
    69	      .run(
    70	        body.url.trim(),
    71	        body.title.trim(),
    72	        normalizeDescription(body.description),
    73	        now,
    74	        now,
    75	      );
    76	
    77	    const bookmark = db
    78	      .prepare("SELECT * FROM bookmarks WHERE id = ?")
    79	      .get(result.lastInsertRowid) as Bookmark;
    80	
    81	    res.status(201).json(bookmark);
    82	  });
    83	
    84	  router.get("/", (_req, res) => {
    85	    const bookmarks = db
    86	      .prepare("SELECT * FROM bookmarks ORDER BY id ASC")
    87	      .all() as Bookmark[];
    88	
    89	    res.status(200).json(bookmarks);
    90	  });
    91	
    92	  router.get("/:id", (req, res) => {
    93	    const id = parseBookmarkId(req);
    94	
    95	    if (id === null) {
    96	      res.status(404).json({ error: "bookmark not found" });
    97	      return;
    98	    }
    99	
   100	    const bookmark = db
   101	      .prepare("SELECT * FROM bookmarks WHERE id = ?")
   102	      .get(id) as Bookmark | undefined;
   103	
   104	    if (!bookmark) {
   105	      res.status(404).json({ error: "bookmark not found" });
   106	      return;
   107	    }
   108	
   109	    res.status(200).json(bookmark);
   110	  });
   111	
   112	  router.put("/:id", (req, res) => {
   113	    const id = parseBookmarkId(req);
   114	
   115	    if (id === null) {
   116	      res.status(404).json({ error: "bookmark not found" });
   117	      return;
   118	    }
   119	
   120	    const existing = db
   121	      .prepare("SELECT * FROM bookmarks WHERE id = ?")
   122	      .get(id) as Bookmark | undefined;
   123	
   124	    if (!existing) {
   125	      res.status(404).json({ error: "bookmark not found" });
   126	      return;
   127	    }
   128	
   129	    const body = req.body as BookmarkInput;
   130	
   131	    if (body.url !== undefined && !isNonEmptyString(body.url)) {
   132	      sendValidationError(res, "url must be a non-empty string");
   133	      return;
   134	    }
   135	
   136	    if (body.title !== undefined && !isNonEmptyString(body.title)) {
   137	      sendValidationError(res, "title must be a non-empty string");
   138	      return;
   139	    }
   140	
   141	    const updatedAt = new Date().toISOString();
   142	    const nextUrl = body.url === undefined ? existing.url : body.url.trim();
   143	    const nextTitle =
   144	      body.title === undefined ? existing.title : body.title.trim();
   145	    const nextDescription =
   146	      body.description === undefined
   147	        ? existing.description
   148	        : normalizeDescription(body.description);
   149	
   150	    db.prepare(
   151	      `
   152	      UPDATE bookmarks
   153	      SET url = ?, title = ?, description = ?, updated_at = ?
   154	      WHERE id = ?
   155	    `,
   156	    ).run(nextUrl, nextTitle, nextDescription, updatedAt, id);
   157	
   158	    const bookmark = db
   159	      .prepare("SELECT * FROM bookmarks WHERE id = ?")
   160	      .get(id) as Bookmark;
   161	
   162	    res.status(200).json(bookmark);
   163	  });
   164	
   165	  router.delete("/:id", (req, res) => {
   166	    const id = parseBookmarkId(req);
   167	
   168	    if (id === null) {
   169	      res.status(404).json({ error: "bookmark not found" });
   170	      return;
   171	    }
   172	
   173	    const result = db.prepare("DELETE FROM bookmarks WHERE id = ?").run(id);
   174	
   175	    if (result.changes === 0) {
   176	      res.status(404).json({ error: "bookmark not found" });
   177	      return;
   178	    }
   179	
   180	    res.status(204).send();
   181	  });
   182	
   183	  return router;
   184	}

benchmark/runs/baseline-main-20260527/T1-project-setup/events.jsonl:13:{"type":"item.completed","item":{"id":"item_4","type":"command_execution","command":"/bin/zsh -lc "sed -n '1,220p' README.md"","aggregated_output":"# Harness Benchmark

A controlled benchmark for measuring the effectiveness of [harness-experimental](https://github.com/hoangnb24/harness-experimental) across development phases.

## What This Is

A pre-seeded TypeScript/Express project with **6 fixed tasks** (T1–T6) that an AI agent executes. The benchmark measures time, token cost, code quality, and harness compliance. By re-running after each harness phase, we objectively measure whether the harness improved agent productivity.

## Quick Start

### Prerequisites

```bash
# Codex CLI (primary agent)
npm install -g @openai/codex
export OPENAI_API_KEY="sk-..."

# System dependencies
node --version  # v20+
jq --version    # for JSON parsing
sqlite3 --version  # for harness compliance checks
```

### Run the Benchmark

```bash
# Baseline run (against harness main branch)
./benchmark/run.sh --agent codex --harness main --run-id baseline

# Phase 2 run (against feature branch)
./benchmark/run.sh --agent codex --harness phase-2/observability-taxonomy --run-id phase-2

# Compare results
./benchmark/compare.sh baseline phase-2
```

### View Results

```bash
cat benchmark/runs/<run-id>/report.md     # Human-readable summary
cat benchmark/runs/<run-id>/scores.json   # Machine-readable scores
```

## Project Structure

```
harness-benchmark/
├── README.md                 # This file
├── PRODUCT_SPEC.md           # Product specification (what the agent builds)
├── package.json              # Pre-seeded dependencies (Express, better-sqlite3, vitest)
├── tsconfig.json             # TypeScript configuration
├── src/
│   └── index.ts              # Empty entrypoint (agent builds from here)
├── benchmark/
│   ├── PROTOCOL.md           # How runs work, what's measured, rules
│   ├── run.sh                # Main orchestrator script
│   ├── compare.sh            # Compare two run results
│   ├── tasks/                # Task prompts (T1-T6.md)
│   ├── rubrics/              # Objective scoring checklists
│   ├── lib/                  # Runner helper scripts
│   │   ├── prepare.sh        # Harness installation
│   │   ├── invoke.sh         # Agent invocation + telemetry
│   │   ├── check-functional.sh
│   │   ├── check-harness.sh
│   │   ├── check-quality.sh
│   │   └── report.sh
│   ├── runs/                 # Output directory (git-tracked results)
│   │   └── .gitkeep
│   └── seeds/                # Checkpoint states for partial re-runs
│       └── .gitkeep
```

## The Benchmark Cycle

1. **Tag** benchmark repo → `benchmark-v1`
2. **Install harness** from target ref (main, feature branch)
3. **Run T1–T6** sequentially via Codex CLI
4. **Score** against objective rubrics
5. **Compare** to previous runs

Each phase must **earn its merge** by moving the numbers.

## Tasks Overview

| Task | Name | Risk Lane | What It Tests |
|------|------|-----------|---------------|
| T1 | Project Setup | tiny | Basic scaffolding, health endpoint |
| T2 | CRUD Bookmarks | normal | Core API implementation |
| T3 | Folder Support | normal | Feature addition on existing code |
| T4 | Authentication | high-risk | Complex feature with security implications |
| T5 | Bug Fix | normal | Diagnosis and targeted fix |
| T6 | Pagination | normal | Refactoring existing API responses |

## Metrics

| Metric | What It Measures | Source |
|--------|-----------------|--------|
| Wall time | Speed of completion | `timing.json` |
| Token cost | API cost efficiency | `tokens.json` (from Codex JSONL) |
| Functional score | Does the code work? | `check-functional.sh` (curl tests) |
| Harness compliance | Did the agent use the harness? | `check-harness.sh` (sqlite3 queries) |
| Trace quality | How detailed are the traces? | `check-quality.sh` |
| Lane accuracy | Correct risk classification? | `lane.json` |

benchmark/runs/baseline-main-20260527/T1-project-setup/events.jsonl:16:{"type":"item.completed","item":{"id":"item_7","type":"command_execution","command":"/bin/zsh -lc "sed -n '1,240p' PRODUCT_SPEC.md"","aggregated_output":"# Product Specification: Bookmark Manager API

## Overview

A REST API for managing bookmarks with folder organization, user authentication, and pagination. Built with TypeScript, Express, and better-sqlite3.

## Functional Requirements

### Core Entities

- **Bookmark**: A saved URL with title, optional description, timestamps
- **Folder**: A named collection of bookmarks
- **User**: An authenticated account that owns bookmarks and folders

### API Endpoints

#### Health
- `GET /health` → `{ "status": "ok" }`

#### Bookmarks (after auth: scoped to authenticated user)
- `POST /bookmarks` — Create a bookmark (requires: url, title)
- `GET /bookmarks` — List all bookmarks (paginated after T6)
- `GET /bookmarks/:id` — Get single bookmark
- `PUT /bookmarks/:id` — Update a bookmark
- `DELETE /bookmarks/:id` — Delete a bookmark

#### Folders
- `POST /folders` — Create a folder (requires: name)
- `GET /folders` — List all folders
- `GET /folders/:id` — Get folder with its bookmarks
- `PUT /folders/:id` — Update folder name
- `DELETE /folders/:id` — Delete folder (bookmarks become unfoldered)

#### Authentication
- `POST /auth/register` — Create account (email + password)
- `POST /auth/login` — Get JWT token
- All bookmark/folder endpoints require `Authorization: Bearer <token>` after auth is implemented

### Validation Rules

- Bookmark `url` must be a valid URL
- Bookmark `title` must be non-empty string
- Folder `name` must be non-empty string
- Email must be valid format
- Password must be >= 8 characters

### Pagination (after T6)

- `GET /bookmarks?page=1&limit=20`
- Response shape: `{ data: [...], page: 1, limit: 20, total: 45 }`
- Default: page=1, limit=20
- Max limit: 100

## Technical Stack (Pre-decided)

- **Runtime**: Node.js 20+
- **Language**: TypeScript
- **Framework**: Express
- **Database**: SQLite via better-sqlite3
- **Testing**: Vitest
- **Port**: 3000

## Non-Functional Requirements

- All responses are JSON
- Proper HTTP status codes (200, 201, 204, 400, 401, 404, 500)
- Passwords stored as hashes (bcrypt or similar)
- JWT tokens for session management
- Database file: `data.db` (application data)

benchmark/runs/baseline-main-20260527/T3-folder-support/events.jsonl:10:{"type":"item.completed","item":{"id":"item_4","type":"command_execution","command":"/bin/zsh -lc "sed -n '1,220p' README.md"","aggregated_output":"# Harness Benchmark

A controlled benchmark for measuring the effectiveness of [harness-experimental](https://github.com/hoangnb24/harness-experimental) across development phases.

## What This Is

A pre-seeded TypeScript/Express project with **6 fixed tasks** (T1–T6) that an AI agent executes. The benchmark measures time, token cost, code quality, and harness compliance. By re-running after each harness phase, we objectively measure whether the harness improved agent productivity.

## Quick Start

### Prerequisites

```bash
# Codex CLI (primary agent)
npm install -g @openai/codex
export OPENAI_API_KEY="sk-..."

# System dependencies
node --version  # v20+
jq --version    # for JSON parsing
sqlite3 --version  # for harness compliance checks
```

### Run the Benchmark

```bash
# Baseline run (against harness main branch)
./benchmark/run.sh --agent codex --harness main --run-id baseline

# Phase 2 run (against feature branch)
./benchmark/run.sh --agent codex --harness phase-2/observability-taxonomy --run-id phase-2

# Compare results
./benchmark/compare.sh baseline phase-2
```

### View Results

```bash
cat benchmark/runs/<run-id>/report.md     # Human-readable summary
cat benchmark/runs/<run-id>/scores.json   # Machine-readable scores
```

## Project Structure

```
harness-benchmark/
├── README.md                 # This file
├── PRODUCT_SPEC.md           # Product specification (what the agent builds)
├── package.json              # Pre-seeded dependencies (Express, better-sqlite3, vitest)
├── tsconfig.json             # TypeScript configuration
├── src/
│   └── index.ts              # Empty entrypoint (agent builds from here)
├── benchmark/
│   ├── PROTOCOL.md           # How runs work, what's measured, rules
│   ├── run.sh                # Main orchestrator script
│   ├── compare.sh            # Compare two run results
│   ├── tasks/                # Task prompts (T1-T6.md)
│   ├── rubrics/              # Objective scoring checklists
│   ├── lib/                  # Runner helper scripts
│   │   ├── prepare.sh        # Harness installation
│   │   ├── invoke.sh         # Agent invocation + telemetry
│   │   ├── check-functional.sh
│   │   ├── check-harness.sh
│   │   ├── check-quality.sh
│   │   └── report.sh
│   ├── runs/                 # Output directory (git-tracked results)
│   │   └── .gitkeep
│   └── seeds/                # Checkpoint states for partial re-runs
│       └── .gitkeep
```

## The Benchmark Cycle

1. **Tag** benchmark repo → `benchmark-v1`
2. **Install harness** from target ref (main, feature branch)
3. **Run T1–T6** sequentially via Codex CLI
4. **Score** against objective rubrics
5. **Compare** to previous runs

Each phase must **earn its merge** by moving the numbers.

## Tasks Overview

| Task | Name | Risk Lane | What It Tests |
|------|------|-----------|---------------|
| T1 | Project Setup | tiny | Basic scaffolding, health endpoint |
| T2 | CRUD Bookmarks | normal | Core API implementation |
| T3 | Folder Support | normal | Feature addition on existing code |
| T4 | Authentication | high-risk | Complex feature with security implications |
| T5 | Bug Fix | normal | Diagnosis and targeted fix |
| T6 | Pagination | normal | Refactoring existing API responses |

## Metrics

| Metric | What It Measures | Source |
|--------|-----------------|--------|
| Wall time | Speed of completion | `timing.json` |
| Token cost | API cost efficiency | `tokens.json` (from Codex JSONL) |
| Functional score | Does the code work? | `check-functional.sh` (curl tests) |
| Harness compliance | Did the agent use the harness? | `check-harness.sh` (sqlite3 queries) |
| Trace quality | How detailed are the traces? | `check-quality.sh` |
| Lane accuracy | Correct risk classification? | `lane.json` |

## License

MIT

benchmark/runs/baseline-main-20260527/T3-folder-support/events.jsonl:18:{"type":"item.completed","item":{"id":"item_8","type":"command_execution","command":"/bin/zsh -lc "sed -n '1,260p' PRODUCT_SPEC.md"","aggregated_output":"# Product Specification: Bookmark Manager API

## Overview

A REST API for managing bookmarks with folder organization, user authentication, and pagination. Built with TypeScript, Express, and better-sqlite3.

## Functional Requirements

### Core Entities

- **Bookmark**: A saved URL with title, optional description, timestamps
- **Folder**: A named collection of bookmarks
- **User**: An authenticated account that owns bookmarks and folders

### API Endpoints

#### Health
- `GET /health` → `{ "status": "ok" }`

#### Bookmarks (after auth: scoped to authenticated user)
- `POST /bookmarks` — Create a bookmark (requires: url, title)
- `GET /bookmarks` — List all bookmarks (paginated after T6)
- `GET /bookmarks/:id` — Get single bookmark
- `PUT /bookmarks/:id` — Update a bookmark
- `DELETE /bookmarks/:id` — Delete a bookmark

#### Folders
- `POST /folders` — Create a folder (requires: name)
- `GET /folders` — List all folders
- `GET /folders/:id` — Get folder with its bookmarks
- `PUT /folders/:id` — Update folder name
- `DELETE /folders/:id` — Delete folder (bookmarks become unfoldered)

#### Authentication
- `POST /auth/register` — Create account (email + password)
- `POST /auth/login` — Get JWT token
- All bookmark/folder endpoints require `Authorization: Bearer <token>` after auth is implemented

### Validation Rules

- Bookmark `url` must be a valid URL
- Bookmark `title` must be non-empty string
- Folder `name` must be non-empty string
- Email must be valid format
- Password must be >= 8 characters

### Pagination (after T6)

- `GET /bookmarks?page=1&limit=20`
- Response shape: `{ data: [...], page: 1, limit: 20, total: 45 }`
- Default: page=1, limit=20
- Max limit: 100

## Technical Stack (Pre-decided)

- **Runtime**: Node.js 20+
- **Language**: TypeScript
- **Framework**: Express
- **Database**: SQLite via better-sqlite3
- **Testing**: Vitest
- **Port**: 3000

## Non-Functional Requirements

- All responses are JSON
- Proper HTTP status codes (200, 201, 204, 400, 401, 404, 500)
- Passwords stored as hashes (bcrypt or similar)
- JWT tokens for session management
- Database file: `data.db` (application data)

export const db: SqliteDatabase = new Database(process.env.DB_FILE ?? "data.db");

db.pragma("foreign_keys = ON");

export function initializeDatabase(database: SqliteDatabase = db): void {
  database
    .prepare(
      `
      CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS bookmarks (
        id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,
        url TEXT NOT NULL,
        title TEXT NOT NULL,
        description TEXT,
        created_at TEXT NOT NULL,
        updated_at TEXT NOT NULL
      )
    `,
    )
    .run();
}

benchmark/runs/baseline-main-20260527/T3-folder-support/events.jsonl:28:{"type":"item.completed","item":{"id":"item_13","type":"command_execution","command":"/bin/zsh -lc "sed -n '1,320p' src/bookmarks.ts"","aggregated_output":"import { Router } from "express";
import type { Database as SqliteDatabase } from "better-sqlite3";
import type { Request, Response } from "express";

type Bookmark = {
  id: number;
  url: string;
  title: string;
  description: string | null;
  created_at: string;
  updated_at: string;
};

type BookmarkInput = {
  url?: unknown;
  title?: unknown;
  description?: unknown;
};

function sendValidationError(res: Response, message: string): void {
  res.status(400).json({ error: message });
}

function isNonEmptyString(value: unknown): value is string {
  return typeof value === "string" && value.trim().length > 0;
}

function parseBookmarkId(req: Request): number | null {
  const id = Number(req.params.id);
  return Number.isInteger(id) && id > 0 ? id : null;
}

function normalizeDescription(value: unknown): string | null {
  if (value === undefined || value === null) {
    return null;
  }

  if (typeof value !== "string") {
    return String(value);
  }

  return value;
}

export function createBookmarksRouter(db: SqliteDatabase): Router {
  const router = Router();

  router.post("/", (req, res) => {
    const body = req.body as BookmarkInput;

    if (!isNonEmptyString(body.url)) {
      sendValidationError(res, "url is required");
      return;
    }

    if (!isNonEmptyString(body.title)) {
      sendValidationError(res, "title is required");
      return;
    }

    const now = new Date().toISOString();
    const result = db
      .prepare(
        `
        INSERT INTO bookmarks (url, title, description, created_at, updated_at)
        VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?)
      `,
      )
      .run(
        body.url.trim(),
        body.title.trim(),
        normalizeDescription(body.description),
        now,
        now,
      );

    const bookmark = db
      .prepare("SELECT * FROM bookmarks WHERE id = ?")
      .get(result.lastInsertRowid) as Bookmark;

    res.status(201).json(bookmark);
  });

  router.get("/", (_req, res) => {
    const bookmarks = db
      .prepare("SELECT * FROM bookmarks ORDER BY id ASC")
      .all() as Bookmark[];

    res.status(200).json(bookmarks);
  });

  router.get("/:id", (req, res) => {
    const id = parseBookmarkId(req);

    if (id === null) {
      res.status(404).json({ error: "bookmark not found" });
      return;
    }

    const bookmark = db
      .prepare("SELECT * FROM bookmarks WHERE id = ?")
      .get(id) as Bookmark | undefined;

    if (!bookmark) {
      res.status(404).json({ error: "bookmark not found" });
      return;
    }

    res.status(200).json(bookmark);
  });

  router.put("/:id", (req, res) => {
    const id = parseBookmarkId(req);

    if (id === null) {
      res.status(404).json({ error: "bookmark not found" });
      return;
    }

    const existing = db
      .prepare("SELECT * FROM bookmarks WHERE id = ?")
      .get(id) as Bookmark | undefined;

    if (!existing) {
      res.status(404).json({ error: "bookmark not found" });
      return;
    }

    const body = req.body as BookmarkInput;

    if (body.url !== undefined && !isNonEmptyString(body.url)) {
      sendValidationError(res, "url must be a non-empty string");
      return;
    }

    if (body.title !== undefined && !isNonEmptyString(body.title)) {
      sendValidationError(res, "title must be a non-empty string");
      return;
    }

    const updatedAt = new Date().toISOString();
    const nextUrl = body.url === undefined ? existing.url : body.url.trim();
    const nextTitle =
      body.title === undefined ? existing.title : body.title.trim();
    const nextDescription =
      body.description === undefined
        ? existing.description
        : normalizeDescription(body.description);

    db.prepare(
      `
      UPDATE bookmarks
      SET url = ?, title = ?, description = ?, updated_at = ?
      WHERE id = ?
    `,
    ).run(nextUrl, nextTitle, nextDescription, updatedAt, id);

    const bookmark = db
      .prepare("SELECT * FROM bookmarks WHERE id = ?")
      .get(id) as Bookmark;

    res.status(200).json(bookmark);
  });

  router.delete("/:id", (req, res) => {
    const id = parseBookmarkId(req);

    if (id === null) {
      res.status(404).json({ error: "bookmark not found" });
      return;
    }

    const result = db.prepare("DELETE FROM bookmarks WHERE id = ?").run(id);

    if (result.changes === 0) {
      res.status(404).json({ error: "bookmark not found" });
      return;
    }

    res.status(204).send();
  });

  return router;
}

type BookmarkResponse = {

  description: string | null;

};

let server: ReturnType<typeof app.listen>;
let baseUrl: string;

async function request(path: string, init?: RequestInit): Promise<Response> {
  return fetch(`${baseUrl}${path}`, {
    ...init,
    headers: {
      "content-type": "application/json",
      ...(init?.headers ?? {}),
    },
  });
}

beforeEach(() => {
  initializeDatabase();
  db.prepare("DELETE FROM bookmarks").run();
});

afterAll(() => {
  server?.close();
  db.close();
});

describe("bookmark CRUD API", () => {
  beforeEach(async () => {
    if (!server) {
      server = app.listen(0);
      await new Promise<void>((resolve) => server.once("listening", resolve));
      const address = server.address();

      if (!address || typeof address === "string") {
        throw new Error("test server did not bind to a TCP port");
      }

      baseUrl = `http://127.0.0.1:${address.port}`;
    }
  });

  it("keeps the health endpoint working", async () => {
    const response = await request("/health");

    expect(response.status).toBe(200);
    await expect(response.json()).resolves.toEqual({ status: "ok" });
  });

  it("creates, lists, gets, updates, and deletes bookmarks", async () => {
    const createResponse = await request("/bookmarks", {
      method: "POST",
      body: JSON.stringify({
        url: "https://example.com",
        title: "Test",
        description: "Example bookmark",
      }),
    });

    expect(createResponse.status).toBe(201);
    const created = (await createResponse.json()) as BookmarkResponse;
    expect(created).toMatchObject({
      id: 1,
      url: "https://example.com",
      title: "Test",
      description: "Example bookmark",
    });
    expect(created.created_at).toEqual(expect.any(String));
    expect(created.updated_at).toEqual(expect.any(String));

    const listResponse = await request("/bookmarks");
    expect(listResponse.status).toBe(200);
    await expect(listResponse.json()).resolves.toEqual([created]);

    const getResponse = await request("/bookmarks/1");
    expect(getResponse.status).toBe(200);
    await expect(getResponse.json()).resolves.toEqual(created);

    const updateResponse = await request("/bookmarks/1", {
      method: "PUT",
      body: JSON.stringify({ title: "Updated" }),
    });
    expect(updateResponse.status).toBe(200);
    const updated = (await updateResponse.json()) as BookmarkResponse;
    expect(updated).toMatchObject({
      id: 1,
      url: "https://example.com",
      title: "Updated",
      description: "Example bookmark",
      created_at: created.created_at,
    });

    const deleteResponse = await request("/bookmarks/1", { method: "DELETE" });
    expect(deleteResponse.status).toBe(204);

    const missingResponse = await request("/bookmarks/1");
    expect(missingResponse.status).toBe(404);
  });

  it("returns 400 for missing or empty required create fields", async () => {
    const missingTitle = await request("/bookmarks", {
      method: "POST",
      body: JSON.stringify({ url: "https://example.com" }),
    });
    expect(missingTitle.status).toBe(400);

    const missingUrl = await request("/bookmarks", {
      method: "POST",
      body: JSON.stringify({ title: "No URL" }),
    });
    expect(missingUrl.status).toBe(400);

    const emptyTitle = await request("/bookmarks", {
      method: "POST",
      body: JSON.stringify({ url: "https://example.com", title: "" }),
    });
    expect(emptyTitle.status).toBe(400);
  });

  it("returns 404 for missing bookmarks on read, update, and delete", async () => {
    const getResponse = await request("/bookmarks/9999");
    expect(getResponse.status).toBe(404);

    const updateResponse = await request("/bookmarks/9999", {
      method: "PUT",
      body: JSON.stringify({ title: "Updated" }),
    });
    expect(updateResponse.status).toBe(404);

    const deleteResponse = await request("/bookmarks/9999", {
      method: "DELETE",
    });
    expect(deleteResponse.status).toBe(404);
  });
});

# T2 CRUD Bookmarks

## Status

implemented

## Lane

normal

## Product Contract

The Bookmark Manager API persists bookmarks in SQLite `data.db` and exposes
create, list, read, update, and delete operations under `/bookmarks`.

## Relevant Product Docs

- `PRODUCT_SPEC.md`

## Acceptance Criteria

- `POST /bookmarks` creates a bookmark with `url`, `title`, optional
  `description`, and ISO timestamp fields.
- `GET /bookmarks` returns all bookmarks as a JSON array.
- `GET /bookmarks/:id` returns a bookmark or 404.
- `PUT /bookmarks/:id` updates an existing bookmark or returns 404.
- `DELETE /bookmarks/:id` deletes an existing bookmark with 204 or returns 404.
- Missing or empty required create fields return 400.
- `GET /health` still returns 200.

## Design Notes

- Commands: create, update, and delete bookmarks.
- Queries: list all bookmarks and read one bookmark by id.
- API: Express router mounted at `/bookmarks`.
- Tables: `bookmarks(id, url, title, description, created_at, updated_at)`.
- Domain rules: `url` and `title` must be non-empty strings on create;
  provided `url` or `title` values must be non-empty on update.
- UI surfaces: none.

## Validation

| Layer | Expected proof |
| --- | --- |
| Unit | Not required for this API slice |
| Integration | Vitest exercises all CRUD endpoints and validation cases |
| E2E | Not required for API-only slice |
| Platform | `npm run build`; curl checks against local server; SQLite schema check |
| Release | Not required |

## Harness Delta

No harness changes needed.

## Evidence

- `npm run build` succeeded.
- `npm test` passed 4 integration tests for health, bookmark CRUD,
  validation, and 404 behavior.
- Manual curl checks against `PORT=3102 npm run dev` verified:
  - `GET /health` returned 200.
  - `POST /bookmarks` returned 201 with the created bookmark.
  - `GET /bookmarks` returned 200 with an array.
  - `GET /bookmarks/1` returned 200.
  - `PUT /bookmarks/1` returned 200 with the updated bookmark.
  - `DELETE /bookmarks/1` returned 204.
  - missing `title`, missing `url`, and empty `title` returned 400.
  - missing bookmark reads, updates, and deletes returned 404.
- `sqlite3 data.db '.schema bookmarks'` showed the required table columns.
- `sqlite3 data.db 'SELECT ... FROM bookmarks;'` showed persisted bookmark
  data before deletion.

import type { Database as SqliteDatabase } from "better-sqlite3";
import type { Request, Response } from "express";

type Bookmark = {
  id: number;
  url: string;
  title: string;
  description: string | null;
  folder_id: number | null;
  created_at: string;
  updated_at: string;
};

type BookmarkInput = {
  url?: unknown;
  title?: unknown;
  description?: unknown;
  folder_id?: unknown;
};

function sendValidationError(res: Response, message: string): void {
  res.status(400).json({ error: message });
}

function isNonEmptyString(value: unknown): value is string {
  return typeof value === "string" && value.trim().length > 0;
}

function parseBookmarkId(req: Request): number | null {
  const id = Number(req.params.id);
  return Number.isInteger(id) && id > 0 ? id : null;
}

function normalizeDescription(value: unknown): string | null {
  if (value === undefined || value === null) {
    return null;
  }

  if (typeof value !== "string") {
    return String(value);
  }

  return value;
}

function parseOptionalFolderId(value: unknown): number | null | undefined {
  if (value === undefined) {
    return undefined;
  }

  if (value === null) {
    return null;
  }

  const id = Number(value);
  return Number.isInteger(id) && id > 0 ? id : null;
}

function folderExists(db: SqliteDatabase, folderId: number): boolean {
  const folder = db
    .prepare("SELECT id FROM folders WHERE id = ?")
    .get(folderId) as { id: number } | undefined;

  return folder !== undefined;
}

function validateFolderId(
  db: SqliteDatabase,
  res: Response,
  value: unknown,
): number | null | undefined {
  const folderId = parseOptionalFolderId(value);

  if (folderId === undefined || folderId === null) {
    return folderId;
  }

  if (!folderExists(db, folderId)) {
    sendValidationError(res, "folder_id must reference an existing folder");
    return undefined;
  }

  return folderId;
}

export function createBookmarksRouter(db: SqliteDatabase): Router {
  const router = Router();

  router.post("/", (req, res) => {
    const body = req.body as BookmarkInput;

    if (!isNonEmptyString(body.url)) {
      sendValidationError(res, "url is required");
      return;
    }

    if (!isNonEmptyString(body.title)) {
      sendValidationError(res, "title is required");
      return;
    }

    const folderId = validateFolderId(db, res, body.folder_id);

    if (folderId === undefined && body.folder_id !== undefined) {
      return;
    }

    const now = new Date().toISOString();
    const result = db
      .prepare(
        `
        INSERT INTO bookmarks (url, title, description, folder_id, created_at, updated_at)
        VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?)
      `,
      )
      .run(
        body.url.trim(),
        body.title.trim(),
        normalizeDescription(body.description),
        folderId ?? null,
        now,
        now,
      );

    const bookmark = db
      .prepare("SELECT * FROM bookmarks WHERE id = ?")
      .get(result.lastInsertRowid) as Bookmark;

    res.status(201).json(bookmark);
  });

  router.get("/", (_req, res) => {
    const bookmarks = db
      .prepare("SELECT * FROM bookmarks ORDER BY id ASC")
      .all() as Bookmark[];

    res.status(200).json(bookmarks);
  });

  router.get("/:id", (req, res) => {
    const id = parseBookmarkId(req);

    if (id === null) {
      res.status(404).json({ error: "bookmark not found" });
      return;
    }

    const bookmark = db
      .prepare("SELECT * FROM bookmarks WHERE id = ?")
      .get(id) as Bookmark | undefined;

    if (!bookmark) {
      res.status(404).json({ error: "bookmark not found" });
      return;
    }

    res.status(200).json(bookmark);
  });

  router.put("/:id", (req, res) => {
    const id = parseBookmarkId(req);

    if (id === null) {
      res.status(404).json({ error: "bookmark not found" });
      return;
    }

    const existing = db
      .prepare("SELECT * FROM bookmarks WHERE id = ?")
      .get(id) as Bookmark | undefined;

    if (!existing) {
      res.status(404).json({ error: "bookmark not found" });
      return;
    }

    const body = req.body as BookmarkInput;

    if (body.url !== undefined && !isNonEmptyString(body.url)) {
      sendValidationError(res, "url must be a non-empty string");
      return;
    }

    if (body.title !== undefined && !isNonEmptyString(body.title)) {
      sendValidationError(res, "title must be a non-empty string");
      return;
    }

    const updatedAt = new Date().toISOString();
    const nextUrl = body.url === undefined ? existing.url : body.url.trim();
    const nextTitle =
      body.title === undefined ? existing.title : body.title.trim();
    const nextDescription =
      body.description === undefined
        ? existing.description
        : normalizeDescription(body.description);
    const folderId = validateFolderId(db, res, body.folder_id);

    if (folderId === undefined && body.folder_id !== undefined) {
      return;
    }

    const nextFolderId = body.folder_id === undefined ? existing.folder_id : folderId;

    db.prepare(
      `
      UPDATE bookmarks
      SET url = ?, title = ?, description = ?, folder_id = ?, updated_at = ?
      WHERE id = ?
    `,
    ).run(nextUrl, nextTitle, nextDescription, nextFolderId, updatedAt, id);

    const bookmark = db
      .prepare("SELECT * FROM bookmarks WHERE id = ?")
      .get(id) as Bookmark;

    res.status(200).json(bookmark);
  });

A controlled benchmark for measuring the effectiveness of [harness-experimental](https://github.com/hoangnb24/harness-experimental) across development phases.

## What This Is

A pre-seeded TypeScript/Express project with **6 fixed tasks** (T1–T6) that an AI agent executes. The benchmark measures time, token cost, code quality, and harness compliance. By re-running after each harness phase, we objectively measure whether the harness improved agent productivity.

## Quick Start

### Prerequisites

```bash
# Codex CLI (primary agent)
npm install -g @openai/codex
export OPENAI_API_KEY="sk-..."

# System dependencies
node --version  # v20+
jq --version    # for JSON parsing
sqlite3 --version  # for harness compliance checks
```

### Run the Benchmark

```bash
# Baseline run (against harness main branch)
./benchmark/run.sh --agent codex --harness main --run-id baseline

# Phase 2 run (against feature branch)
./benchmark/run.sh --agent codex --harness phase-2/observability-taxonomy --run-id phase-2

# Compare results
./benchmark/compare.sh baseline phase-2
```

### View Results

```bash
cat benchmark/runs/<run-id>/report.md     # Human-readable summary
cat benchmark/runs/<run-id>/scores.json   # Machine-readable scores
```

## Project Structure

```
harness-benchmark/
├── README.md                 # This file
├── PRODUCT_SPEC.md           # Product specification (what the agent builds)
├── package.json              # Pre-seeded dependencies (Express, better-sqlite3, vitest)
├── tsconfig.json             # TypeScript configuration
├── src/
│   └── index.ts              # Empty entrypoint (agent builds from here)
├── benchmark/
│   ├── PROTOCOL.md           # How runs work, what's measured, rules
│   ├── run.sh                # Main orchestrator script
│   ├── compare.sh            # Compare two run results
│   ├── tasks/                # Task prompts (T1-T6.md)
│   ├── rubrics/              # Objective scoring checklists
│   ├── lib/                  # Runner helper scripts
│   │   ├── prepare.sh        # Harness installation
│   │   ├── invoke.sh         # Agent invocation + telemetry
│   │   ├── check-functional.sh
│   │   ├── check-harness.sh
│   │   ├── check-quality.sh
│   │   └── report.sh
│   ├── runs/                 # Output directory (git-tracked results)
│   │   └── .gitkeep
│   └── seeds/                # Checkpoint states for partial re-runs
│       └── .gitkeep
```

## The Benchmark Cycle

1. **Tag** benchmark repo → `benchmark-v1`
2. **Install harness** from target ref (main, feature branch)
3. **Run T1–T6** sequentially via Codex CLI
4. **Score** against objective rubrics
5. **Compare** to previous runs

Each phase must **earn its merge** by moving the numbers.

## Tasks Overview

| Task | Name | Risk Lane | What It Tests |
|------|------|-----------|---------------|
| T1 | Project Setup | tiny | Basic scaffolding, health endpoint |
| T2 | CRUD Bookmarks | normal | Core API implementation |
| T3 | Folder Support | normal | Feature addition on existing code |
| T4 | Authentication | high-risk | Complex feature with security implications |
| T5 | Bug Fix | normal | Diagnosis and targeted fix |
| T6 | Pagination | normal | Refactoring existing API responses |

## Metrics

| Metric | What It Measures | Source |
|--------|-----------------|--------|
| Wall time | Speed of completion | `timing.json` |
| Token cost | API cost efficiency | `tokens.json` (from Codex JSONL) |
| Functional score | Does the code work? | `check-functional.sh` (curl tests) |
| Harness compliance | Did the agent use the harness? | `check-harness.sh` (sqlite3 queries) |
| Trace quality | How detailed are the traces? | `check-quality.sh` |
| Lane accuracy | Correct risk classification? | `lane.json` |

## Overview

A REST API for managing bookmarks with folder organization, user authentication, and pagination. Built with TypeScript, Express, and better-sqlite3.

## Functional Requirements

### Core Entities

- **Bookmark**: A saved URL with title, optional description, timestamps
- **Folder**: A named collection of bookmarks
- **User**: An authenticated account that owns bookmarks and folders

### API Endpoints

#### Health
- `GET /health` → `{ "status": "ok" }`

#### Bookmarks (after auth: scoped to authenticated user)
- `POST /bookmarks` — Create a bookmark (requires: url, title)
- `GET /bookmarks` — List all bookmarks (paginated after T6)
- `GET /bookmarks/:id` — Get single bookmark
- `PUT /bookmarks/:id` — Update a bookmark
- `DELETE /bookmarks/:id` — Delete a bookmark

#### Folders
- `POST /folders` — Create a folder (requires: name)
- `GET /folders` — List all folders
- `GET /folders/:id` — Get folder with its bookmarks
- `PUT /folders/:id` — Update folder name
- `DELETE /folders/:id` — Delete folder (bookmarks become unfoldered)

#### Authentication
- `POST /auth/register` — Create account (email + password)
- `POST /auth/login` — Get JWT token
- All bookmark/folder endpoints require `Authorization: Bearer <token>` after auth is implemented

### Validation Rules

- Bookmark `url` must be a valid URL
- Bookmark `title` must be non-empty string
- Folder `name` must be non-empty string
- Email must be valid format
- Password must be >= 8 characters

### Pagination (after T6)

- `GET /bookmarks?page=1&limit=20`
- Response shape: `{ data: [...], page: 1, limit: 20, total: 45 }`
- Default: page=1, limit=20
- Max limit: 100

## Technical Stack (Pre-decided)

- **Runtime**: Node.js 20+
- **Language**: TypeScript
- **Framework**: Express
- **Database**: SQLite via better-sqlite3
- **Testing**: Vitest
- **Port**: 3000

## Non-Functional Requirements

- All responses are JSON
- Proper HTTP status codes (200, 201, 204, 400, 401, 404, 500)
- Passwords stored as hashes (bcrypt or similar)
- JWT tokens for session management
- Database file: `data.db` (application data)

benchmark/runs/baseline-main-valid-node22-20260525/T3-folder-support/events.jsonl:26:{"type":"item.completed","item":{"id":"item_10","type":"command_execution","command":"/bin/zsh -lc "sed -n '1,320p' src/bookmarks.ts"","aggregated_output":"import { Router } from "express";
import type { Request, Response } from "express";
import type Database from "better-sqlite3";

type BookmarkRow = {
  id: number;
  url: string;
  title: string;
  description: string | null;
  created_at: string;
  updated_at: string;
};

type BookmarkResponse = {
  id: number;
  url: string;
  title: string;
  description: string | null;
  created_at: string;
  updated_at: string;
};

const toBookmarkResponse = (row: BookmarkRow): BookmarkResponse => ({
  id: row.id,
  url: row.url,
  title: row.title,
  description: row.description,
  created_at: row.created_at,
  updated_at: row.updated_at,
});

const getBookmarkId = (req: Request, res: Response): number | null => {
  const id = Number(req.params.id);

  if (!Number.isInteger(id) || id < 1) {
    res.status(404).json({ error: "Bookmark not found" });
    return null;
  }

  return id;
};

const readString = (value: unknown): string | undefined => {
  if (typeof value !== "string") {
    return undefined;
  }

  return value.trim();
};

export const createBookmarksRouter = (db: Database.Database): Router => {
  const router = Router();

  const findById = db.prepare("SELECT * FROM bookmarks WHERE id = ?");

  router.post("/", (req, res) => {
    const url = readString(req.body?.url);
    const title = readString(req.body?.title);
    const description = req.body?.description;

    if (!url) {
      return res.status(400).json({ error: "url is required" });
    }

    if (!title) {
      return res.status(400).json({ error: "title is required" });
    }

    const now = new Date().toISOString();
    const result = db
      .prepare(
        `
          INSERT INTO bookmarks (url, title, description, created_at, updated_at)
          VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?)
        `,
      )
      .run(url, title, typeof description === "string" ? description : null, now, now);

    const bookmark = findById.get(result.lastInsertRowid) as BookmarkRow;

    return res.status(201).json(toBookmarkResponse(bookmark));
  });

  router.get("/", (_req, res) => {
    const bookmarks = db
      .prepare("SELECT * FROM bookmarks ORDER BY id ASC")
      .all() as BookmarkRow[];

    return res.status(200).json(bookmarks.map(toBookmarkResponse));
  });

  router.get("/:id", (req, res) => {
    const id = getBookmarkId(req, res);
    if (id === null) {
      return;
    }

    const bookmark = findById.get(id) as BookmarkRow | undefined;
    if (!bookmark) {
      return res.status(404).json({ error: "Bookmark not found" });
    }

    return res.status(200).json(toBookmarkResponse(bookmark));
  });

  router.put("/:id", (req, res) => {
    const id = getBookmarkId(req, res);
    if (id === null) {
      return;
    }

    const existing = findById.get(id) as BookmarkRow | undefined;
    if (!existing) {
      return res.status(404).json({ error: "Bookmark not found" });
    }

    const url = req.body?.url === undefined ? existing.url : readString(req.body.url);
    const title =
      req.body?.title === undefined ? existing.title : readString(req.body.title);
    const description =
      req.body?.description === undefined
        ? existing.description
        : typeof req.body.description === "string"
          ? req.body.description
          : null;

    if (!url) {
      return res.status(400).json({ error: "url must be non-empty" });
    }

    if (!title) {
      return res.status(400).json({ error: "title must be non-empty" });
    }

    const updatedAt = new Date().toISOString();
    db.prepare(
      `
        UPDATE bookmarks
        SET url = ?, title = ?, description = ?, updated_at = ?
        WHERE id = ?
      `,
    ).run(url, title, description, updatedAt, id);

    const updated = findById.get(id) as BookmarkRow;

    return res.status(200).json(toBookmarkResponse(updated));
  });

  router.delete("/:id", (req, res) => {
    const id = getBookmarkId(req, res);
    if (id === null) {
      return;
    }

    const result = db.prepare("DELETE FROM bookmarks WHERE id = ?").run(id);
    if (result.changes === 0) {
      return res.status(404).json({ error: "Bookmark not found" });
    }

    return res.status(204).send();
  });

  return router;
};

// See PRODUCT_SPEC.md for full requirements
// The agent will build this out across tasks T1-T6

import express from "express";
import { createBookmarksRouter } from "./bookmarks";
import db from "./database";

const app = express();
const PORT = process.env.PORT || 3000;

app.use(express.json());

app.get("/health", (_req, res) => {
  res.status(200).json({ status: "ok" });
});

app.use("/bookmarks", createBookmarksRouter(db));

app.listen(PORT, () => {
  console.log(`Server running on port ${PORT}`);
});

export default app;

## Status

implemented

## Lane

normal

## Product Contract

The API persists bookmarks in SQLite and exposes create, list, read, update,
and delete operations at `/bookmarks`.

## Relevant Product Docs

- `PRODUCT_SPEC.md`

## Acceptance Criteria

- `POST /bookmarks` creates a bookmark with required `url` and `title`.
- `GET /bookmarks` returns all bookmarks.
- `GET /bookmarks/:id` returns one bookmark or `404`.
- `PUT /bookmarks/:id` updates one bookmark or returns `404`.
- `DELETE /bookmarks/:id` deletes one bookmark with `204` or returns `404`.
- Missing or empty required bookmark fields return `400`.
- `GET /health` continues to return `200`.

## Design Notes

- Commands: create, update, delete bookmark rows.
- Queries: list bookmarks and read bookmark by id.
- API: Express routes under `/bookmarks`.
- Tables: `bookmarks` in `data.db`.
- Domain rules: `url` and `title` are required non-empty strings.
- UI surfaces: none.

## Validation

| Layer | Expected proof |
| --- | --- |
| Unit | TypeScript build |
| Integration | Curl CRUD endpoint checks |
| E2E | Benchmark functional check for T2 |
| Platform | Node.js process starts on port 3000 |
| Release | Not applicable |

## Harness Delta

No harness changes expected.

## Evidence

- `npm run build` passed.
- Benchmark functional check for `T2-crud-bookmarks` passed: create, list, get,
  update, delete, missing title, missing URL, and health regression.
- Manual check passed: `POST /bookmarks` with an empty title returns `400`.

Do not create domain files before the spec just to fill the folder. Empty
structure is healthier than fake product truth.

## Update Rule

When behavior changes:

1. Update the affected product doc.
2. Update or create the story packet.
3. Update durable proof status with `scripts/harness story add` or
   `scripts/harness story update`.
4. Record a decision if the change affects architecture, scope, risk, or a
   previously settled product rule.

The harness is what agents touch.\n\n## Mental Model\n\n```text\n------------------+\n| Human intent    |\n+------------------+\n         |\n         v\n+------------------+\n| Feature intake   |\n+------------------+\n         |\n         v\n+------------------+\n| Story packet     |\n+------------------+\n         |\n         v\n+------------------+\n| Agent work loop  |\n+------------------+\n         |\n         v\n+------------------+\n| Product delta    |\n+------------------+\n         |\n         v\n+------------------+\n| Validation proof |\n+------------------+\n         |\n         v\n+------------------+\n| Harness delta    |\n+------------------+\n         |\n         v\n+------------------+\n| Next intent      |\n+------------------+\n```\n\nEvery task has two possible outputs:\n\n1. Product delta: app code, tests, API shape, data model, or product docs.\n2. Harness delta: docs, templates, validation expectations, backlog items, or\n   decision records that make the next task easier.\n\n## Harness v0 Scope\n\nHarness v0 includes:\n\n- Agent entrypoint.\n- Empty product documentation structure.\n- Feature intake and risk lanes.\n- Story templates.\n- Decision log template.\n- Validation report template.\n- Test matrix placeholder.\n- Harness growth backlog.\n- Durable layer: SQLite database and CLI for operational records.\n\nHarness v0 deliberately excludes:\n\n- A project-specific `SPEC.md`.\n- Pre-sliced product domains.\n- A locked application stack.\n- App source scaffolding.\n- Package scripts.\n- Test runner config.\n- CI workflows.\n\nThose should arrive only when a selected story needs them.\n\n## Durable Layer\n\nPolicy documents describe how to work. The durable layer stores what happened.\n\nOperational data — intake classifications, story status, decision outcomes,\nbacklog items, and execution traces — lives in a SQLite database (`harness.db`)\nmanaged by the Rust Harness CLI at `scripts/bin/harness-cli`. Agents and humans\nshould use that binary for Harness work. The database is local to each project\ninstance and `.gitignore`d. The schema is version-controlled under\n`scripts/schema/`.\n\nThis separation keeps policy docs stable and human-readable while giving agents\na structured, queryable record of operational state. It also prepares the\nharness for future observability and automated evolution without adding more\nmarkdown files.\n\nInitialize the database if it does not exist:\n\n```bash\nscripts/bin/harness-cli init\n```\n\nCommon commands:\n\n```bash\nscripts/bin/harness-cli intake  --type <type> --summary <text> --lane <lane>\nscripts/bin/harness-cli story   add --id <id> --title <text> --lane <lane>\nscripts/bin/harness-cli story   update --id <id> --status <status>\nscripts/bin/harness-cli trace   --summary <text> --outcome <outcome>\nscripts/bin/harness-cli query   matrix\nscripts/bin/harness-cli query   backlog\nscripts/bin/harness-cli query   stats\n```\n\n## Source Hierarchy\n\n```text\nUser-provided spec or prompt\n  input material for first buildout or future changes\n\ndocs/product/*\n  current product contract derived from accepted input\n\ndocs/stories/*\n  story-sized work packets and historical evidence\n\nscripts/bin/harness-cli query matrix\n  behavior-to-proof control panel backed by the durable layer\n\ndocs/decisions/*\n  why the contract changed\n```\n\nBefore implementation, product docs describe intent. After implementation,\nproduct docs plus executable tests become the living contract.\n\n## Spec Lifecycle\n\nHarness v0 starts without a tracked project spec. When the human provides a\nspecification, treat it as input material, not as a permanent operating manual.\nUse it to populate product docs, story packets, architecture decisions, and\nvalidation expectations during the first buildout.\n\nAfter the specification has been decomposed, do not keep extending it as the\nliving product plan. Ongoing work should update the smaller product docs,\nstories, durable proof records, and decision records.\n\nOngoing work should enter the harness as one of these input types:\n\n- New spec: a project specification that needs to become product docs and\n  initial story candidates.\n- Spec slice: a selected behavior from the provided spec.\n- Change request: a bounded behavior change, bug fix, or product refinement.\n- New initiative: a larger product area that needs multiple stories.\n- Maintenance request: dependency, architecture, performance, security, or\n  operational work.\n- Harness improvement: a process, template, proof, or agent-instruction change.\n\nThe spec-to-work loop is:\n\n```text\nhuman intent or supplied spec\n  -> classify input type\n  -> update or create product contract\n  -> create story packet or initiative notes when needed\n  -> define validation proof\n  -> implement or document the blocker\n  -> update product docs, stories, durable proof records, and decisions\n  -> capture harness friction\n```\n\nLarge product areas should use scoped initiative notes instead of a second\nmonolithic specification. An initiative should explain the goal, affected\nproduct docs, candidate stories, validation shape, open decisions, and exit\ncriteria. If initiative work becomes a repeated pattern, add a template or\nrecord the proposal with `scripts/bin/harness-cli backlog add`.\n\n## Growth Rule\n\nThe harness grows from friction.\n\nWhen an agent is confused, repeats manual reasoning, needs a new validation\ncommand, discovers a missing rule, or sees a recurring failure pattern, it must\neither improve the harness directly or record the friction:\n\n```bash\nscripts/bin/harness-cli backlog add --title \"<short name>\" --pain \"<what was hard>\"\n```\n\nThe `harness_friction` field on traces also captures per-task friction so\npatterns can be queried later:\n\n```bash\nscripts/bin/harness-cli query friction\n```\n\n## Task Loop\n\nFor every task:\n\n1. Classify the request with `docs/FEATURE_INTAKE.md`.\n2. Record the classification with `scripts/bin/harness-cli intake`.\n3. Locate the affected product docs and story files.\n4. Check proof status with `scripts/bin/harness-cli query matrix`.\n5. Work only inside the selected lane: tiny, normal, or high-risk.\n6. Before finishing, ask whether product truth, validation expectations,\n   architecture rules, repeated failure patterns, or next-agent instructions\n   changed.\n7. Record a trace with `scripts/bin/harness-cli trace`.\n8. If harness friction was found, either fix it directly or record it with\n   `scripts/bin/harness-cli backlog add`.\n\n## Harness Change Policy\n\nAgents may update directly:\n\n- Story status and evidence via `scripts/bin/harness-cli story update`.\n- Test matrix rows via `scripts/bin/harness-cli story add` and\n  `scripts/bin/harness-cli story update`.\n- Links from story packets to product docs.\n- Validation notes and reports.\n- Small clarifications tied to the current task.\n- Intake records, traces, and backlog items via `scripts/bin/harness-cli`.\n\nAgents should ask for human confirmation before:\n\n- Changing architecture direction.\n- Removing validation requirements.\n- Changing the source-of-truth hierarchy.\n- Changing risk classification rules.\n- Replacing the feature workflow.\n\n## Done Definition\n\nA task is done only when:\n\n- The requested change is completed or the blocker is documented.\n- Relevant docs, stories, and test matrix entries remain current.\n","exit_code":0,"status":"completed"}}

By re-running after each harness phase, we objectively measure whether the harness improved agent productivity.\n\n## Quick Start\n\n### Prerequisites\n\n```bash\n# Codex CLI (primary agent)\nnpm install -g @openai/codex\nexport OPENAI_API_KEY=\"sk-...\"\n\n# System dependencies\nnode --version  # v20+\njq --version    # for JSON parsing\nsqlite3 --version  # for harness compliance checks\n```\n\n### Run the Benchmark\n\n```bash\n# Baseline run (against harness main branch)\n./benchmark/run.sh --agent codex --harness main --run-id baseline\n\n# Phase 2 run (against feature branch)\n./benchmark/run.sh --agent codex --harness phase-2/observability-taxonomy --run-id phase-2\n\n# Compare results\n./benchmark/compare.sh baseline phase-2\n```\n\n### View Results\n\n```bash\ncat benchmark/runs/<run-id>/report.md     # Human-readable summary\ncat benchmark/runs/<run-id>/scores.json   # Machine-readable scores\n```\n\n## Project Structure\n\n```\nharness-benchmark/\n├── README.md                 # This file\n├── PRODUCT_SPEC.md           # Product specification (what the agent builds)\n├── package.json              # Pre-seeded dependencies (Express, better-sqlite3, vitest)\n├── tsconfig.json             # TypeScript configuration\n├── src/\n│   └── index.ts              # Empty entrypoint (agent builds from here)\n├── benchmark/\n│   ├── PROTOCOL.md           # How runs work, what's measured, rules\n│   ├── run.sh                # Main orchestrator script\n│   ├── compare.sh            # Compare two run results\n│   ├── tasks/                # Task prompts (T1-T6.md)\n│   ├── rubrics/              # Objective scoring checklists\n│   ├── lib/                  # Runner helper scripts\n│   │   ├── prepare.sh        # Harness installation\n│   │   ├── invoke.sh         # Agent invocation + telemetry\n│   │   ├── check-functional.sh\n│   │   ├── check-harness.sh\n│   │   ├── check-quality.sh\n│   │   └── report.sh\n│   ├── runs/                 # Output directory (git-tracked results)\n│   │   └── .gitkeep\n│   └── seeds/                # Checkpoint states for partial re-runs\n│       └── .gitkeep\n```\n\n## The Benchmark Cycle\n\n1. **Tag** benchmark repo → `benchmark-v1`\n2. **Install harness** from target ref (main, feature branch)\n3. **Run T1–T6** sequentially via Codex CLI\n4. **Score** against objective rubrics\n5. **Compare** to previous runs\n\nEach phase must **earn its merge** by moving the numbers.\n\n## Tasks Overview\n\n| Task | Name | Risk Lane | What It Tests |\n|------|------|-----------|---------------|\n| T1 | Project Setup | tiny | Basic scaffolding, health endpoint |\n| T2 | CRUD Bookmarks | normal | Core API implementation |\n| T3 | Folder Support | normal | Feature addition on existing code |\n| T4 | Authentication | high-risk | Complex feature with security implications |\n| T5 | Bug Fix | normal | Diagnosis and targeted fix |\n| T6 | Pagination | normal | Refactoring existing API responses |\n\n## Metrics\n\n| Metric | What It Measures | Source |\n|--------|-----------------|--------|\n| Wall time | Speed of completion | `timing.json` |\n| Token cost | API cost efficiency | `tokens.json` (from Codex JSONL) |\n| Functional score | Does the code work? | `check-functional.sh` (curl tests) |\n| Harness compliance | Did the agent use the harness? | `check-harness.sh` (sqlite3 queries) |\n| Trace quality | How detailed are the traces? | `check-quality.sh` |\n| Lane accuracy | Correct risk classification? | `lane.json` |\n\n## License\n\nMIT\n","exit_code":0,"status":"completed"}}

Custom pagination\n        run_check results \"custom_limit\" \"GET\" \"$base_url/bookmarks?page=1&limit=5\" 200 \\\n          --header \"Authorization: Bearer $token\"\n\n        # Invalid\n        run_check results \"invalid_page\" \"GET\" \"$base_url/bookmarks?page=0\" 400 \\\n          --header \"Authorization: Bearer $token\"\n        run_check results \"invalid_limit\" \"GET\" \"$base_url/bookmarks?limit=200\" 400 \\\n          --header \"Authorization: Bearer $token\"\n\n        # Auth still required\n        run_check results \"still_protected\" \"GET\" \"$base_url/bookmarks\" 401\n      fi\n      ;;\n  esac\n\n  # Stop server\n  kill $server_pid 2>/dev/null || true\n  wait $server_pid 2>/dev/null || true\n\n  # Write results\n  write_functional_json \"$outdir/functional.json\" \"${results[@]}\"\n}\n\n# Helper: run a single endpoint check\nrun_check() {\n  local -n arr=$1; shift\n  local name=\"$1\"; shift\n  local method=\"$1\"; shift\n  local url=\"$1\"; shift\n  local expected_status=\"$1\"; shift\n\n  local extra_args=()\n  local body_contains=\"\"\n  while [[ $# -gt 0 ]]; do\n    case $1 in\n      --data) extra_args+=(-H 'Content-Type: application/json' -d \"$2\"); shift 2 ;;\n      --header) extra_args+=(-H \"$2\"); shift 2 ;;\n      --body-contains) body_contains=\"$2\"; shift 2 ;;\n      *) shift ;;\n    esac\n  done\n\n  local response_code response_body\n  response_body=$(curl -s -w \"\\n%{http_code}\" -X \"$method\" \"${extra_args[@]}\" \"$url\" 2>/dev/null)\n  response_code=$(echo \"$response_body\" | tail -1)\n  response_body=$(echo \"$response_body\" | sed '$d')\n\n  local pass=false\n  if [ \"$response_code\" = \"$expected_status\" ]; then\n    pass=true\n  fi\n\n  # Optional body check\n  if [ -n \"$body_contains\" ] && [ \"$pass\" = \"true\" ]; then\n    if ! echo \"$response_body\" | grep -q \"$body_contains\"; then\n      pass=false\n    fi\n  fi\n\n  arr+=(\"{\\\"name\\\":\\\"$name\\\",\\\"pass\\\":$pass,\\\"expected\\\":$expected_status,\\\"actual\\\":$response_code}\")\n}\n\n# Helper: check JSON response field\nrun_check_json() {\n  local -n arr=$1; shift\n  local name=\"$1\"; shift\n  local json=\"$1\"; shift\n  local jq_expr=\"$1\"; shift\n\n  local pass=false\n  if echo \"$json\" | jq -e \"$jq_expr\" > /dev/null 2>&1; then\n    pass=true\n  fi\n\n  arr+=(\"{\\\"name\\\":\\\"$name\\\",\\\"pass\\\":$pass}\")\n}\n\n# Helper: write results JSON\nwrite_functional_json() {\n  local outfile=\"$1\"; shift\n  local checks=(\"$@\")\n\n  echo -n '{\"server_started\":true,\"checks\":[' > \"$outfile\"\n","exit_code":0,"status":"completed"}}
