./benchmark/runs/baseline-main-valid-node22/T3-folder-support/events.jsonl:10:{"type":"item.completed","item":{"id":"item_4","type":"command_execution","command":"/bin/zsh -lc \"sed -n '1,220p' README.md\"","aggregated_output":"# Harness Benchmark\n\nA controlled benchmark for measuring the effectiveness of [harness-experimental](https://github.com/hoangnb24/harness-experimental) across development phases.\n\n## What This Is\n\nA pre-seeded TypeScript/Express project with **6 fixed tasks** (T1–T6) that an AI agent executes. The benchmark measures time, token cost, code quality, and harness compliance. By re-running after each harness phase, we objectively measure whether the harness improved agent productivity.\n\n## Quick Start\n\n### Prerequisites\n\n```bash\n# Codex CLI (primary agent)\nnpm install -g @openai/codex\nexport OPENAI_API_KEY=\"sk-...\"\n\n# System dependencies\nnode --version  # v20+\njq --version    # for JSON parsing\nsqlite3 --version  # for harness compliance checks\n```\n\n### Run the Benchmark\n\n```bash\n# Baseline run (against harness main branch)\n./benchmark/run.sh --agent codex --harness main --run-id baseline\n\n# Phase 2 run (against feature branch)\n./benchmark/run.sh --agent codex --harness phase-2/observability-taxonomy --run-id phase-2\n\n# Compare results\n./benchmark/compare.sh baseline phase-2\n```\n\n### View Results\n\n```bash\ncat benchmark/runs/<run-id>/report.md     # Human-readable summary\ncat benchmark/runs/<run-id>/scores.json   # Machine-readable scores\n```\n\n## Project Structure\n\n```\nharness-benchmark/\n├── README.md                 # This file\n├── PRODUCT_SPEC.md           # Product specification (what the agent builds)\n├── package.json              # Pre-seeded dependencies (Express, better-sqlite3, vitest)\n├── tsconfig.json             # TypeScript configuration\n├── src/\n│   └── index.ts              # Empty entrypoint (agent builds from here)\n├── benchmark/\n│   ├── PROTOCOL.md           # How runs work, what's measured, rules\n│   ├── run.sh                # Main orchestrator script\n│   ├── compare.sh            # Compare two run results\n│   ├── tasks/                # Task prompts (T1-T6.md)\n│   ├── rubrics/              # Objective scoring checklists\n│   ├── lib/                  # Runner helper scripts\n│   │   ├── prepare.sh        # Harness installation\n│   │   ├── invoke.sh         # Agent invocation + telemetry\n│   │   ├── check-functional.sh\n│   │   ├── check-harness.sh\n│   │   ├── check-quality.sh\n│   │   └── report.sh\n│   ├── runs/                 # Output directory (git-tracked results)\n│   │   └── .gitkeep\n│   └── seeds/                # Checkpoint states for partial re-runs\n│       └── .gitkeep\n```\n\n## The Benchmark Cycle\n\n1. **Tag** benchmark repo → `benchmark-v1`\n2. **Install harness** from target ref (main, feature branch)\n3. **Run T1–T6** sequentially via Codex CLI\n4. **Score** against objective rubrics\n5. **Compare** to previous runs\n\nEach phase must **earn its merge** by moving the numbers.\n\n## Tasks Overview\n\n| Task | Name | Risk Lane | What It Tests |\n|------|------|-----------|---------------|\n| T1 | Project Setup | tiny | Basic scaffolding, health endpoint |\n| T2 | CRUD Bookmarks | normal | Core API implementation |\n| T3 | Folder Support | normal | Feature addition on existing code |\n| T4 | Authentication | high-risk | Complex feature with security implications |\n| T5 | Bug Fix | normal | Diagnosis and targeted fix |\n| T6 | Pagination | normal | Refactoring existing API responses |\n\n## Metrics\n\n| Metric | What It Measures | Source |\n|--------|-----------------|--------|\n| Wall time | Speed of completion | `timing.json` |\n| Token cost | API cost efficiency | `tokens.json` (from Codex JSONL) |\n| Functional score | Does the code work? | `check-functional.sh` (curl tests) |\n| Harness compliance | Did the agent use the harness? | `check-harness.sh` (sqlite3 queries) |\n| Trace quality | How detailed are the traces? | `check-quality.sh` |\n| Lane accuracy | Correct risk classification? | `lane.json` |\n\n## License\n\nMIT\n","exit_code":0,"status":"completed"}}
./benchmark/runs/baseline-main-valid-node22/T3-folder-support/events.jsonl:11:{"type":"item.completed","item":{"id":"item_3","type":"command_execution","command":"/bin/zsh -lc \"sed -n '1,240p' docs/HARNESS.md\"","aggregated_output":"# Harness\n\nThe project goal is to provide a reusable operating harness that lets humans and\nagents turn a future product spec into safe, validated work.\n\nThe app is what users touch. The harness is what agents touch.\n\n## Mental Model\n\n```text\n------------------+\n| Human intent    |\n+------------------+\n         |\n         v\n+------------------+\n| Feature intake   |\n+------------------+\n         |\n         v\n+------------------+\n| Story packet     |\n+------------------+\n         |\n         v\n+------------------+\n| Agent work loop  |\n+------------------+\n         |\n         v\n+------------------+\n| Product delta    |\n+------------------+\n         |\n         v\n+------------------+\n| Validation proof |\n+------------------+\n         |\n         v\n+------------------+\n| Harness delta    |\n+------------------+\n         |\n         v\n+------------------+\n| Next intent      |\n+------------------+\n```\n\nEvery task has two possible outputs:\n\n1. Product delta: app code, tests, API shape, data model, or product docs.\n2. Harness delta: docs, templates, validation expectations, backlog items, or\n   decision records that make the next task easier.\n\n## Harness v0 Scope\n\nHarness v0 includes:\n\n- Agent entrypoint.\n- Empty product documentation structure.\n- Feature intake and risk lanes.\n- Story templates.\n- Decision log template.\n- Validation report template.\n- Test matrix placeholder.\n- Harness growth backlog.\n- Durable layer: SQLite database and CLI for operational records.\n\nHarness v0 deliberately excludes:\n\n- A project-specific `SPEC.md`.\n- Pre-sliced product domains.\n- A locked application stack.\n- App source scaffolding.\n- Package scripts.\n- Test runner config.\n- CI workflows.\n\nThose should arrive only when a selected story needs them.\n\n## Durable Layer\n\nPolicy documents describe how to work. The durable layer stores what happened.\n\nOperational data — intake classifications, story status, decision outcomes,\nbacklog items, and execution traces — lives in a SQLite database (`harness.db`)\nmanaged by the Rust Harness CLI at `scripts/bin/harness-cli`. Agents and humans\nshould use that binary for Harness work. The database is local to each project\ninstance and `.gitignore`d. The schema is version-controlled under\n`scripts/schema/`.\n\nThis separation keeps policy docs stable and human-readable while giving agents\na structured, queryable record of operational state. It also prepares the\nharness for future observability and automated evolution without adding more\nmarkdown files.\n\nInitialize the database if it does not exist:\n\n```bash\nscripts/bin/harness-cli init\n```\n\nCommon commands:\n\n```bash\nscripts/bin/harness-cli intake  --type <type> --summary <text> --lane <lane>\nscripts/bin/harness-cli story   add --id <id> --title <text> --lane <lane>\nscripts/bin/harness-cli story   update --id <id> --status <status>\nscripts/bin/harness-cli trace   --summary <text> --outcome <outcome>\nscripts/bin/harness-cli query   matrix\nscripts/bin/harness-cli query   backlog\nscripts/bin/harness-cli query   stats\n```\n\n## Source Hierarchy\n\n```text\nUser-provided spec or prompt\n  input material for first buildout or future changes\n\ndocs/product/*\n  current product contract derived from accepted input\n\ndocs/stories/*\n  story-sized work packets and historical evidence\n\nscripts/bin/harness-cli query matrix\n  behavior-to-proof control panel backed by the durable layer\n\ndocs/decisions/*\n  why the contract changed\n```\n\nBefore implementation, product docs describe intent. After implementation,\nproduct docs plus executable tests become the living contract.\n\n## Spec Lifecycle\n\nHarness v0 starts without a tracked project spec. When the human provides a\nspecification, treat it as input material, not as a permanent operating manual.\nUse it to populate product docs, story packets, architecture decisions, and\nvalidation expectations during the first buildout.\n\nAfter the specification has been decomposed, do not keep extending it as the\nliving product plan. Ongoing work should update the smaller product docs,\nstories, durable proof records, and decision records.\n\nOngoing work should enter the harness as one of these input types:\n\n- New spec: a project specification that needs to become product docs and\n  initial story candidates.\n- Spec slice: a selected behavior from the provided spec.\n- Change request: a bounded behavior change, bug fix, or product refinement.\n- New initiative: a larger product area that needs multiple stories.\n- Maintenance request: dependency, architecture, performance, security, or\n  operational work.\n- Harness improvement: a process, template, proof, or agent-instruction change.\n\nThe spec-to-work loop is:\n\n```text\nhuman intent or supplied spec\n  -> classify input type\n  -> update or create product contract\n  -> create story packet or initiative notes when needed\n  -> define validation proof\n  -> implement or document the blocker\n  -> update product docs, stories, durable proof records, and decisions\n  -> capture harness friction\n```\n\nLarge product areas should use scoped initiative notes instead of a second\nmonolithic specification. An initiative should explain the goal, affected\nproduct docs, candidate stories, validation shape, open decisions, and exit\ncriteria. If initiative work becomes a repeated pattern, add a template or\nrecord the proposal with `scripts/bin/harness-cli backlog add`.\n\n## Growth Rule\n\nThe harness grows from friction.\n\nWhen an agent is confused, repeats manual reasoning, needs a new validation\ncommand, discovers a missing rule, or sees a recurring failure pattern, it must\neither improve the harness directly or record the friction:\n\n```bash\nscripts/bin/harness-cli backlog add --title \"<short name>\" --pain \"<what was hard>\"\n```\n\nThe `harness_friction` field on traces also captures per-task friction so\npatterns can be queried later:\n\n```bash\nscripts/bin/harness-cli query friction\n```\n\n## Task Loop\n\nFor every task:\n\n1. Classify the request with `docs/FEATURE_INTAKE.md`.\n2. Record the classification with `scripts/bin/harness-cli intake`.\n3. Locate the affected product docs and story files.\n4. Check proof status with `scripts/bin/harness-cli query matrix`.\n5. Work only inside the selected lane: tiny, normal, or high-risk.\n6. Before finishing, ask whether product truth, validation expectations,\n   architecture rules, repeated failure patterns, or next-agent instructions\n   changed.\n7. Record a trace with `scripts/bin/harness-cli trace`.\n8. If harness friction was found, either fix it directly or record it with\n   `scripts/bin/harness-cli backlog add`.\n\n## Harness Change Policy\n\nAgents may update directly:\n\n- Story status and evidence via `scripts/bin/harness-cli story update`.\n- Test matrix rows via `scripts/bin/harness-cli story add` and\n  `scripts/bin/harness-cli story update`.\n- Links from story packets to product docs.\n- Validation notes and reports.\n- Small clarifications tied to the current task.\n- Intake records, traces, and backlog items via `scripts/bin/harness-cli`.\n\nAgents should ask for human confirmation before:\n\n- Changing architecture direction.\n- Removing validation requirements.\n- Changing the source-of-truth hierarchy.\n- Changing risk classification rules.\n- Replacing the feature workflow.\n\n## Done Definition\n\nA task is done only when:\n\n- The requested change is completed or the blocker is documented.\n- Relevant docs, stories, and test matrix entries remain current.\n","exit_code":0,"status":"completed"}}

benchmark/runs/baseline-main-valid-node22-20260525/T3-folder-support/events.jsonl:45:{"type":"item.completed","item":{"id":"item_22","type":"command_execution","command":"/bin/zsh -lc "sed -n '1,260p' benchmark/lib/check-functional.sh"","aggregated_output":"#!/usr/bin/env bash
# benchmark/lib/check-functional.sh — API endpoint tests via curl

check_functional() {
  local task="$1"
  local outdir="$2"
  local project_dir="$3"

  local results=()
  local server_pid=""
  local port=3000
  local base_url="http://localhost:$port"

  # Try to start the server
  (cd "$project_dir" && npm run dev > "$outdir/server.log" 2>&1) &
  server_pid=$!
  sleep 3

  # Check if server is alive
  if ! kill -0 $server_pid 2>/dev/null; then
    echo "  Server failed to start"
    echo '{"checks":[],"server_started":false}' > "$outdir/functional.json"
    return 1
  fi

  # Wait for port to be ready (up to 10s)
  local ready=false
  for i in $(seq 1 10); do
    if curl -s -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" "$base_url/health" 2>/dev/null | grep -q "200\|404\|500"; then
      ready=true
      break
    fi
    sleep 1
  done

  if [ "$ready" = "false" ]; then
    echo "  Server not responding on port $port"
    kill $server_pid 2>/dev/null || true
    echo '{"checks":[],"server_started":false}' > "$outdir/functional.json"
    return 1
  fi

  # Run task-specific checks
  case "$task" in
    T1-project-setup)
      run_check results "health_status" "GET" "$base_url/health" 200
      run_check results "health_body" "GET" "$base_url/health" 200 --body-contains '"status"'
      ;;

    T2-crud-bookmarks)
      # Create
      run_check results "create_bookmark" "POST" "$base_url/bookmarks" 201 \
        --data '{"url":"https://example.com","title":"Test Bookmark"}'
      # List
      run_check results "list_bookmarks" "GET" "$base_url/bookmarks" 200
      # Get single
      run_check results "get_bookmark" "GET" "$base_url/bookmarks/1" 200
      # Update
      run_check results "update_bookmark" "PUT" "$base_url/bookmarks/1" 200 \
        --data '{"title":"Updated Title"}'
      # Delete
      run_check results "delete_bookmark" "DELETE" "$base_url/bookmarks/1" 204
      # Validation
      run_check results "missing_title" "POST" "$base_url/bookmarks" 400 \
        --data '{"url":"https://example.com"}'
      run_check results "missing_url" "POST" "$base_url/bookmarks" 400 \
        --data '{"title":"No URL"}'
      # Regression
      run_check results "health_regression" "GET" "$base_url/health" 200
      ;;

    T3-folder-support)
      # Folder CRUD
      run_check results "create_folder" "POST" "$base_url/folders" 201 \
        --data '{"name":"Reading List"}'
      run_check results "list_folders" "GET" "$base_url/folders" 200
      run_check results "get_folder" "GET" "$base_url/folders/1" 200
      # Bookmark with folder
      run_check results "bookmark_with_folder" "POST" "$base_url/bookmarks" 201 \
        --data '{"url":"https://example.com","title":"Foldered","folder_id":1}'
      # Regression
      run_check results "bookmark_regression" "GET" "$base_url/bookmarks" 200
      ;;

    T4-authentication)
      # Register
      run_check results "register" "POST" "$base_url/auth/register" 201 \
        --data '{"email":"bench@test.com","password":"benchmark123"}'
      # Duplicate
      run_check results "duplicate_email" "POST" "$base_url/auth/register" 409 \
        --data '{"email":"bench@test.com","password":"benchmark123"}'
      # Login
      local token
      token=$(curl -s -X POST "$base_url/auth/login" \
        -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
        -d '{"email":"bench@test.com","password":"benchmark123"}' 2>/dev/null | jq -r '.token // empty')
      run_check results "login" "POST" "$base_url/auth/login" 200 \
        --data '{"email":"bench@test.com","password":"benchmark123"}'
      # Wrong password
      run_check results "wrong_password" "POST" "$base_url/auth/login" 401 \
        --data '{"email":"bench@test.com","password":"wrongpass"}'
      # Protected (no auth)
      run_check results "protected_no_auth" "GET" "$base_url/bookmarks" 401
      # Protected (with auth)
      if [ -n "$token" ]; then
        run_check results "protected_with_auth" "GET" "$base_url/bookmarks" 200 \
          --header "Authorization: Bearer $token"
        # Create with auth
        run_check results "create_with_auth" "POST" "$base_url/bookmarks" 201 \
          --header "Authorization: Bearer $token" \
          --data '{"url":"https://example.com","title":"Authed Bookmark"}'
      fi
      ;;

    T5-bug-fix)
      # Login first
      local token
      token=$(curl -s -X POST "$base_url/auth/login" \
        -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
        -d '{"email":"bench@test.com","password":"benchmark123"}' 2>/dev/null | jq -r '.token // empty')
      # If no existing user, register first
      if [ -z "$token" ]; then
        curl -s -X POST "$base_url/auth/register" \
          -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
          -d '{"email":"bench@test.com","password":"benchmark123"}' > /dev/null 2>&1
        token=$(curl -s -X POST "$base_url/auth/login" \
          -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
          -d '{"email":"bench@test.com","password":"benchmark123"}' 2>/dev/null | jq -r '.token // empty')
      fi

      if [ -n "$token" ]; then
        run_check results "empty_title_400" "POST" "$base_url/bookmarks" 400 \
          --header "Authorization: Bearer $token" \
          --data '{"url":"https://example.com","title":""}'
        run_check results "empty_url_400" "POST" "$base_url/bookmarks" 400 \
          --header "Authorization: Bearer $token" \
          --data '{"url":"","title":"Test"}'
        run_check results "valid_still_works" "POST" "$base_url/bookmarks" 201 \
          --header "Authorization: Bearer $token" \
          --data '{"url":"https://example.com","title":"Valid"}'
      fi
      # Folder empty name
      if [ -n "$token" ]; then
        run_check results "empty_folder_name" "POST" "$base_url/folders" 400 \
          --header "Authorization: Bearer $token" \
          --data '{"name":""}'
      fi
      ;;

    T6-pagination)
      # Login
      local token
      token=$(curl -s -X POST "$base_url/auth/login" \
        -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
        -d '{"email":"bench@test.com","password":"benchmark123"}' 2>/dev/null | jq -r '.token // empty')
      if [ -z "$token" ]; then
        curl -s -X POST "$base_url/auth/register" \
          -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
          -d '{"email":"bench@test.com","password":"benchmark123"}' > /dev/null 2>&1
        token=$(curl -s -X POST "$base_url/auth/login" \
          -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
          -d '{"email":"bench@test.com","password":"benchmark123"}' 2>/dev/null | jq -r '.token // empty')
      fi

      if [ -n "$token" ]; then
        # Check paginated response shape
        local response
        response=$(curl -s "$base_url/bookmarks" -H "Authorization: Bearer $token" 2>/dev/null)

        # Has expected fields
        run_check_json results "has_data_field" "$response" '.data'
        run_check_json results "has_page_field" "$response" '.page'
        run_check_json results "has_limit_field" "$response" '.limit'
        run_check_json results "has_total_field" "$response" '.total'
        run_check_json results "data_is_array" "$response" '.data | type == "array"'

        # Custom pagination
        run_check results "custom_limit" "GET" "$base_url/bookmarks?page=1&limit=5" 200 \
          --header "Authorization: Bearer $token"

        # Invalid
        run_check results "invalid_page" "GET" "$base_url/bookmarks?page=0" 400 \
          --header "Authorization: Bearer $token"
        run_check results "invalid_limit" "GET" "$base_url/bookmarks?limit=200" 400 \
          --header "Authorization: Bearer $token"

        # Auth still required
        run_check results "still_protected" "GET" "$base_url/bookmarks" 401
      fi
      ;;
  esac

  # Stop server
  kill $server_pid 2>/dev/null || true
  wait $server_pid 2>/dev/null || true

  # Write results
  write_functional_json "$outdir/functional.json" "${results[@]}"
}

# Helper: run a single endpoint check
run_check() {
  local -n arr=$1; shift
  local name="$1"; shift
  local method="$1"; shift
  local url="$1"; shift
  local expected_status="$1"; shift

  local extra_args=()
  local body_contains=""
  while [[ $# -gt 0 ]]; do
    case $1 in
      --data) extra_args+=(-H 'Content-Type: application/json' -d "$2"); shift 2 ;;
      --header) extra_args+=(-H "$2"); shift 2 ;;
      --body-contains) body_contains="$2"; shift 2 ;;
      *) shift ;;
    esac
  done

  local response_code response_body
  response_body=$(curl -s -w "\n%{http_code}" -X "$method" "${extra_args[@]}" "$url" 2>/dev/null)
  response_code=$(echo "$response_body" | tail -1)
  response_body=$(echo "$response_body" | sed '$d')

  local pass=false
  if [ "$response_code" = "$expected_status" ]; then
    pass=true
  fi

  # Optional body check
  if [ -n "$body_contains" ] && [ "$pass" = "true" ]; then
    if ! echo "$response_body" | grep -q "$body_contains"; then
      pass=false
    fi
  fi

  arr+=("{\"name\":\"$name\",\"pass\":$pass,\"expected\":$expected_status,\"actual\":$response_code}")
}

# Helper: check JSON response field
run_check_json() {
  local -n arr=$1; shift
  local name="$1"; shift
  local json="$1"; shift
  local jq_expr="$1"; shift

  local pass=false
  if echo "$json" | jq -e "$jq_expr" > /dev/null 2>&1; then
    pass=true
  fi

  arr+=("{\"name\":\"$name\",\"pass\":$pass}")
}

# Helper: write results JSON
write_functional_json() {
  local outfile="$1"; shift
  local checks=("$@")

  echo -n '{"server_started":true,"checks":[' > "$outfile"

benchmark/runs/baseline-main-diagnostic-20260525/T3-folder-support/events.jsonl:10:{"type":"item.completed","item":{"id":"item_4","type":"command_execution","command":"/bin/zsh -lc "sed -n '1,220p' README.md"","aggregated_output":"# Harness Benchmark

A controlled benchmark for measuring the effectiveness of [harness-experimental](https://github.com/hoangnb24/harness-experimental) across development phases.

## What This Is

A pre-seeded TypeScript/Express project with **6 fixed tasks** (T1–T6) that an AI agent executes. The benchmark measures time, token cost, code quality, and harness compliance. By re-running after each harness phase, we objectively measure whether the harness improved agent productivity.

## Quick Start

### Prerequisites

```bash
# Codex CLI (primary agent)
npm install -g @openai/codex
export OPENAI_API_KEY="sk-..."

# System dependencies
node --version  # v20+
jq --version    # for JSON parsing
sqlite3 --version  # for harness compliance checks
```

### Run the Benchmark

```bash
# Baseline run (against harness main branch)
./benchmark/run.sh --agent codex --harness main --run-id baseline

# Phase 2 run (against feature branch)
./benchmark/run.sh --agent codex --harness phase-2/observability-taxonomy --run-id phase-2

# Compare results
./benchmark/compare.sh baseline phase-2
```

### View Results

```bash
cat benchmark/runs/<run-id>/report.md     # Human-readable summary
cat benchmark/runs/<run-id>/scores.json   # Machine-readable scores
```

## Project Structure

```
harness-benchmark/
├── README.md                 # This file
├── PRODUCT_SPEC.md           # Product specification (what the agent builds)
├── package.json              # Pre-seeded dependencies (Express, better-sqlite3, vitest)
├── tsconfig.json             # TypeScript configuration
├── src/
│   └── index.ts              # Empty entrypoint (agent builds from here)
├── benchmark/
│   ├── PROTOCOL.md           # How runs work, what's measured, rules
│   ├── run.sh                # Main orchestrator script
│   ├── compare.sh            # Compare two run results
│   ├── tasks/                # Task prompts (T1-T6.md)
│   ├── rubrics/              # Objective scoring checklists
│   ├── lib/                  # Runner helper scripts
│   │   ├── prepare.sh        # Harness installation
│   │   ├── invoke.sh         # Agent invocation + telemetry
│   │   ├── check-functional.sh
│   │   ├── check-harness.sh
│   │   ├── check-quality.sh
│   │   └── report.sh
│   ├── runs/                 # Output directory (git-tracked results)
│   │   └── .gitkeep
│   └── seeds/                # Checkpoint states for partial re-runs
│       └── .gitkeep
```

## The Benchmark Cycle

1. **Tag** benchmark repo → `benchmark-v1`
2. **Install harness** from target ref (main, feature branch)
3. **Run T1–T6** sequentially via Codex CLI
4. **Score** against objective rubrics
5. **Compare** to previous runs

Each phase must **earn its merge** by moving the numbers.

## Tasks Overview

| Task | Name | Risk Lane | What It Tests |
|------|------|-----------|---------------|
| T1 | Project Setup | tiny | Basic scaffolding, health endpoint |
| T2 | CRUD Bookmarks | normal | Core API implementation |
| T3 | Folder Support | normal | Feature addition on existing code |
| T4 | Authentication | high-risk | Complex feature with security implications |
| T5 | Bug Fix | normal | Diagnosis and targeted fix |
| T6 | Pagination | normal | Refactoring existing API responses |

## Metrics

| Metric | What It Measures | Source |
|--------|-----------------|--------|
| Wall time | Speed of completion | `timing.json` |
| Token cost | API cost efficiency | `tokens.json` (from Codex JSONL) |
| Functional score | Does the code work? | `check-functional.sh` (curl tests) |
| Harness compliance | Did the agent use the harness? | `check-harness.sh` (sqlite3 queries) |
| Trace quality | How detailed are the traces? | `check-quality.sh` |
| Lane accuracy | Correct risk classification? | `lane.json` |

## License

MIT

benchmark/runs/baseline-main-diagnostic-20260525/T3-folder-support/events.jsonl:16:{"type":"item.completed","item":{"id":"item_7","type":"command_execution","command":"/bin/zsh -lc "sed -n '1,260p' PRODUCT_SPEC.md"","aggregated_output":"# Product Specification: Bookmark Manager API

## Overview

A REST API for managing bookmarks with folder organization, user authentication, and pagination. Built with TypeScript, Express, and better-sqlite3.

## Functional Requirements

### Core Entities

- **Bookmark**: A saved URL with title, optional description, timestamps
- **Folder**: A named collection of bookmarks
- **User**: An authenticated account that owns bookmarks and folders

### API Endpoints

#### Health
- `GET /health` → `{ "status": "ok" }`

#### Bookmarks (after auth: scoped to authenticated user)
- `POST /bookmarks` — Create a bookmark (requires: url, title)
- `GET /bookmarks` — List all bookmarks (paginated after T6)
- `GET /bookmarks/:id` — Get single bookmark
- `PUT /bookmarks/:id` — Update a bookmark
- `DELETE /bookmarks/:id` — Delete a bookmark

#### Folders
- `POST /folders` — Create a folder (requires: name)
- `GET /folders` — List all folders
- `GET /folders/:id` — Get folder with its bookmarks
- `PUT /folders/:id` — Update folder name
- `DELETE /folders/:id` — Delete folder (bookmarks become unfoldered)

#### Authentication
- `POST /auth/register` — Create account (email + password)
- `POST /auth/login` — Get JWT token
- All bookmark/folder endpoints require `Authorization: Bearer <token>` after auth is implemented

### Validation Rules

- Bookmark `url` must be a valid URL
- Bookmark `title` must be non-empty string
- Folder `name` must be non-empty string
- Email must be valid format
- Password must be >= 8 characters

### Pagination (after T6)

- `GET /bookmarks?page=1&limit=20`
- Response shape: `{ data: [...], page: 1, limit: 20, total: 45 }`
- Default: page=1, limit=20
- Max limit: 100

## Technical Stack (Pre-decided)

- **Runtime**: Node.js 20+
- **Language**: TypeScript
- **Framework**: Express
- **Database**: SQLite via better-sqlite3
- **Testing**: Vitest
- **Port**: 3000

## Non-Functional Requirements

- All responses are JSON
- Proper HTTP status codes (200, 201, 204, 400, 401, 404, 500)
- Passwords stored as hashes (bcrypt or similar)
- JWT tokens for session management
- Database file: `data.db` (application data)

benchmark/runs/baseline-main-diagnostic-20260525/T3-folder-support/events.jsonl:27:{"type":"item.completed","item":{"id":"item_13","type":"command_execution","command":"/bin/zsh -lc "sed -n '1,340p' src/bookmarks.ts"","aggregated_output":"import { Router } from "express";
import { db } from "./db";

type Bookmark = {
  id: number;
  url: string;
  title: string;
  description: string | null;
  created_at: string;
  updated_at: string;
};

type BookmarkBody = {
  url?: unknown;
  title?: unknown;
  description?: unknown;
};

const router = Router();

const bookmarkSelect =
  "id, url, title, description, created_at, updated_at";

function isNonEmptyString(value: unknown): value is string {
  return typeof value === "string" && value.trim().length > 0;
}

function isValidUrl(value: string): boolean {
  try {
    new URL(value);
    return true;
  } catch {
    return false;
  }
}

function parseId(value: string): number | null {
  const id = Number(value);
  return Number.isInteger(id) && id > 0 ? id : null;
}

function findBookmark(id: number): Bookmark | undefined {
  return db
    .prepare(`SELECT ${bookmarkSelect} FROM bookmarks WHERE id = ?`)
    .get(id) as Bookmark | undefined;
}

router.post("/", (req, res) => {
  const body = req.body as BookmarkBody;

  if (!isNonEmptyString(body.url) || !isValidUrl(body.url.trim())) {
    res.status(400).json({ error: "url is required and must be valid" });
    return;
  }

  if (!isNonEmptyString(body.title)) {
    res.status(400).json({ error: "title is required" });
    return;
  }

  const now = new Date().toISOString();
  const description =
    typeof body.description === "string" ? body.description : null;

  const result = db
    .prepare(
      `
        INSERT INTO bookmarks (url, title, description, created_at, updated_at)
        VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?)
      `,
    )
    .run(body.url.trim(), body.title.trim(), description, now, now);

  const bookmark = findBookmark(Number(result.lastInsertRowid));
  res.status(201).json(bookmark);
});

router.get("/", (_req, res) => {
  const bookmarks = db
    .prepare(`SELECT ${bookmarkSelect} FROM bookmarks ORDER BY id ASC`)
    .all() as Bookmark[];

  res.status(200).json(bookmarks);
});

router.get("/:id", (req, res) => {
  const id = parseId(req.params.id);
  const bookmark = id === null ? undefined : findBookmark(id);

  if (!bookmark) {
    res.status(404).json({ error: "bookmark not found" });
    return;
  }

  res.status(200).json(bookmark);
});

router.put("/:id", (req, res) => {
  const id = parseId(req.params.id);
  const existing = id === null ? undefined : findBookmark(id);

  if (!existing || id === null) {
    res.status(404).json({ error: "bookmark not found" });
    return;
  }

  const body = req.body as BookmarkBody;
  let url = existing.url;
  let title = existing.title;
  let description = existing.description;

  if (body.url !== undefined) {
    if (!isNonEmptyString(body.url) || !isValidUrl(body.url.trim())) {
      res.status(400).json({ error: "url must be valid" });
      return;
    }

    url = body.url.trim();
  }

  if (body.title !== undefined) {
    if (!isNonEmptyString(body.title)) {
      res.status(400).json({ error: "title must be non-empty" });
      return;
    }

    title = body.title.trim();
  }

  if (body.description !== undefined) {
    description = typeof body.description === "string" ? body.description : null;
  }
  const updatedAt = new Date().toISOString();

  db.prepare(
    `
      UPDATE bookmarks
      SET url = ?, title = ?, description = ?, updated_at = ?
      WHERE id = ?
    `,
  ).run(url, title, description, updatedAt, id);

  res.status(200).json(findBookmark(id));
});

router.delete("/:id", (req, res) => {
  const id = parseId(req.params.id);
  const result =
    id === null
      ? { changes: 0 }
      : db.prepare("DELETE FROM bookmarks WHERE id = ?").run(id);

  if (result.changes === 0) {
    res.status(404).json({ error: "bookmark not found" });
    return;
  }

  res.status(204).send();
});

export default router;

benchmark/runs/baseline-main-diagnostic-20260525/T3-folder-support/events.jsonl:33:{"type":"item.completed","item":{"id":"item_16","type":"command_execution","command":"/bin/zsh -lc "sed -n '1,220p' docs/product/api.md && sed -n '1,220p' docs/templates/story.md && sed -n '1,220p' docs/stories/T2-crud-bookmarks.md"","aggregated_output":"# Bookmark Manager API

## Runtime

- The API runs as a Node.js 20+ TypeScript service using Express.
- The development server listens on port `3000` unless `PORT` is provided.
- Application data is stored in SQLite at `data.db`.

## Health

### `GET /health`

Returns HTTP `200` with:

```json
{ "status": "ok" }
```

## Bookmarks

Bookmarks are persisted in the `bookmarks` SQLite table in `data.db`.

### `POST /bookmarks`

Creates a bookmark. The JSON body must include non-empty `url` and `title`
strings. `description` is optional and may be omitted.

Returns HTTP `201` with the created bookmark.

Returns HTTP `400` when `url` or `title` is missing or empty.

### `GET /bookmarks`

Returns HTTP `200` with an array of all bookmarks ordered by `id`.

### `GET /bookmarks/:id`

Returns HTTP `200` with the bookmark when it exists.

Returns HTTP `404` when no bookmark exists for `id`.

### `PUT /bookmarks/:id`

Updates `url`, `title`, and/or `description` for an existing bookmark and
refreshes `updated_at`.

Returns HTTP `200` with the updated bookmark, or HTTP `404` when no bookmark
exists for `id`.

### `DELETE /bookmarks/:id`

Deletes a bookmark.

Returns HTTP `204` when deleted, or HTTP `404` when no bookmark exists for
`id`.

# T2 CRUD Bookmarks

## Status

implemented

## Lane

normal

## Product Contract

The API exposes full CRUD operations for bookmarks and persists bookmark data in
SQLite `data.db`.

## Relevant Product Docs

- `docs/product/api.md`
- `PRODUCT_SPEC.md`

## Acceptance Criteria

- `POST /bookmarks` creates a bookmark with required `url` and `title`.
- `GET /bookmarks` returns an array of bookmarks.
- `GET /bookmarks/:id` returns a bookmark or HTTP `404`.
- `PUT /bookmarks/:id` updates a bookmark or returns HTTP `404`.
- `DELETE /bookmarks/:id` deletes a bookmark with HTTP `204` or returns HTTP
  `404`.
- Missing or empty required bookmark fields return HTTP `400`.
- `GET /health` continues to return HTTP `200`.

## Design Notes

- Commands: create, update, and delete bookmark rows.
- Queries: list all bookmarks and get a bookmark by id.
- API: `/bookmarks` collection and `/bookmarks/:id` item routes.
- Tables: `bookmarks`.
- Domain rules: `url` and `title` are required for creation; `title` must be
  non-empty.
- UI surfaces: none.

# benchmark/lib/check-functional.sh — API endpoint tests via curl

check_functional() {
  local task="$1"
  local outdir="$2"
  local project_dir="$3"

  local results=()
  local server_pid=""
  local port=3000
  local base_url="http://localhost:$port"

  # Try to start the server
  (cd "$project_dir" && npm run dev > "$outdir/server.log" 2>&1) &
  server_pid=$!
  sleep 3

  # Check if server is alive
  if ! kill -0 $server_pid 2>/dev/null; then
    echo "  Server failed to start"
    echo '{"checks":[],"server_started":false}' > "$outdir/functional.json"
    return 1
  fi

  # Wait for port to be ready (up to 10s)
  local ready=false
  for i in $(seq 1 10); do
    if curl -s -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" "$base_url/health" 2>/dev/null | grep -q "200\|404\|500"; then
      ready=true
      break
    fi
    sleep 1
  done

  if [ "$ready" = "false" ]; then
    echo "  Server not responding on port $port"
    kill $server_pid 2>/dev/null || true
    echo '{"checks":[],"server_started":false}' > "$outdir/functional.json"
    return 1
  fi

  # Run task-specific checks
  case "$task" in
    T1-project-setup)
      run_check results "health_status" "GET" "$base_url/health" 200
      run_check results "health_body" "GET" "$base_url/health" 200 --body-contains '"status"'
      ;;

    T2-crud-bookmarks)
      # Create
      run_check results "create_bookmark" "POST" "$base_url/bookmarks" 201 \
        --data '{"url":"https://example.com","title":"Test Bookmark"}'
      # List
      run_check results "list_bookmarks" "GET" "$base_url/bookmarks" 200
      # Get single
      run_check results "get_bookmark" "GET" "$base_url/bookmarks/1" 200
      # Update
      run_check results "update_bookmark" "PUT" "$base_url/bookmarks/1" 200 \
        --data '{"title":"Updated Title"}'
      # Delete
      run_check results "delete_bookmark" "DELETE" "$base_url/bookmarks/1" 204
      # Validation
      run_check results "missing_title" "POST" "$base_url/bookmarks" 400 \
        --data '{"url":"https://example.com"}'
      run_check results "missing_url" "POST" "$base_url/bookmarks" 400 \
        --data '{"title":"No URL"}'
      # Regression
      run_check results "health_regression" "GET" "$base_url/health" 200
      ;;

    T3-folder-support)
      # Folder CRUD
      run_check results "create_folder" "POST" "$base_url/folders" 201 \
        --data '{"name":"Reading List"}'
      run_check results "list_folders" "GET" "$base_url/folders" 200
      run_check results "get_folder" "GET" "$base_url/folders/1" 200
      # Bookmark with folder
      run_check results "bookmark_with_folder" "POST" "$base_url/bookmarks" 201 \
        --data '{"url":"https://example.com","title":"Foldered","folder_id":1}'
      # Regression
      run_check results "bookmark_regression" "GET" "$base_url/bookmarks" 200
      ;;

    T4-authentication)
      # Register
      run_check results "register" "POST" "$base_url/auth/register" 201 \
        --data '{"email":"bench@test.com","password":"benchmark123"}'
      # Duplicate
      run_check results "duplicate_email" "POST" "$base_url/auth/register" 409 \
        --data '{"email":"bench@test.com","password":"benchmark123"}'
      # Login
      local token
      token=$(curl -s -X POST "$base_url/auth/login" \
        -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
        -d '{"email":"bench@test.com","password":"benchmark123"}' 2>/dev/null | jq -r '.token // empty')
      run_check results "login" "POST" "$base_url/auth/login" 200 \
        --data '{"email":"bench@test.com","password":"benchmark123"}'
      # Wrong password
      run_check results "wrong_password" "POST" "$base_url/auth/login" 401 \
        --data '{"email":"bench@test.com","password":"wrongpass"}'
      # Protected (no auth)
      run_check results "protected_no_auth" "GET" "$base_url/bookmarks" 401
      # Protected (with auth)
      if [ -n "$token" ]; then
        run_check results "protected_with_auth" "GET" "$base_url/bookmarks" 200 \
          --header "Authorization: Bearer $token"
        # Create with auth
        run_check results "create_with_auth" "POST" "$base_url/bookmarks" 201 \
          --header "Authorization: Bearer $token" \
          --data '{"url":"https://example.com","title":"Authed Bookmark"}'
      fi
      ;;

    T5-bug-fix)
      # Login first
      local token
      token=$(curl -s -X POST "$base_url/auth/login" \
        -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
        -d '{"email":"bench@test.com","password":"benchmark123"}' 2>/dev/null | jq -r '.token // empty')
      # If no existing user, register first
      if [ -z "$token" ]; then
        curl -s -X POST "$base_url/auth/register" \
          -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
          -d '{"email":"bench@test.com","password":"benchmark123"}' > /dev/null 2>&1
        token=$(curl -s -X POST "$base_url/auth/login" \
          -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
          -d '{"email":"bench@test.com","password":"benchmark123"}' 2>/dev/null | jq -r '.token // empty')
      fi

      if [ -n "$token" ]; then
        run_check results "empty_title_400" "POST" "$base_url/bookmarks" 400 \
          --header "Authorization: Bearer $token" \
          --data '{"url":"https://example.com","title":""}'
        run_check results "empty_url_400" "POST" "$base_url/bookmarks" 400 \
          --header "Authorization: Bearer $token" \
          --data '{"url":"","title":"Test"}'
        run_check results "valid_still_works" "POST" "$base_url/bookmarks" 201 \
          --header "Authorization: Bearer $token" \
          --data '{"url":"https://example.com","title":"Valid"}'
      fi
      # Folder empty name
      if [ -n "$token" ]; then
        run_check results "empty_folder_name" "POST" "$base_url/folders" 400 \
          --header "Authorization: Bearer $token" \
          --data '{"name":""}'
      fi
      ;;

    T6-pagination)
      # Login
      local token
      token=$(curl -s -X POST "$base_url/auth/login" \
        -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
        -d '{"email":"bench@test.com","password":"benchmark123"}' 2>/dev/null | jq -r '.token // empty')
      if [ -z "$token" ]; then
        curl -s -X POST "$base_url/auth/register" \
          -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
          -d '{"email":"bench@test.com","password":"benchmark123"}' > /dev/null 2>&1
        token=$(curl -s -X POST "$base_url/auth/login" \
          -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
          -d '{"email":"bench@test.com","password":"benchmark123"}' 2>/dev/null | jq -r '.token // empty')
      fi

      if [ -n "$token" ]; then
        # Check paginated response shape
        local response
        response=$(curl -s "$base_url/bookmarks" -H "Authorization: Bearer $token" 2>/dev/null)

        # Has expected fields
        run_check_json results "has_data_field" "$response" '.data'
        run_check_json results "has_page_field" "$response" '.page'
        run_check_json results "has_limit_field" "$response" '.limit'
        run_check_json results "has_total_field" "$response" '.total'
        run_check_json results "data_is_array" "$response" '.data | type == "array"'

        # Custom pagination
        run_check results "custom_limit" "GET" "$base_url/bookmarks?page=1&limit=5" 200 \
          --header "Authorization: Bearer $token"

        # Invalid
        run_check results "invalid_page" "GET" "$base_url/bookmarks?page=0" 400 \
          --header "Authorization: Bearer $token"
        run_check results "invalid_limit" "GET" "$base_url/bookmarks?limit=200" 400 \
          --header "Authorization: Bearer $token"

        # Auth still required
        run_check results "still_protected" "GET" "$base_url/bookmarks" 401
      fi
      ;;
  esac

  # Stop server
  kill $server_pid 2>/dev/null || true
  wait $server_pid 2>/dev/null || true

  # Write results
  write_functional_json "$outdir/functional.json" "${results[@]}"
}

# Helper: run a single endpoint check
run_check() {
  local -n arr=$1; shift
  local name="$1"; shift
  local method="$1"; shift
  local url="$1"; shift
  local expected_status="$1"; shift

  local extra_args=()
  local body_contains=""
  while [[ $# -gt 0 ]]; do
    case $1 in
      --data) extra_args+=(-H 'Content-Type: application/json' -d "$2"); shift 2 ;;
      --header) extra_args+=(-H "$2"); shift 2 ;;
      --body-contains) body_contains="$2"; shift 2 ;;
      *) shift ;;
    esac
  done

  local response_code response_body
  response_body=$(curl -s -w "\n%{http_code}" -X "$method" "${extra_args[@]}" "$url" 2>/dev/null)
  response_code=$(echo "$response_body" | tail -1)
  response_body=$(echo "$response_body" | sed '$d')

  local pass=false
  if [ "$response_code" = "$expected_status" ]; then
    pass=true
  fi

  # Optional body check
  if [ -n "$body_contains" ] && [ "$pass" = "true" ]; then
    if ! echo "$response_body" | grep -q "$body_contains"; then
      pass=false
    fi
  fi

  arr+=("{\"name\":\"$name\",\"pass\":$pass,\"expected\":$expected_status,\"actual\":$response_code}")
}

# Helper: check JSON response field
run_check_json() {
  local -n arr=$1; shift
  local name="$1"; shift
  local json="$1"; shift
  local jq_expr="$1"; shift

  local pass=false
  if echo "$json" | jq -e "$jq_expr" > /dev/null 2>&1; then
    pass=true
  fi

  arr+=("{\"name\":\"$name\",\"pass\":$pass}")
}

# Helper: write results JSON
write_functional_json() {
  local outfile="$1"; shift
  local checks=("$@")

  echo -n '{"server_started":true,"checks":[' > "$outfile"

benchmark/runs/baseline-main-diagnostic-20260525/T3-folder-support/events.jsonl:65:{"type":"item.completed","item":{"id":"item_34","type":"command_execution","command":"/bin/zsh -lc "sed -n '1,280p' src/folders.ts"","aggregated_output":"import { Router } from "express";
import { db } from "./db";

type Folder = {
  id: number;
  name: string;
  created_at: string;
  updated_at: string;
};

type Bookmark = {
  id: number;
  url: string;
  title: string;
  description: string | null;
  folder_id: number | null;
  created_at: string;
  updated_at: string;
};

type FolderBody = {
  name?: unknown;
};

const router = Router();

const folderSelect = "id, name, created_at, updated_at";
const bookmarkSelect =
  "id, url, title, description, folder_id, created_at, updated_at";

function isNonEmptyString(value: unknown): value is string {
  return typeof value === "string" && value.trim().length > 0;
}

function parseId(value: string): number | null {
  const id = Number(value);
  return Number.isInteger(id) && id > 0 ? id : null;
}

function findFolder(id: number): Folder | undefined {
  return db
    .prepare(`SELECT ${folderSelect} FROM folders WHERE id = ?`)
    .get(id) as Folder | undefined;
}

router.post("/", (req, res) => {
  const body = req.body as FolderBody;

  if (!isNonEmptyString(body.name)) {
    res.status(400).json({ error: "name is required" });
    return;
  }

  const now = new Date().toISOString();
  const result = db
    .prepare(
      `
        INSERT INTO folders (name, created_at, updated_at)
        VALUES (?, ?, ?)
      `,
    )
    .run(body.name.trim(), now, now);

  res.status(201).json(findFolder(Number(result.lastInsertRowid)));
});

router.get("/", (_req, res) => {
  const folders = db
    .prepare(`SELECT ${folderSelect} FROM folders ORDER BY id ASC`)
    .all() as Folder[];

  res.status(200).json(folders);
});

router.get("/:id", (req, res) => {
  const id = parseId(req.params.id);
  const folder = id === null ? undefined : findFolder(id);

  if (!folder || id === null) {
    res.status(404).json({ error: "folder not found" });
    return;
  }

  const bookmarks = db
    .prepare(
      `SELECT ${bookmarkSelect} FROM bookmarks WHERE folder_id = ? ORDER BY id ASC`,
    )
    .all(id) as Bookmark[];

  res.status(200).json({ ...folder, bookmarks });
});

router.put("/:id", (req, res) => {
  const id = parseId(req.params.id);
  const existing = id === null ? undefined : findFolder(id);

  if (!existing || id === null) {
    res.status(404).json({ error: "folder not found" });
    return;
  }

  const body = req.body as FolderBody;

  if (!isNonEmptyString(body.name)) {
    res.status(400).json({ error: "name must be non-empty" });
    return;
  }

  db.prepare(
    `
      UPDATE folders
      SET name = ?, updated_at = ?
      WHERE id = ?
    `,
  ).run(body.name.trim(), new Date().toISOString(), id);

  res.status(200).json(findFolder(id));
});

router.delete("/:id", (req, res) => {
  const id = parseId(req.params.id);
  const existing = id === null ? undefined : findFolder(id);

  if (!existing || id === null) {
    res.status(404).json({ error: "folder not found" });
    return;
  }

  const deleteFolder = db.transaction((folderId: number) => {
    db.prepare("UPDATE bookmarks SET folder_id = NULL WHERE folder_id = ?").run(
      folderId,
    );
    db.prepare("DELETE FROM folders WHERE id = ?").run(folderId);
  });

  deleteFolder(id);
  res.status(204).send();
});

export default router;

function isNonEmptyString(value: unknown): value is string {

function isValidUrl(value: string): boolean {

}

function parseId(value: string): number | null {

  return Number.isInteger(id) && id > 0 ? id : null;
}

function parseFolderId(value: unknown): number | null | undefined {
  if (value === undefined) {

  if (value === null) {
    return null;
  }

  const id = Number(value);
  return Number.isInteger(id) && id > 0 ? id : undefined;
}

function findBookmark(id: number): Bookmark | undefined {
  return db
    .prepare(`SELECT ${bookmarkSelect} FROM bookmarks WHERE id = ?`)

}

function folderExists(id: number): boolean {
  const row = db.prepare("SELECT id FROM folders WHERE id = ?").get(id);

}

  if (typeof folderId === "number" && !folderExists(folderId)) {

    )
    .run(body.url.trim(), body.title.trim(), description, folderId ?? null, now, now);

  const bookmark = findBookmark(Number(result.lastInsertRowid));
  res.status(201).json(bookmark);
});

router.get("/", (_req, res) => {

    folderId = parsedFolderId;

import { Router } from "express";
import { db } from "./db";

type Folder = {
  id: number;
  name: string;
  created_at: string;
  updated_at: string;
};

type Bookmark = {
  id: number;
  url: string;
  title: string;
  description: string | null;
  folder_id: number | null;
  created_at: string;
  updated_at: string;
};

type FolderBody = {
  name?: unknown;
};

const router = Router();

const folderSelect = "id, name, created_at, updated_at";
const bookmarkSelect =
  "id, url, title, description, folder_id, created_at, updated_at";

function isNonEmptyString(value: unknown): value is string {
  return typeof value === "string" && value.trim().length > 0;
}

function parseId(value: string): number | null {
  const id = Number(value);
  return Number.isInteger(id) && id > 0 ? id : null;
}

function findFolder(id: number): Folder | undefined {
  return db
    .prepare(`SELECT ${folderSelect} FROM folders WHERE id = ?`)
    .get(id) as Folder | undefined;
}

router.post("/", (req, res) => {
  const body = req.body as FolderBody;

  if (!isNonEmptyString(body.name)) {
    res.status(400).json({ error: "name is required" });
    return;
  }

  const now = new Date().toISOString();
  const result = db
    .prepare(
      `
        INSERT INTO folders (name, created_at, updated_at)
        VALUES (?, ?, ?)
      `,
    )
    .run(body.name.trim(), now, now);

  res.status(201).json(findFolder(Number(result.lastInsertRowid)));
});

router.get("/", (_req, res) => {
  const folders = db
    .prepare(`SELECT ${folderSelect} FROM folders ORDER BY id ASC`)
    .all() as Folder[];

  res.status(200).json(folders);
});

router.get("/:id", (req, res) => {
  const id = parseId(req.params.id);
  const folder = id === null ? undefined : findFolder(id);

  if (!folder || id === null) {
    res.status(404).json({ error: "folder not found" });
    return;
  }

  const bookmarks = db
    .prepare(
      `SELECT ${bookmarkSelect} FROM bookmarks WHERE folder_id = ? ORDER BY id ASC`,
    )
    .all(id) as Bookmark[];

  res.status(200).json({ ...folder, bookmarks });
});

router.put("/:id", (req, res) => {
  const id = parseId(req.params.id);
  const existing = id === null ? undefined : findFolder(id);

  if (!existing || id === null) {
    res.status(404).json({ error: "folder not found" });
    return;
  }

  const body = req.body as FolderBody;

  if (!isNonEmptyString(body.name)) {
    res.status(400).json({ error: "name must be non-empty" });
    return;
  }

  db.prepare(
    `
      UPDATE folders
      SET name = ?, updated_at = ?
      WHERE id = ?
    `,
  ).run(body.name.trim(), new Date().toISOString(), id);

  res.status(200).json(findFolder(id));
});

router.delete("/:id", (req, res) => {
  const id = parseId(req.params.id);
  const existing = id === null ? undefined : findFolder(id);

  if (!existing || id === null) {
    res.status(404).json({ error: "folder not found" });
    return;
  }

  const deleteFolder = db.transaction((folderId: number) => {
    db.prepare("UPDATE bookmarks SET folder_id = NULL WHERE folder_id = ?").run(
      folderId,
    );
    db.prepare("DELETE FROM folders WHERE id = ?").run(folderId);
  });

  deleteFolder(id);
  res.status(204).send();
});

export default router;
import Database from "better-sqlite3";

export const db = new Database("data.db");

db.pragma("journal_mode = WAL");
db.pragma("foreign_keys = ON");

export function initializeDatabase(): void {
  db.exec(`
    CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS folders (
      id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,
      name TEXT NOT NULL,
      created_at TEXT NOT NULL,
      updated_at TEXT NOT NULL
    );

    CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS bookmarks (
      id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,
      url TEXT NOT NULL,
      title TEXT NOT NULL,
      description TEXT,
      folder_id INTEGER,
      created_at TEXT NOT NULL,
      updated_at TEXT NOT NULL,
      FOREIGN KEY (folder_id) REFERENCES folders(id)
    )
  `);

  const columns = db.prepare("PRAGMA table_info(bookmarks)").all() as Array<{
    name: string;
  }>;

  if (!columns.some((column) => column.name === "folder_id")) {
    db.exec(
      "ALTER TABLE bookmarks ADD COLUMN folder_id INTEGER REFERENCES folders(id)",
    );
  }
}
// Bookmark Manager API — entrypoint
// See PRODUCT_SPEC.md for full requirements
// The agent will build this out across tasks T1-T6

import express from "express";
import bookmarksRouter from "./bookmarks";
import { initializeDatabase } from "./db";
import foldersRouter from "./folders";

const app = express();
const PORT = process.env.PORT || 3000;

app.use(express.json());

initializeDatabase();

app.get("/health", (_req, res) => {
  res.status(200).json({ status: "ok" });
});

app.use("/bookmarks", bookmarksRouter);
app.use("/folders", foldersRouter);

app.listen(PORT, () => {
  console.log(`Server running on port ${PORT}`);
});

export default app;

benchmark/runs/baseline-main-valid-node22-20260525/T2-crud-bookmarks/events.jsonl:5:{"type":"item.completed","item":{"id":"item_1","type":"command_execution","command":"/bin/zsh -lc "sed -n '1,220p' README.md"","aggregated_output":"# Harness Benchmark

A controlled benchmark for measuring the effectiveness of [harness-experimental](https://github.com/hoangnb24/harness-experimental) across development phases.

## What This Is

A pre-seeded TypeScript/Express project with **6 fixed tasks** (T1–T6) that an AI agent executes. The benchmark measures time, token cost, code quality, and harness compliance. By re-running after each harness phase, we objectively measure whether the harness improved agent productivity.

## Quick Start

### Prerequisites

```bash
# Codex CLI (primary agent)
npm install -g @openai/codex
export OPENAI_API_KEY="sk-..."

# System dependencies
node --version  # v20+
jq --version    # for JSON parsing
sqlite3 --version  # for harness compliance checks
```

### Run the Benchmark

```bash
# Baseline run (against harness main branch)
./benchmark/run.sh --agent codex --harness main --run-id baseline

# Phase 2 run (against feature branch)
./benchmark/run.sh --agent codex --harness phase-2/observability-taxonomy --run-id phase-2

# Compare results
./benchmark/compare.sh baseline phase-2
```

### View Results

```bash
cat benchmark/runs/<run-id>/report.md     # Human-readable summary
cat benchmark/runs/<run-id>/scores.json   # Machine-readable scores
```

## Project Structure

```
harness-benchmark/
├── README.md                 # This file
├── PRODUCT_SPEC.md           # Product specification (what the agent builds)
├── package.json              # Pre-seeded dependencies (Express, better-sqlite3, vitest)
├── tsconfig.json             # TypeScript configuration
├── src/
│   └── index.ts              # Empty entrypoint (agent builds from here)
├── benchmark/
│   ├── PROTOCOL.md           # How runs work, what's measured, rules
│   ├── run.sh                # Main orchestrator script
│   ├── compare.sh            # Compare two run results
│   ├── tasks/                # Task prompts (T1-T6.md)
│   ├── rubrics/              # Objective scoring checklists
│   ├── lib/                  # Runner helper scripts
│   │   ├── prepare.sh        # Harness installation
│   │   ├── invoke.sh         # Agent invocation + telemetry
│   │   ├── check-functional.sh
│   │   ├── check-harness.sh
│   │   ├── check-quality.sh
│   │   └── report.sh
│   ├── runs/                 # Output directory (git-tracked results)
│   │   └── .gitkeep
│   └── seeds/                # Checkpoint states for partial re-runs
│       └── .gitkeep
```

## The Benchmark Cycle

1. **Tag** benchmark repo → `benchmark-v1`
2. **Install harness** from target ref (main, feature branch)
3. **Run T1–T6** sequentially via Codex CLI
4. **Score** against objective rubrics
5. **Compare** to previous runs

Each phase must **earn its merge** by moving the numbers.

## Tasks Overview

| Task | Name | Risk Lane | What It Tests |
|------|------|-----------|---------------|
| T1 | Project Setup | tiny | Basic scaffolding, health endpoint |
| T2 | CRUD Bookmarks | normal | Core API implementation |
| T3 | Folder Support | normal | Feature addition on existing code |
| T4 | Authentication | high-risk | Complex feature with security implications |
| T5 | Bug Fix | normal | Diagnosis and targeted fix |
| T6 | Pagination | normal | Refactoring existing API responses |

## Metrics

| Metric | What It Measures | Source |
|--------|-----------------|--------|
| Wall time | Speed of completion | `timing.json` |
| Token cost | API cost efficiency | `tokens.json` (from Codex JSONL) |
| Functional score | Does the code work? | `check-functional.sh` (curl tests) |
| Harness compliance | Did the agent use the harness? | `check-harness.sh` (sqlite3 queries) |
| Trace quality | How detailed are the traces? | `check-quality.sh` |
| Lane accuracy | Correct risk classification? | `lane.json` |

## License

MIT

benchmark/runs/baseline-main-valid-node22-20260525/T2-crud-bookmarks/events.jsonl:14:{"type":"item.completed","item":{"id":"item_6","type":"command_execution","command":"/bin/zsh -lc "sed -n '1,260p' PRODUCT_SPEC.md"","aggregated_output":"# Product Specification: Bookmark Manager API

## Overview

A REST API for managing bookmarks with folder organization, user authentication, and pagination. Built with TypeScript, Express, and better-sqlite3.

## Functional Requirements

### Core Entities

- **Bookmark**: A saved URL with title, optional description, timestamps
- **Folder**: A named collection of bookmarks
- **User**: An authenticated account that owns bookmarks and folders

### API Endpoints

#### Health
- `GET /health` → `{ "status": "ok" }`

#### Bookmarks (after auth: scoped to authenticated user)
- `POST /bookmarks` — Create a bookmark (requires: url, title)
- `GET /bookmarks` — List all bookmarks (paginated after T6)
- `GET /bookmarks/:id` — Get single bookmark
- `PUT /bookmarks/:id` — Update a bookmark
- `DELETE /bookmarks/:id` — Delete a bookmark

#### Folders
- `POST /folders` — Create a folder (requires: name)
- `GET /folders` — List all folders
- `GET /folders/:id` — Get folder with its bookmarks
- `PUT /folders/:id` — Update folder name
- `DELETE /folders/:id` — Delete folder (bookmarks become unfoldered)

#### Authentication
- `POST /auth/register` — Create account (email + password)
- `POST /auth/login` — Get JWT token
- All bookmark/folder endpoints require `Authorization: Bearer <token>` after auth is implemented

### Validation Rules

- Bookmark `url` must be a valid URL
- Bookmark `title` must be non-empty string
- Folder `name` must be non-empty string
- Email must be valid format
- Password must be >= 8 characters

### Pagination (after T6)

- `GET /bookmarks?page=1&limit=20`
- Response shape: `{ data: [...], page: 1, limit: 20, total: 45 }`
- Default: page=1, limit=20
- Max limit: 100

## Technical Stack (Pre-decided)

- **Runtime**: Node.js 20+
- **Language**: TypeScript
- **Framework**: Express
- **Database**: SQLite via better-sqlite3
- **Testing**: Vitest
- **Port**: 3000

## Non-Functional Requirements

- All responses are JSON
- Proper HTTP status codes (200, 201, 204, 400, 401, 404, 500)
- Passwords stored as hashes (bcrypt or similar)
- JWT tokens for session management
- Database file: `data.db` (application data)

# benchmark/lib/check-functional.sh — API endpoint tests via curl

check_functional() {
  local task="$1"
  local outdir="$2"
  local project_dir="$3"

  local results=()
  local server_pid=""
  local port=3000
  local base_url="http://localhost:$port"

  # Try to start the server
  (cd "$project_dir" && npm run dev > "$outdir/server.log" 2>&1) &
  server_pid=$!
  sleep 3

  # Check if server is alive
  if ! kill -0 $server_pid 2>/dev/null; then
    echo "  Server failed to start"
    echo '{"checks":[],"server_started":false}' > "$outdir/functional.json"
    return 1
  fi

  # Wait for port to be ready (up to 10s)
  local ready=false
  for i in $(seq 1 10); do
    if curl -s -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" "$base_url/health" 2>/dev/null | grep -q "200\|404\|500"; then
      ready=true
      break
    fi
    sleep 1
  done

  if [ "$ready" = "false" ]; then
    echo "  Server not responding on port $port"
    kill $server_pid 2>/dev/null || true
    echo '{"checks":[],"server_started":false}' > "$outdir/functional.json"
    return 1
  fi

  # Run task-specific checks
  case "$task" in
    T1-project-setup)
      run_check results "health_status" "GET" "$base_url/health" 200
      run_check results "health_body" "GET" "$base_url/health" 200 --body-contains '"status"'
      ;;

    T2-crud-bookmarks)
      # Create
      run_check results "create_bookmark" "POST" "$base_url/bookmarks" 201 \
        --data '{"url":"https://example.com","title":"Test Bookmark"}'
      # List
      run_check results "list_bookmarks" "GET" "$base_url/bookmarks" 200
      # Get single
      run_check results "get_bookmark" "GET" "$base_url/bookmarks/1" 200
      # Update
      run_check results "update_bookmark" "PUT" "$base_url/bookmarks/1" 200 \
        --data '{"title":"Updated Title"}'
      # Delete
      run_check results "delete_bookmark" "DELETE" "$base_url/bookmarks/1" 204
      # Validation
      run_check results "missing_title" "POST" "$base_url/bookmarks" 400 \
        --data '{"url":"https://example.com"}'
      run_check results "missing_url" "POST" "$base_url/bookmarks" 400 \
        --data '{"title":"No URL"}'
      # Regression
      run_check results "health_regression" "GET" "$base_url/health" 200
      ;;

    T3-folder-support)
      # Folder CRUD
      run_check results "create_folder" "POST" "$base_url/folders" 201 \
        --data '{"name":"Reading List"}'
      run_check results "list_folders" "GET" "$base_url/folders" 200
      run_check results "get_folder" "GET" "$base_url/folders/1" 200
      # Bookmark with folder
      run_check results "bookmark_with_folder" "POST" "$base_url/bookmarks" 201 \
        --data '{"url":"https://example.com","title":"Foldered","folder_id":1}'
      # Regression
      run_check results "bookmark_regression" "GET" "$base_url/bookmarks" 200
      ;;

    T4-authentication)
      # Register
      run_check results "register" "POST" "$base_url/auth/register" 201 \
        --data '{"email":"bench@test.com","password":"benchmark123"}'
      # Duplicate
      run_check results "duplicate_email" "POST" "$base_url/auth/register" 409 \
        --data '{"email":"bench@test.com","password":"benchmark123"}'
      # Login
      local token
      token=$(curl -s -X POST "$base_url/auth/login" \
        -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
        -d '{"email":"bench@test.com","password":"benchmark123"}' 2>/dev/null | jq -r '.token // empty')
      run_check results "login" "POST" "$base_url/auth/login" 200 \
        --data '{"email":"bench@test.com","password":"benchmark123"}'
      # Wrong password
      run_check results "wrong_password" "POST" "$base_url/auth/login" 401 \
        --data '{"email":"bench@test.com","password":"wrongpass"}'
      # Protected (no auth)
      run_check results "protected_no_auth" "GET" "$base_url/bookmarks" 401
      # Protected (with auth)
      if [ -n "$token" ]; then
        run_check results "protected_with_auth" "GET" "$base_url/bookmarks" 200 \
          --header "Authorization: Bearer $token"
        # Create with auth
        run_check results "create_with_auth" "POST" "$base_url/bookmarks" 201 \
          --header "Authorization: Bearer $token" \
          --data '{"url":"https://example.com","title":"Authed Bookmark"}'
      fi
      ;;

    T5-bug-fix)
      # Login first
      local token
      token=$(curl -s -X POST "$base_url/auth/login" \
        -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
        -d '{"email":"bench@test.com","password":"benchmark123"}' 2>/dev/null | jq -r '.token // empty')
      # If no existing user, register first
      if [ -z "$token" ]; then
        curl -s -X POST "$base_url/auth/register" \
          -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
          -d '{"email":"bench@test.com","password":"benchmark123"}' > /dev/null 2>&1
        token=$(curl -s -X POST "$base_url/auth/login" \
          -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
          -d '{"email":"bench@test.com","password":"benchmark123"}' 2>/dev/null | jq -r '.token // empty')
      fi

      if [ -n "$token" ]; then
        run_check results "empty_title_400" "POST" "$base_url/bookmarks" 400 \
          --header "Authorization: Bearer $token" \
          --data '{"url":"https://example.com","title":""}'
        run_check results "empty_url_400" "POST" "$base_url/bookmarks" 400 \
          --header "Authorization: Bearer $token" \
          --data '{"url":"","title":"Test"}'
        run_check results "valid_still_works" "POST" "$base_url/bookmarks" 201 \
          --header "Authorization: Bearer $token" \
          --data '{"url":"https://example.com","title":"Valid"}'
      fi
      # Folder empty name
      if [ -n "$token" ]; then
        run_check results "empty_folder_name" "POST" "$base_url/folders" 400 \
          --header "Authorization: Bearer $token" \
          --data '{"name":""}'
      fi
      ;;

    T6-pagination)
      # Login
      local token
      token=$(curl -s -X POST "$base_url/auth/login" \
        -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
        -d '{"email":"bench@test.com","password":"benchmark123"}' 2>/dev/null | jq -r '.token // empty')
      if [ -z "$token" ]; then
        curl -s -X POST "$base_url/auth/register" \
          -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
          -d '{"email":"bench@test.com","password":"benchmark123"}' > /dev/null 2>&1
        token=$(curl -s -X POST "$base_url/auth/login" \
          -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
          -d '{"email":"bench@test.com","password":"benchmark123"}' 2>/dev/null | jq -r '.token // empty')
      fi

      if [ -n "$token" ]; then
        # Check paginated response shape
        local response
        response=$(curl -s "$base_url/bookmarks" -H "Authorization: Bearer $token" 2>/dev/null)

        # Has expected fields
        run_check_json results "has_data_field" "$response" '.data'
        run_check_json results "has_page_field" "$response" '.page'
        run_check_json results "has_limit_field" "$response" '.limit'
        run_check_json results "has_total_field" "$response" '.total'
        run_check_json results "data_is_array" "$response" '.data | type == "array"'

        # Custom pagination
        run_check results "custom_limit" "GET" "$base_url/bookmarks?page=1&limit=5" 200 \
          --header "Authorization: Bearer $token"

        # Invalid
        run_check results "invalid_page" "GET" "$base_url/bookmarks?page=0" 400 \
          --header "Authorization: Bearer $token"
        run_check results "invalid_limit" "GET" "$base_url/bookmarks?limit=200" 400 \
          --header "Authorization: Bearer $token"

        # Auth still required
        run_check results "still_protected" "GET" "$base_url/bookmarks" 401
      fi
      ;;
  esac

  # Stop server
  kill $server_pid 2>/dev/null || true
  wait $server_pid 2>/dev/null || true

  # Write results
  write_functional_json "$outdir/functional.json" "${results[@]}"
}

# Helper: run a single endpoint check
run_check() {
  local -n arr=$1; shift
  local name="$1"; shift
  local method="$1"; shift
  local url="$1"; shift
  local expected_status="$1"; shift

  local extra_args=()
  local body_contains=""
  while [[ $# -gt 0 ]]; do
    case $1 in
      --data) extra_args+=(-H 'Content-Type: application/json' -d "$2"); shift 2 ;;
      --header) extra_args+=(-H "$2"); shift 2 ;;
      --body-contains) body_contains="$2"; shift 2 ;;
      *) shift ;;
    esac
  done

  local response_code response_body
  response_body=$(curl -s -w "\n%{http_code}" -X "$method" "${extra_args[@]}" "$url" 2>/dev/null)
  response_code=$(echo "$response_body" | tail -1)
  response_body=$(echo "$response_body" | sed '$d')

  local pass=false
  if [ "$response_code" = "$expected_status" ]; then
    pass=true
  fi

  # Optional body check
  if [ -n "$body_contains" ] && [ "$pass" = "true" ]; then
    if ! echo "$response_body" | grep -q "$body_contains"; then
      pass=false
    fi
  fi

  arr+=("{\"name\":\"$name\",\"pass\":$pass,\"expected\":$expected_status,\"actual\":$response_code}")
}

# Helper: check JSON response field
run_check_json() {
  local -n arr=$1; shift
  local name="$1"; shift
  local json="$1"; shift
  local jq_expr="$1"; shift

  local pass=false
  if echo "$json" | jq -e "$jq_expr" > /dev/null 2>&1; then
    pass=true
  fi

  arr+=("{\"name\":\"$name\",\"pass\":$pass}")
}

# Helper: write results JSON
write_functional_json() {
  local outfile="$1"; shift
  local checks=("$@")

  echo -n '{"server_started":true,"checks":[' > "$outfile"

benchmark/runs/baseline-main-valid-node22-20260525/T1-project-setup/events.jsonl:12:{"type":"item.completed","item":{"id":"item_5","type":"command_execution","command":"/bin/zsh -lc "sed -n '1,220p' README.md"","aggregated_output":"# Harness Benchmark

A controlled benchmark for measuring the effectiveness of [harness-experimental](https://github.com/hoangnb24/harness-experimental) across development phases.

## What This Is

A pre-seeded TypeScript/Express project with **6 fixed tasks** (T1–T6) that an AI agent executes. The benchmark measures time, token cost, code quality, and harness compliance. By re-running after each harness phase, we objectively measure whether the harness improved agent productivity.

## Quick Start

### Prerequisites

```bash
# Codex CLI (primary agent)
npm install -g @openai/codex
export OPENAI_API_KEY="sk-..."

# System dependencies
node --version  # v20+
jq --version    # for JSON parsing
sqlite3 --version  # for harness compliance checks
```

### Run the Benchmark

```bash
# Baseline run (against harness main branch)
./benchmark/run.sh --agent codex --harness main --run-id baseline

# Phase 2 run (against feature branch)
./benchmark/run.sh --agent codex --harness phase-2/observability-taxonomy --run-id phase-2

# Compare results
./benchmark/compare.sh baseline phase-2
```

### View Results

```bash
cat benchmark/runs/<run-id>/report.md     # Human-readable summary
cat benchmark/runs/<run-id>/scores.json   # Machine-readable scores
```

## Project Structure

```
harness-benchmark/
├── README.md                 # This file
├── PRODUCT_SPEC.md           # Product specification (what the agent builds)
```

## Overview

A REST API for managing bookmarks with folder organization, user authentication, and pagination. Built with TypeScript, Express, and better-sqlite3.

## Functional Requirements

### Core Entities

- **Bookmark**: A saved URL with title, optional description, timestamps
- **Folder**: A named collection of bookmarks
- **User**: An authenticated account that owns bookmarks and folders

### API Endpoints

#### Health
- `GET /health` → `{ "status": "ok" }`

#### Bookmarks (after auth: scoped to authenticated user)
- `POST /bookmarks` — Create a bookmark (requires: url, title)
- `GET /bookmarks` — List all bookmarks (paginated after T6)
- `GET /bookmarks/:id` — Get single bookmark
- `PUT /bookmarks/:id` — Update a bookmark
- `DELETE /bookmarks/:id` — Delete a bookmark

#### Folders
- `POST /folders` — Create a folder (requires: name)
- `GET /folders` — List all folders
- `GET /folders/:id` — Get folder with its bookmarks
- `PUT /folders/:id` — Update folder name
- `DELETE /folders/:id` — Delete folder (bookmarks become unfoldered)

#### Authentication
- `POST /auth/register` — Create account (email + password)
- `POST /auth/login` — Get JWT token
- All bookmark/folder endpoints require `Authorization: Bearer <token>` after auth is implemented

### Validation Rules

- Bookmark `url` must be a valid URL
- Bookmark `title` must be non-empty string
- Folder `name` must be non-empty string
- Email must be valid format
- Password must be >= 8 characters

### Pagination (after T6)

- `GET /bookmarks?page=1&limit=20`
- Response shape: `{ data: [...], page: 1, limit: 20, total: 45 }`
- Default: page=1, limit=20
- Max limit: 100

## Technical Stack (Pre-decided)

- **Runtime**: Node.js 20+
- **Language**: TypeScript
- **Framework**: Express
- **Database**: SQLite via better-sqlite3
- **Testing**: Vitest
- **Port**: 3000

## Non-Functional Requirements

- All responses are JSON
- Proper HTTP status codes (200, 201, 204, 400, 401, 404, 500)
- Passwords stored as hashes (bcrypt or similar)
- JWT tokens for session management
- Database file: `data.db` (application data)

A controlled benchmark for measuring the effectiveness of [harness-experimental](https://github.com/hoangnb24/harness-experimental) across development phases.

## What This Is

A pre-seeded TypeScript/Express project with **6 fixed tasks** (T1–T6) that an AI agent executes. The benchmark measures time, token cost, code quality, and harness compliance. By re-running after each harness phase, we objectively measure whether the harness improved agent productivity.

## Quick Start

### Prerequisites

```bash
# Codex CLI (primary agent)
npm install -g @openai/codex
export OPENAI_API_KEY="sk-..."

# System dependencies
node --version  # v20+
jq --version    # for JSON parsing
sqlite3 --version  # for harness compliance checks
```

### Run the Benchmark

```bash
# Baseline run (against harness main branch)
./benchmark/run.sh --agent codex --harness main --run-id baseline

# Phase 2 run (against feature branch)
./benchmark/run.sh --agent codex --harness phase-2/observability-taxonomy --run-id phase-2

# Compare results
./benchmark/compare.sh baseline phase-2
```

### View Results

```bash
cat benchmark/runs/<run-id>/report.md     # Human-readable summary
cat benchmark/runs/<run-id>/scores.json   # Machine-readable scores
```

const db: Database.Database = new Database("data.db");

db.pragma("journal_mode = WAL");
db.pragma("foreign_keys = ON");

db.exec(`
  CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS users (
    id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,
    email TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,
    password_hash TEXT NOT NULL,
    created_at TEXT NOT NULL
  )
`);

db.exec(`
  CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS folders (
    id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,
    user_id INTEGER NOT NULL,
    name TEXT NOT NULL,
    created_at TEXT NOT NULL,
    updated_at TEXT NOT NULL,
    FOREIGN KEY (user_id) REFERENCES users(id) ON DELETE CASCADE
  )
`);

db.exec(`
  CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS bookmarks (
    id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,
    user_id INTEGER NOT NULL,
    url TEXT NOT NULL,
    title TEXT NOT NULL,
    description TEXT,
    folder_id INTEGER,
    created_at TEXT NOT NULL,
    updated_at TEXT NOT NULL,
    FOREIGN KEY (user_id) REFERENCES users(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    FOREIGN KEY (folder_id) REFERENCES folders(id) ON DELETE SET NULL
  )
`);

const bookmarkColumns = db.prepare("PRAGMA table_info(bookmarks)").all() as Array<{
  name: string;
}>;

if (!bookmarkColumns.some((column) => column.name === "folder_id")) {
  db.exec(`
    ALTER TABLE bookmarks
    ADD COLUMN folder_id INTEGER REFERENCES folders(id) ON DELETE SET NULL
  `);
}

if (!bookmarkColumns.some((column) => column.name === "user_id")) {
  db.exec(`
    ALTER TABLE bookmarks
    ADD COLUMN user_id INTEGER
  `);
}

const folderColumns = db.prepare("PRAGMA table_info(folders)").all() as Array<{
  name: string;
}>;

if (!folderColumns.some((column) => column.name === "user_id")) {
  db.exec(`
    ALTER TABLE folders
    ADD COLUMN user_id INTEGER
  `);
}

export default db;

benchmark/runs/baseline-main-valid-node22-20260525/T6-pagination/events.jsonl:24:{"type":"item.completed","item":{"id":"item_12","type":"command_execution","command":"/bin/zsh -lc "sed -n '1,240p' src/index.ts"","aggregated_output":"// Bookmark Manager API — entrypoint
// See PRODUCT_SPEC.md for full requirements
// The agent will build this out across tasks T1-T6

import express from "express";
import { createAuthRouter, requireAuth } from "./auth";
import { createBookmarksRouter } from "./bookmarks";
import db from "./database";
import { createFoldersRouter } from "./folders";

const app = express();
const PORT = process.env.PORT || 3000;

app.use(express.json());

app.get("/health", (_req, res) => {
  res.status(200).json({ status: "ok" });
});

app.use("/auth", createAuthRouter(db));
app.use("/bookmarks", requireAuth, createBookmarksRouter(db));
app.use("/folders", requireAuth, createFoldersRouter(db));

app.listen(PORT, () => {
  console.log(`Server running on port ${PORT}`);
});

export default app;
