  id: number;
  url: string;
  title: string;
  description: string | null;
  folder_id: number | null;
  created_at: string;
  updated_at: string;
};

const toBookmarkResponse = (row: BookmarkRow): BookmarkResponse => ({
  id: row.id,
  url: row.url,
  title: row.title,
  description: row.description,
  folder_id: row.folder_id,
  created_at: row.created_at,
  updated_at: row.updated_at,
});

const getBookmarkId = (req: Request, res: Response): number | null => {
  const id = Number(req.params.id);

  if (!Number.isInteger(id) || id < 1) {
    res.status(404).json({ error: "Bookmark not found" });
    return null;
  }

  return id;
};

const readNonEmptyString = (value: unknown): string | undefined => {
  if (typeof value !== "string") {
    return undefined;
  }

  const trimmed = value.trim();
  return trimmed.length > 0 ? trimmed : undefined;
};

const readOptionalFolderId = (value: unknown): number | null | undefined => {
  if (value === undefined || value === null) {
    return null;
  }

  if (typeof value !== "number" || !Number.isInteger(value) || value < 1) {
    return undefined;
  }

  return value;
};

export const createBookmarksRouter = (db: Database.Database): Router => {
  const router = Router();

  const findById = db.prepare("SELECT * FROM bookmarks WHERE id = ? AND user_id = ?");
  const findFolderById = db.prepare("SELECT id FROM folders WHERE id = ? AND user_id = ?");

  const validateFolderId = (
    value: unknown,
    userId: number,
    res: Response,
  ): number | null | undefined => {
    const folderId = readOptionalFolderId(value);

    if (folderId === undefined) {
      res.status(400).json({ error: "folder_id must reference an existing folder" });
      return undefined;
    }

    if (folderId !== null && !findFolderById.get(folderId, userId)) {
      res.status(400).json({ error: "folder_id must reference an existing folder" });
      return undefined;
    }

    return folderId;
  };

  router.post("/", (req, res) => {
    const userId = getAuthenticatedUserId(req);
    const url = readNonEmptyString(req.body?.url);
    const title = readNonEmptyString(req.body?.title);
    const description = req.body?.description;
    const folderId = validateFolderId(req.body?.folder_id, userId, res);

    if (folderId === undefined) {
      return;
    }

    if (!url) {
      return res.status(400).json({ error: "url must be a non-empty string" });
    }

    if (!title) {
      return res.status(400).json({ error: "title must be a non-empty string" });
    }

    const now = new Date().toISOString();
    const result = db
      .prepare(
        `
          INSERT INTO bookmarks (user_id, url, title, description, folder_id, created_at, updated_at)
          VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?)
        `,
      )
      .run(
        userId,
        url,
        title,
        typeof description === "string" ? description : null,
        folderId,
        now,
        now,
      );

    const bookmark = findById.get(result.lastInsertRowid, userId) as BookmarkRow;

    return res.status(201).json(toBookmarkResponse(bookmark));
  });

  router.get("/", (req, res) => {
    const userId = getAuthenticatedUserId(req);
    const bookmarks = db
      .prepare("SELECT * FROM bookmarks WHERE user_id = ? ORDER BY id ASC")
      .all(userId) as BookmarkRow[];

    return res.status(200).json(bookmarks.map(toBookmarkResponse));
  });

  router.get("/:id", (req, res) => {
    const userId = getAuthenticatedUserId(req);
    const id = getBookmarkId(req, res);
    if (id === null) {
      return;
    }

    const bookmark = findById.get(id, userId) as BookmarkRow | undefined;
    if (!bookmark) {
      return res.status(404).json({ error: "Bookmark not found" });
    }

    return res.status(200).json(toBookmarkResponse(bookmark));
  });

  router.put("/:id", (req, res) => {
    const userId = getAuthenticatedUserId(req);
    const id = getBookmarkId(req, res);
    if (id === null) {
      return;
    }

    const existing = findById.get(id, userId) as BookmarkRow | undefined;
    if (!existing) {
      return res.status(404).json({ error: "Bookmark not found" });
    }

    const url =
      req.body?.url === undefined ? existing.url : readNonEmptyString(req.body.url);
    const title =
      req.body?.title === undefined
        ? existing.title
        : readNonEmptyString(req.body.title);
    const description =
      req.body?.description === undefined
        ? existing.description
        : typeof req.body.description === "string"
          ? req.body.description
          : null;
    const folderId =
      req.body?.folder_id === undefined
        ? existing.folder_id
        : validateFolderId(req.body.folder_id, userId, res);

    if (folderId === undefined) {
      return;
    }

    if (!url) {
      return res.status(400).json({ error: "url must be a non-empty string" });
    }

    if (!title) {
      return res.status(400).json({ error: "title must be a non-empty string" });
    }

    const updatedAt = new Date().toISOString();
    db.prepare(
      `
        UPDATE bookmarks
        SET url = ?, title = ?, description = ?, folder_id = ?, updated_at = ?
        WHERE id = ? AND user_id = ?
      `,
    ).run(url, title, description, folderId, updatedAt, id, userId);

    const updated = findById.get(id, userId) as BookmarkRow;

    return res.status(200).json(toBookmarkResponse(updated));
  });

  router.delete("/:id", (req, res) => {
    const userId = getAuthenticatedUserId(req);
    const id = getBookmarkId(req, res);
    if (id === null) {
      return;
    }

    const result = db.prepare("DELETE FROM bookmarks WHERE id = ? AND user_id = ?").run(
      id,
      userId,
    );
    if (result.changes === 0) {
      return res.status(404).json({ error: "Bookmark not found" });
    }

    return res.status(204).send();
  });

  return router;
};

import type { Request, Response } from "express";
import type Database from "better-sqlite3";
import { getAuthenticatedUserId } from "./auth";

type FolderRow = {
  id: number;
  user_id: number;
  name: string;
  created_at: string;
  updated_at: string;
};

type BookmarkRow = {
  id: number;
  user_id: number;
  url: string;
  title: string;
  description: string | null;
  folder_id: number | null;
  created_at: string;
  updated_at: string;
};

type FolderResponse = Omit<FolderRow, "user_id">;

type FolderWithBookmarksResponse = FolderResponse & {
  bookmarks: BookmarkResponse[];
};

type BookmarkResponse = Omit<BookmarkRow, "user_id">;

const toFolderResponse = (row: FolderRow): FolderResponse => ({
  id: row.id,
  name: row.name,
  created_at: row.created_at,
  updated_at: row.updated_at,
});

const toBookmarkResponse = (row: BookmarkRow): BookmarkResponse => ({
  id: row.id,
  url: row.url,
  title: row.title,
  description: row.description,
  folder_id: row.folder_id,
  created_at: row.created_at,
  updated_at: row.updated_at,
});

const readNonEmptyString = (value: unknown): string | undefined => {
  if (typeof value !== "string") {
    return undefined;
  }

  const trimmed = value.trim();
  return trimmed.length > 0 ? trimmed : undefined;
};

const getFolderId = (req: Request, res: Response): number | null => {
  const id = Number(req.params.id);

  if (!Number.isInteger(id) || id < 1) {
    res.status(404).json({ error: "Folder not found" });
    return null;
  }

  return id;
};

export const createFoldersRouter = (db: Database.Database): Router => {
  const router = Router();

  const findById = db.prepare("SELECT * FROM folders WHERE id = ? AND user_id = ?");
  const findBookmarksByFolderId = db.prepare(
    "SELECT * FROM bookmarks WHERE folder_id = ? AND user_id = ? ORDER BY id ASC",
  );

  router.post("/", (req, res) => {
    const userId = getAuthenticatedUserId(req);
    const name = readNonEmptyString(req.body?.name);

    if (!name) {
      return res.status(400).json({ error: "name must be a non-empty string" });
    }

    const now = new Date().toISOString();
    const result = db
      .prepare(
        `
          INSERT INTO folders (user_id, name, created_at, updated_at)
          VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?)
        `,
      )
      .run(userId, name, now, now);

    const folder = findById.get(result.lastInsertRowid, userId) as FolderRow;

    return res.status(201).json(toFolderResponse(folder));
  });

  router.get("/", (req, res) => {
    const userId = getAuthenticatedUserId(req);
    const folders = db
      .prepare("SELECT * FROM folders WHERE user_id = ? ORDER BY id ASC")
      .all(userId) as FolderRow[];

    return res.status(200).json(folders.map(toFolderResponse));
  });

  router.get("/:id", (req, res) => {
    const userId = getAuthenticatedUserId(req);
    const id = getFolderId(req, res);
    if (id === null) {
      return;
    }

    const folder = findById.get(id, userId) as FolderRow | undefined;
    if (!folder) {
      return res.status(404).json({ error: "Folder not found" });
    }

    const bookmarks = findBookmarksByFolderId.all(id, userId) as BookmarkRow[];
    const response: FolderWithBookmarksResponse = {
      ...toFolderResponse(folder),
      bookmarks: bookmarks.map(toBookmarkResponse),
    };

    return res.status(200).json(response);
  });

  router.put("/:id", (req, res) => {
    const userId = getAuthenticatedUserId(req);
    const id = getFolderId(req, res);
    if (id === null) {
      return;
    }

    const existing = findById.get(id, userId) as FolderRow | undefined;
    if (!existing) {
      return res.status(404).json({ error: "Folder not found" });
    }

    const name =
      req.body?.name === undefined ? existing.name : readNonEmptyString(req.body.name);
    if (!name) {
      return res.status(400).json({ error: "name must be a non-empty string" });
    }

    const updatedAt = new Date().toISOString();
    db.prepare("UPDATE folders SET name = ?, updated_at = ? WHERE id = ? AND user_id = ?").run(
      name,
      updatedAt,
      id,
      userId,
    );

    const updated = findById.get(id, userId) as FolderRow;

    return res.status(200).json(toFolderResponse(updated));
  });

  router.delete("/:id", (req, res) => {
    const userId = getAuthenticatedUserId(req);
    const id = getFolderId(req, res);
    if (id === null) {
      return;
    }

    const result = db.prepare("DELETE FROM folders WHERE id = ? AND user_id = ?").run(
      id,
      userId,
    );
    if (result.changes === 0) {
      return res.status(404).json({ error: "Folder not found" });
    }

    return res.status(204).send();
  });

  return router;
};

benchmark/runs/baseline-main-valid-node22-20260525/T6-pagination/events.jsonl:39:{"type":"item.completed","item":{"id":"item_19","type":"command_execution","command":"/bin/zsh -lc "sed -n '1,220p' docs/stories/US-005-validation-empty-strings.md"","aggregated_output":"# US-005 Validation Rejects Empty Strings

## Status

implemented

## Lane

normal

## Product Contract

Bookmark and folder create/update endpoints reject missing, non-string, empty, or
whitespace-only required text fields with `400` JSON responses before database
insertion or update.

## Relevant Product Docs

- `PRODUCT_SPEC.md`
- `docs/stories/US-002-crud-bookmarks.md`
- `docs/stories/US-003-folder-support.md`

## Acceptance Criteria

- `POST /bookmarks` rejects empty or whitespace-only `url` with `400`.
- `POST /bookmarks` rejects empty or whitespace-only `title` with `400`.
- `PUT /bookmarks/:id` rejects provided empty or whitespace-only `url` or
  `title` with `400`.
- `POST /folders` rejects empty or whitespace-only `name` with `400`.
- `PUT /folders/:id` rejects provided empty or whitespace-only `name` with
  `400`.
- Validation errors include an `error` field explaining the invalid field.

## Design Notes

- Commands: create/update bookmark and folder rows.
- Queries: read existing records before updates.
- API: Express routes under `/bookmarks` and `/folders`.
- Tables: `bookmarks`, `folders`.
- Domain rules: required text fields are trimmed and must remain non-empty.
- UI surfaces: none.

## Validation

| Layer | Expected proof |
| --- | --- |
| Unit | TypeScript build |
| Integration | Manual HTTP smoke checks for invalid bookmark and folder fields |
| E2E | Existing benchmark functional coverage remains applicable |
| Platform | Node/Express server starts on a local port |
| Release | Not applicable |

## Harness Delta

No harness changes expected.

## Evidence

- `npm run build` passed.
- `npm test -- --passWithNoTests` passed with no test files present.
- Manual HTTP smoke check passed for empty and whitespace-only bookmark title,
  empty bookmark URL, empty folder name, empty bookmark update title,
  whitespace-only folder update name, and valid create/update regressions.

     1	# US-006 Bookmark Pagination
     2	
     3	## Status
     4	
     5	implemented
     6	
     7	## Lane
     8	
     9	normal
    10	
    11	## Product Contract
    12	
    13	`GET /bookmarks` returns a paginated JSON envelope scoped to the authenticated
    14	user instead of a raw array.
    15	
    16	## Relevant Product Docs
    17	
    18	- `PRODUCT_SPEC.md`
    19	- `docs/stories/US-002-crud-bookmarks.md`
    20	- `docs/stories/US-004-authentication/overview.md`
    21	
    22	## Acceptance Criteria
    23	
    24	- `GET /bookmarks` returns `data`, `page`, `limit`, and `total`.
    25	- Default pagination uses `page=1` and `limit=20`.
    26	- `page` must be an integer greater than or equal to 1.
    27	- `limit` must be an integer from 1 through 100.
    28	- `total` counts all bookmarks for the authenticated user, not only returned
    29	  rows.

benchmark/runs/baseline-main-valid-node22-20260525/T4-authentication/events.jsonl:7:{"type":"item.completed","item":{"id":"item_2","type":"command_execution","command":"/bin/zsh -lc "sed -n '1,220p' README.md"","aggregated_output":"# Harness Benchmark

A controlled benchmark for measuring the effectiveness of [harness-experimental](https://github.com/hoangnb24/harness-experimental) across development phases.

## What This Is

A pre-seeded TypeScript/Express project with **6 fixed tasks** (T1–T6) that an AI agent executes. The benchmark measures time, token cost, code quality, and harness compliance. By re-running after each harness phase, we objectively measure whether the harness improved agent productivity.

## Quick Start

### Prerequisites

```bash
# Codex CLI (primary agent)
npm install -g @openai/codex
export OPENAI_API_KEY="sk-..."

# System dependencies
node --version  # v20+
jq --version    # for JSON parsing
sqlite3 --version  # for harness compliance checks
```

### Run the Benchmark

```bash
# Baseline run (against harness main branch)
./benchmark/run.sh --agent codex --harness main --run-id baseline

# Phase 2 run (against feature branch)
./benchmark/run.sh --agent codex --harness phase-2/observability-taxonomy --run-id phase-2

# Compare results
./benchmark/compare.sh baseline phase-2
```

### View Results

```bash
cat benchmark/runs/<run-id>/report.md     # Human-readable summary
cat benchmark/runs/<run-id>/scores.json   # Machine-readable scores
```

## Project Structure

```
harness-benchmark/
├── README.md                 # This file
├── PRODUCT_SPEC.md           # Product specification (what the agent builds)
├── package.json              # Pre-seeded dependencies (Express, better-sqlite3, vitest)
├── tsconfig.json             # TypeScript configuration
├── src/
│   └── index.ts              # Empty entrypoint (agent builds from here)
├── benchmark/
│   ├── PROTOCOL.md           # How runs work, what's measured, rules
│   ├── run.sh                # Main orchestrator script
│   ├── compare.sh            # Compare two run results
│   ├── tasks/                # Task prompts (T1-T6.md)
│   ├── rubrics/              # Objective scoring checklists
│   ├── lib/                  # Runner helper scripts
│   │   ├── prepare.sh        # Harness installation
│   │   ├── invoke.sh         # Agent invocation + telemetry
│   │   ├── check-functional.sh
│   │   ├── check-harness.sh
│   │   ├── check-quality.sh
│   │   └── report.sh
│   ├── runs/                 # Output directory (git-tracked results)
│   │   └── .gitkeep
│   └── seeds/                # Checkpoint states for partial re-runs
│       └── .gitkeep
```

## The Benchmark Cycle

1. **Tag** benchmark repo → `benchmark-v1`
2. **Install harness** from target ref (main, feature branch)
3. **Run T1–T6** sequentially via Codex CLI
4. **Score** against objective rubrics
5. **Compare** to previous runs

Each phase must **earn its merge** by moving the numbers.

## Tasks Overview

| Task | Name | Risk Lane | What It Tests |
|------|------|-----------|---------------|
| T1 | Project Setup | tiny | Basic scaffolding, health endpoint |
| T2 | CRUD Bookmarks | normal | Core API implementation |
| T3 | Folder Support | normal | Feature addition on existing code |
| T4 | Authentication | high-risk | Complex feature with security implications |
| T5 | Bug Fix | normal | Diagnosis and targeted fix |
| T6 | Pagination | normal | Refactoring existing API responses |

## Metrics

| Metric | What It Measures | Source |
|--------|-----------------|--------|
| Wall time | Speed of completion | `timing.json` |
| Token cost | API cost efficiency | `tokens.json` (from Codex JSONL) |
| Functional score | Does the code work? | `check-functional.sh` (curl tests) |
| Harness compliance | Did the agent use the harness? | `check-harness.sh` (sqlite3 queries) |
| Trace quality | How detailed are the traces? | `check-quality.sh` |
| Lane accuracy | Correct risk classification? | `lane.json` |

## License

MIT

benchmark/runs/baseline-main-valid-node22-20260525/T4-authentication/events.jsonl:16:{"type":"item.completed","item":{"id":"item_7","type":"command_execution","command":"/bin/zsh -lc "sed -n '1,260p' PRODUCT_SPEC.md"","aggregated_output":"# Product Specification: Bookmark Manager API

## Overview

A REST API for managing bookmarks with folder organization, user authentication, and pagination. Built with TypeScript, Express, and better-sqlite3.

## Functional Requirements

### Core Entities

- **Bookmark**: A saved URL with title, optional description, timestamps
- **Folder**: A named collection of bookmarks
- **User**: An authenticated account that owns bookmarks and folders

### API Endpoints

#### Health
- `GET /health` → `{ "status": "ok" }`

#### Bookmarks (after auth: scoped to authenticated user)
- `POST /bookmarks` — Create a bookmark (requires: url, title)
- `GET /bookmarks` — List all bookmarks (paginated after T6)
- `GET /bookmarks/:id` — Get single bookmark
- `PUT /bookmarks/:id` — Update a bookmark
- `DELETE /bookmarks/:id` — Delete a bookmark

#### Folders
- `POST /folders` — Create a folder (requires: name)
- `GET /folders` — List all folders
- `GET /folders/:id` — Get folder with its bookmarks
- `PUT /folders/:id` — Update folder name
- `DELETE /folders/:id` — Delete folder (bookmarks become unfoldered)

#### Authentication
- `POST /auth/register` — Create account (email + password)
- `POST /auth/login` — Get JWT token
- All bookmark/folder endpoints require `Authorization: Bearer <token>` after auth is implemented

### Validation Rules

- Bookmark `url` must be a valid URL
- Bookmark `title` must be non-empty string
- Folder `name` must be non-empty string
- Email must be valid format
- Password must be >= 8 characters

### Pagination (after T6)

- `GET /bookmarks?page=1&limit=20`
- Response shape: `{ data: [...], page: 1, limit: 20, total: 45 }`
- Default: page=1, limit=20
- Max limit: 100

## Technical Stack (Pre-decided)

- **Runtime**: Node.js 20+
- **Language**: TypeScript
- **Framework**: Express
- **Database**: SQLite via better-sqlite3
- **Testing**: Vitest
- **Port**: 3000

## Non-Functional Requirements

- All responses are JSON
- Proper HTTP status codes (200, 201, 204, 400, 401, 404, 500)
- Passwords stored as hashes (bcrypt or similar)
- JWT tokens for session management
- Database file: `data.db` (application data)

// See PRODUCT_SPEC.md for full requirements
// The agent will build this out across tasks T1-T6

import express from "express";
import { createBookmarksRouter } from "./bookmarks";
import db from "./database";
import { createFoldersRouter } from "./folders";

const app = express();
const PORT = process.env.PORT || 3000;

app.use(express.json());

app.get("/health", (_req, res) => {
  res.status(200).json({ status: "ok" });
});

app.use("/bookmarks", createBookmarksRouter(db));
app.use("/folders", createFoldersRouter(db));

app.listen(PORT, () => {
  console.log(`Server running on port ${PORT}`);
});

export default app;
import Database from "better-sqlite3";

const db: Database.Database = new Database("data.db");

db.pragma("journal_mode = WAL");
db.pragma("foreign_keys = ON");

db.exec(`
  CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS folders (
    id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,
    name TEXT NOT NULL,
    created_at TEXT NOT NULL,
    updated_at TEXT NOT NULL
  )
`);

db.exec(`
  CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS bookmarks (
    id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,
    url TEXT NOT NULL,
    title TEXT NOT NULL,
    description TEXT,
    folder_id INTEGER,
    created_at TEXT NOT NULL,
    updated_at TEXT NOT NULL,
    FOREIGN KEY (folder_id) REFERENCES folders(id) ON DELETE SET NULL
  )
`);

const bookmarkColumns = db.prepare("PRAGMA table_info(bookmarks)").all() as Array<{
  name: string;
}>;

if (!bookmarkColumns.some((column) => column.name === "folder_id")) {
  db.exec(`
    ALTER TABLE bookmarks
    ADD COLUMN folder_id INTEGER REFERENCES folders(id) ON DELETE SET NULL
  `);
}

export default db;
import { Router } from "express";
import type { Request, Response } from "express";
import type Database from "better-sqlite3";

type BookmarkRow = {
  id: number;
  url: string;
  title: string;
  description: string | null;
  folder_id: number | null;
  created_at: string;
  updated_at: string;
};

type BookmarkResponse = {
  id: number;
  url: string;
  title: string;
  description: string | null;
  folder_id: number | null;
  created_at: string;
  updated_at: string;
};

const toBookmarkResponse = (row: BookmarkRow): BookmarkResponse => ({
  id: row.id,
  url: row.url,
  title: row.title,
  description: row.description,
  folder_id: row.folder_id,
  created_at: row.created_at,
  updated_at: row.updated_at,
});

const getBookmarkId = (req: Request, res: Response): number | null => {
  const id = Number(req.params.id);

  if (!Number.isInteger(id) || id < 1) {
    res.status(404).json({ error: "Bookmark not found" });
    return null;
  }

  return id;
};

const readString = (value: unknown): string | undefined => {
  if (typeof value !== "string") {
    return undefined;
  }

  return value.trim();
};

const readOptionalFolderId = (value: unknown): number | null | undefined => {
  if (value === undefined || value === null) {
    return null;
  }

  if (typeof value !== "number" || !Number.isInteger(value) || value < 1) {
    return undefined;
  }

  return value;
};

export const createBookmarksRouter = (db: Database.Database): Router => {
  const router = Router();

  const findById = db.prepare("SELECT * FROM bookmarks WHERE id = ?");
  const findFolderById = db.prepare("SELECT id FROM folders WHERE id = ?");

  const validateFolderId = (value: unknown, res: Response): number | null | undefined => {
    const folderId = readOptionalFolderId(value);

    if (folderId === undefined) {
      res.status(400).json({ error: "folder_id must reference an existing folder" });
      return undefined;
    }

    if (folderId !== null && !findFolderById.get(folderId)) {
      res.status(400).json({ error: "folder_id must reference an existing folder" });
      return undefined;
    }

    return folderId;
  };

  router.post("/", (req, res) => {
    const url = readString(req.body?.url);
    const title = readString(req.body?.title);
    const description = req.body?.description;
    const folderId = validateFolderId(req.body?.folder_id, res);

    if (folderId === undefined) {
      return;
    }

    if (!url) {
      return res.status(400).json({ error: "url is required" });
    }

    if (!title) {
      return res.status(400).json({ error: "title is required" });
    }

    const now = new Date().toISOString();
    const result = db
      .prepare(
        `
          INSERT INTO bookmarks (url, title, description, folder_id, created_at, updated_at)
          VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?)
        `,
      )
      .run(
        url,
        title,
        typeof description === "string" ? description : null,
        folderId,
        now,
        now,
      );

    const bookmark = findById.get(result.lastInsertRowid) as BookmarkRow;

    return res.status(201).json(toBookmarkResponse(bookmark));
  });

  router.get("/", (_req, res) => {
    const bookmarks = db
      .prepare("SELECT * FROM bookmarks ORDER BY id ASC")
      .all() as BookmarkRow[];

    return res.status(200).json(bookmarks.map(toBookmarkResponse));
  });

  router.get("/:id", (req, res) => {
    const id = getBookmarkId(req, res);
    if (id === null) {
      return;
    }

    const bookmark = findById.get(id) as BookmarkRow | undefined;
    if (!bookmark) {
      return res.status(404).json({ error: "Bookmark not found" });
    }

    return res.status(200).json(toBookmarkResponse(bookmark));
  });

  router.put("/:id", (req, res) => {
    const id = getBookmarkId(req, res);
    if (id === null) {
      return;
    }

    const existing = findById.get(id) as BookmarkRow | undefined;
    if (!existing) {
      return res.status(404).json({ error: "Bookmark not found" });
    }

    const url = req.body?.url === undefined ? existing.url : readString(req.body.url);
    const title =
      req.body?.title === undefined ? existing.title : readString(req.body.title);
    const description =
      req.body?.description === undefined
        ? existing.description
        : typeof req.body.description === "string"
          ? req.body.description
          : null;
    const folderId =
      req.body?.folder_id === undefined
        ? existing.folder_id
        : validateFolderId(req.body.folder_id, res);

    if (folderId === undefined) {
      return;
    }

    if (!url) {
      return res.status(400).json({ error: "url must be non-empty" });
    }

    if (!title) {
      return res.status(400).json({ error: "title must be non-empty" });
    }

    const updatedAt = new Date().toISOString();
    db.prepare(
      `
        UPDATE bookmarks
        SET url = ?, title = ?, description = ?, folder_id = ?, updated_at = ?
        WHERE id = ?
      `,
    ).run(url, title, description, folderId, updatedAt, id);

    const updated = findById.get(id) as BookmarkRow;

    return res.status(200).json(toBookmarkResponse(updated));
  });

  router.delete("/:id", (req, res) => {
    const id = getBookmarkId(req, res);
    if (id === null) {
      return;
    }

    const result = db.prepare("DELETE FROM bookmarks WHERE id = ?").run(id);
    if (result.changes === 0) {
      return res.status(404).json({ error: "Bookmark not found" });
    }

    return res.status(204).send();
  });

  return router;
};
import { Router } from "express";
import type { Request, Response } from "express";
import type Database from "better-sqlite3";

type FolderRow = {
  id: number;

type BookmarkRow = {

type FolderResponse = FolderRow;

type FolderWithBookmarksResponse = FolderResponse & {

const toFolderResponse = (row: FolderRow): FolderResponse => ({
  id: row.id,
  name: row.name,
  created_at: row.created_at,
  updated_at: row.updated_at,
});

const toBookmarkResponse = (row: BookmarkRow): BookmarkRow => ({
  id: row.id,
  url: row.url,
  title: row.title,
  description: row.description,
  folder_id: row.folder_id,
  created_at: row.created_at,
  updated_at: row.updated_at,
});

const readString = (value: unknown): string | undefined => {
  if (typeof value !== "string") {
    return undefined;
  }

  return value.trim();
};

const getFolderId = (req: Request, res: Response): number | null => {
  const id = Number(req.params.id);

  if (!Number.isInteger(id) || id < 1) {
    res.status(404).json({ error: "Folder not found" });
    return null;
  }

  return id;
};

export const createFoldersRouter = (db: Database.Database): Router => {
  const router = Router();

  const findById = db.prepare("SELECT * FROM folders WHERE id = ?");
  const findBookmarksByFolderId = db.prepare(
    "SELECT * FROM bookmarks WHERE folder_id = ? ORDER BY id ASC",
  );

  router.post("/", (req, res) => {
    const name = readString(req.body?.name);

    if (!name) {
      return res.status(400).json({ error: "name is required" });
    }

    const now = new Date().toISOString();
    const result = db
      .prepare(
        `
          INSERT INTO folders (name, created_at, updated_at)
          VALUES (?, ?, ?)
        `,
      )
      .run(name, now, now);

    const folder = findById.get(result.lastInsertRowid) as FolderRow;

    return res.status(201).json(toFolderResponse(folder));
  });

  router.get("/", (_req, res) => {
    const folders = db.prepare("SELECT * FROM folders ORDER BY id ASC").all() as FolderRow[];

    return res.status(200).json(folders.map(toFolderResponse));
  });

  router.get("/:id", (req, res) => {
    const id = getFolderId(req, res);
    if (id === null) {
      return;
    }

    const folder = findById.get(id) as FolderRow | undefined;
    if (!folder) {
      return res.status(404).json({ error: "Folder not found" });
    }

    const bookmarks = findBookmarksByFolderId.all(id) as BookmarkRow[];
    const response: FolderWithBookmarksResponse = {
      ...toFolderResponse(folder),
      bookmarks: bookmarks.map(toBookmarkResponse),
    };

    return res.status(200).json(response);
  });

  router.put("/:id", (req, res) => {
    const id = getFolderId(req, res);
    if (id === null) {
      return;
    }

    const existing = findById.get(id) as FolderRow | undefined;
    if (!existing) {
      return res.status(404).json({ error: "Folder not found" });
    }

    const name = readString(req.body?.name);
    if (!name) {
      return res.status(400).json({ error: "name must be non-empty" });
    }

    const updatedAt = new Date().toISOString();
    db.prepare("UPDATE folders SET name = ?, updated_at = ? WHERE id = ?").run(
      name,
      updatedAt,
      id,
    );

    const updated = findById.get(id) as FolderRow;

    return res.status(200).json(toFolderResponse(updated));
  });

  router.delete("/:id", (req, res) => {
    const id = getFolderId(req, res);
    if (id === null) {
      return;
    }

    const result = db.prepare("DELETE FROM folders WHERE id = ?").run(id);
    if (result.changes === 0) {
      return res.status(404).json({ error: "Folder not found" });
    }

    return res.status(204).send();
  });

  return router;
};

This directory is intentionally generic and mostly empty in Harness v0.

When a user provides a project spec, derive smaller product contract files here
instead of keeping one large spec as the living plan. Name files by the product
domains that actually exist in that spec, for example `overview.md`,
`billing.md`, `workflows.md`, `permissions.md`, or `api-conventions.md`.

Do not create domain files before the spec just to fill the folder. Empty
structure is healthier than fake product truth.

## Update Rule

When behavior changes:

1. Update the affected product doc.
2. Update or create the story packet.
3. Update durable proof status with `scripts/harness story add` or
   `scripts/harness story update`.
4. Record a decision if the change affects architecture, scope, risk, or a
   previously settled product rule.
# Test Matrix

This file maps product behavior to proof.

No product behavior has been defined or implemented yet. Do not mark a row
implemented until tests or validation evidence exist.

## Status Values

| Status | Meaning |
| --- | --- |
| planned | Accepted as intended behavior, not implemented |
| in_progress | Actively being built |
| implemented | Implemented and proof exists |
| changed | Contract changed after earlier implementation |
| retired | No longer part of the product contract |

## Matrix

| Story | Contract | Unit | Integration | E2E | Platform | Status | Evidence |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| TBD | Add rows when story packets are created | no | no | no | no | planned | none |

## Evidence Rules

- Unit proof covers pure domain and application rules.
- Integration proof covers backend enforcement, data integrity, provider
  behavior, jobs, or service contracts.
- E2E proof covers user-visible browser flows.
- Platform proof covers only shell, deployment, mobile, desktop, or runtime
  behavior that cannot be proven in lower layers.
- A story can be implemented without every proof column if the story packet
  explains why.
